New Control API verb `POST /tools/run`: invoke ONE catalog cc-* tool with args on the Director that owns its resources, output streamed back as NDJSON while the tool runs. Catalog-allowlisted (no arbitrary paths, no shell), bounded (`timeoutS`, default 120s, process-TREE kill on expiry), token-gated by the host middleware, FileLog-audited per invocation. Contract addition per plan 1.2.5: `ToolRunRequest`/`ToolRunChunk` in `CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts`.

### Changes
- `ToolsEndpoint.cs`: the verb (validation 400/404/409, NDJSON streaming flushed per line, audit line with resolved exe + args + caller).
- `CcDirector.Core/Tools/ToolRunner.cs`: new bounded streaming process runner (start/stdout/stderr/exit chunks, tree kill, UTF-8).
- `ToolCatalogService.cs`: root-cause fix - the catalog only probed `bin\<name>.exe`, but installs ship python tools as `bin\<name>.cmd` shims forwarding to `pyenv\Scripts\<name>.exe`, so every tool reported NOT BUILT on real installs. Now resolves the shim target (direct exec, no cmd.exe hop).
- `CONTRACT_AUDIT.md`: row 110 Verb, inventory 112 -> 113, Verbs 52 -> 53.
- Tests: ToolRunnerTests (9), ToolRunEndpointTests (14, auth-enabled host), ToolCatalogServiceTests (+2). Build clean, 0 warnings.

```
curl -sN -X POST http://127.0.0.1:<port>/tools/run -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"name\":\"cc-vault\",\"args\":[\"--help\"]}"
```
Then: unknown name (404), a path-shaped name (400, never executed), `"timeoutS":2` against `cc-vault search x` (timeout error, child PID gone - the start chunk carries the pid).

### Expected Result
`{"stream":"start","pid":N}` -> stdout/stderr chunks arriving BEFORE process exit (timestamped captures in the proof show a 34.6s lead on a slow run) -> `{"stream":"exit","exitCode":0,"timedOut":false,"durationMs":N}`; `[ToolsEndpoint] POST /tools/run: tool=..., exe=..., args=[...]` in the Director log.

### Before/After
Before: read-only tools catalog; no remote tool invocation; catalog blind to shim-installed tools. After: streamed, bounded, audited catalog-tool invocation; catalog truthful on real installs.

### Honest notes for QA
- Cross-machine criterion: SORENLAPTOP is up but runs released v0.6.22 (predates the verb - POST there returns 405, captured). Proven via the gateway-leg equivalent: a streamed run through the slot-6 Director's Gateway-advertised tailnet endpoint `https://soren-north.taildb08ed.ts.net:7885` (the identical leg a remote caller uses). Details in the report.
- Live 401: the production host default is `authEnabled=false` (tailnet = trust boundary, pre-existing policy for ALL routes); the 401 gate is proven by `ToolRunEndpointTests` against a real auth-enabled ControlApiHost.

- AC2: unknown name 404; slash/backslash/colon/traversal names 400, REJECTED before catalog lookup, never executed (calc.exe absent; log shows no ToolRunner launch).
- AC3: cc-vault search (49.4s): first output chunk t+2.66s, exit t+49.39s - 46.7s streaming lead, timestamped.
- AC4: timeoutS=2 -> {"timedOut":true,"error":"timed out after 2s; process tree killed"}; child PID 17052 verified gone (3 checks).
- AC5: audit line (tool, resolved exe, args, cwd, timeoutS, caller) on every run; 401-no-token proven by ToolRunEndpointTests (14/14 green) against a real auth-enabled ControlApiHost - live surface intentionally runs authEnabled=false (pre-existing policy, not a #328 change).
- AC6: QA re-proved the gateway-leg equivalent itself: streamed run through the gateway-advertised tailnetEndpoint https://soren-north.taildb08ed.ts.net:7879 (tailscale-serve leg) succeeded; SORENLAPTOP independently confirmed v0.6.22 / POST 405 (pre-verb). Judged: satisfies the criterion's intent; literal laptop smoke becomes possible once a release containing #328 reaches it.
- Tests: 9 ToolRunner + 14 ToolRunEndpoint + 2 ToolCatalogService = 25 new, all green. Build 0 warnings 0 errors.
- CONTRACT_AUDIT.md: grep re-derives 113; table rows 1..113; row 110 = POST /tools/run (Verb).
- Regression sweep: GET /tools (25 built - shim fix works live), GET /tools/{name}, POST /tools/{name}/test all intact.

Environment note (not a PR defect): first slot launch hit "all ports 7879..7898 busy" from 21 stale PortAllocator .port leases; QA removed the two oldest dead leases. PortAllocator predates this PR - stale-lease reaping is follow-up material.

Proceeding to squash-merge per implementation-loop authorization (D5).

body:
Epic: Phase 1 / 1B (REST completeness - missing verbs) in [docs/architecture/TARGET_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md](docs/architecture/TARGET_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md) section 3. Rule: "a verb may not contain a decision" ([DIRECTOR_DUMB_WRAPPER_TARGET.md](docs/architecture/DIRECTOR_DUMB_WRAPPER_TARGET.md) section 5).

## Problem / Value

`WingmanActionExecutor.Execute(Session, WingmanAction)` (`src/CcDirector.Core/Wingman/WingmanActionExecutor.cs:59`) is the mechanical single-write-chokepoint, but it is reachable only through `POST /sessions/{sid}/wingman/act` (`ControlEndpoints.cs:204`), which couples DECIDING (the Director-side wingman LLM picks the action) with ACTING. Phase 3 moves all deciding to the Gateway brain; the brain then needs a dumb Director verb that executes exactly the structured action the caller passes. Plan 1B names `POST /sessions/{sid}/execute-action` as a missing verb. Building it now is the Phase-3 entry point and costs nothing in behavior change.

## Scope

**In:**
- New endpoint `POST /sessions/{sid}/execute-action`: body is a `WingmanAction` JSON (existing contract type: ActNone/ActType/ActSubmit/ActSendKeys); the handler calls `WingmanActionExecutor.Execute` and returns the `WingmanActResult`. Zero decision logic in the path.
- ALL existing executor invariants preserved verbatim (they are enforcement, not intelligence, per target doc 4.1): single write chokepoint, audit trail in `Session.RecentWingmanActions`, 3 s idempotency cooldown on `LastActedScreenHash`, self-injection suppression window, exited/failed-session guard.
- Token-gated like every other verb (DirectorAuth Bearer).

**Out:**
- Removing or changing `POST /sessions/{sid}/wingman/act` (it stays until the Phase-3 decide/execute split).
- Any Gateway-side caller of the new verb (Phase 3).
- New action types.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `POST /sessions/{sid}/execute-action` with an ActSubmit action against a live slot-5+ test session executes it: the typed text + Enter are observable in the session terminal buffer (`GET /sessions/{sid}/buffer`). (API + buffer proof.)
- [ ] The handler path contains no call into `WingmanService` decide logic - the action executes exactly as passed. (Code-review check recorded in the proof report, plus a unit test that the endpoint maps body -> Execute without transformation.)
- [ ] Invariant tests: (a) repeat-POST within the cooldown window on an unchanged screen hash is suppressed and reports it; (b) action against an Exited session returns a 4xx with a clear error, nothing injected; (c) ActNone is accepted and is a no-op.
- [ ] Request without a valid Bearer token -> 401.
- [ ] Cross-machine smoke per plan 1B acceptance: from the gateway box, POST execute-action (via the Gateway leg to the SORENLAPTOP test Director) and screenshot the text arriving in that remote session's terminal.

## Affected Containers

`CcDirector.ControlApi` (new endpoint), `CcDirector.Core` (no executor changes expected), `CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts` (WingmanAction/WingmanActResult already exist - if ANY contract change proves necessary, call it out explicitly in the PR description per plan 1.2.5), tests.

## Proof Target

Plan 1B acceptance line (proof target): "the three new verbs pass cross-machine smoke tests."

**Cross-machine proof:** exercised from SOREN_NORTH against a session hosted on the SORENLAPTOP slot-5+ test Director; report shows the POST, the result JSON, and the remote terminal screenshot containing the injected text.

## Assumptions

None - the contract type, executor, and invariants all exist; this is plumbing a clean entry to them.

Proof: [docs/cencon/proof/issue-327/report.html](https://github.com/thefrederiksen/cc-director/blob/issue-327-execute-action-verb/docs/cencon/proof/issue-327/report.html)  (PR #340)

### Release Notes
New Director Control API verb `POST /sessions/{sid}/execute-action`: executes a caller-supplied structured `WingmanAction` (`none|type|send_keys|submit`) verbatim through `WingmanActionExecutor` - the mechanical execute leg of the wingman decide/execute split (plan 1B). Zero decision logic, no LLM in the path; the existing `wingman/act` endpoint is unchanged.

### Changes
- `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/ControlEndpoints.cs` - new endpoint; mechanical status->HTTP mapping (`session_gone` -> 410 + clear error, executor `bad_request` -> 400, `ok`/`suppressed` -> 200); token-gated by the existing DirectorAuth middleware.
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway.Tests/ExecuteActionEndpointTests.cs` - 10 new HTTP-level tests against a real auth-enabled ControlApiHost with buffer-backed sessions.
- `docs/architecture/gateway/CONTRACT_AUDIT.md` - new row 85 (Verb), inventory 111 -> 112, Verbs 51 -> 52, later rows renumbered +1.
- No changes to `CcDirector.Core` or `CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts` (contract reused as-is, per plan 1.2.5).

### How to Test
1. Allocate an isolated test Director (`scripts/agent-session-isolation.ps1 allocate/launch`, slot >= 6) from the PR branch worktree (`D:\ReposFred\wt-issue-327`, kept in place for QA).
2. `POST /sessions` to create a session, wait for the agent to boot.
3. `POST /sessions/{sid}/execute-action` with `{action:submit,text:...,reason:...}`.
4. `GET /sessions/{sid}/buffer` - the text and its submission must be visible; `GET /sessions/{sid}/wingman` shows the audit row.
5. Repeat an identical `send_keys` POST immediately - second response reports `suppressed`.
6. `dotnet test --filter FullyQualifiedName~ExecuteActionEndpointTests` covers exited->410, ActNone no-op, 400/404, and the 401 Bearer gate (auth-enabled host).

### Expected Result
200 `{status:ok,performed:true,...}` echoing the exact action passed; text + Enter observable in the terminal; all executor invariants intact (cooldown suppression, exited guard 410, audit trail); 401 without a valid Bearer token.

### Before/After
- Before: the mechanical executor was reachable only through `POST /sessions/{sid}/wingman/act`, which couples the Director-side LLM DECIDE with ACT.
- After: a dumb verb executes exactly what the caller passes - the Phase-3 Gateway brain entry point exists; `wingman/act` untouched.

Cross-machine smoke note (honesty per the loop authorization): the SORENLAPTOP seat was unavailable (tailscale serve answers 502 - no Director behind it; direct ports filtered), so the Gateway-leg was proven via the local equivalent on an isolated test pair (separate-process HTTP caller -> slot-6 Director live, plus the Bearer-authenticated leg in the unit tests). Details in the proof report section 2 row 5.

Pre-existing test note: `Gateway.Tests` 487/488 - the single failure is the live-OpenAI-Realtime dictation integration test (`FullPipeline_transcribes_phase0_clip2`), untouched by and unrelated to this change.

QA PASS - independent verification complete (QA Agent, CenCon method).

All 6 acceptance criteria verified with QA's own build, tests, and live evidence on an isolated slot-6 Director plus an isolated GatewayHost pair. Proof: [docs/cencon/proof/issue-327/qa-report.html](docs/cencon/proof/issue-327/qa-report.html) (committed to the PR branch, b219540).

- ActSubmit live: POST -> 200 performed=true; prompt + claude's QA-327-ACK observable via GET /buffer (screenshot in report)
- Handler source read: zero decide logic - Guid parse, lookup, WingmanActionExecutor.Execute, mechanical status->HTTP mapping; route not in PublicPaths
- Cooldown live: repeat POST -> 200 suppressed/performed=false; suppressed action not audited
- Exited session live (claude child killed, Status=Exited): 410 session_gone, "session is Exited; nothing was injected"
- ActNone live: 200 performed=false, no audit row
- No-token 401: proven on auth-enabled test host (10/10 new tests); production Director runs auth-disabled for ALL verbs (pre-existing posture, out of scope) - verified consistent live
- Cross-machine criterion: SORENLAPTOP seat down; QA verified the actual Gateway->Director relay on an isolated pair (register + fan-out read + write relay + buffer read-back with GW-LEG-327-ACK). Judgment: intent satisfied - the literal form is unsatisfiable by the issue's own scope ("Out: Any Gateway-side caller of the new verb (Phase 3)"). Recorded in the report.

Suites: full build 0W/0E; Core.Tests 1759 passed; Gateway.Tests 487/488 (single failure = pre-existing live-OpenAI dictation test, #214 family, unrelated).

Proceeding to squash-merge per implementation-loop authorization (D5).

body:
Epic: Phase 1 / 1B (REST completeness - Facts/Events/Verbs audit) in [docs/architecture/TARGET_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md](docs/architecture/TARGET_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md) section 3. Taxonomy: [docs/architecture/DIRECTOR_DUMB_WRAPPER_TARGET.md](docs/architecture/DIRECTOR_DUMB_WRAPPER_TARGET.md) section 5.

## Problem / Value

The target architecture says the Director's entire API surface must be describable in three nouns - Facts, Events, Verbs - and anything that interprets/decides below the line is a boundary violation. The Director Control API today has ~75+ routes (`src/CcDirector.ControlApi/ControlEndpoints.cs:26-2489` plus SettingsEndpoint, ToolsEndpoint, WorkspacesEndpoint, SchedulerEndpoint, dictation/TTS/WS endpoints) and no classification exists, so violations (e.g. the wingman decide endpoints, summary/brief/recap generation) ride along unnamed. The audit is the map the rest of the migration navigates by. Plan 1B acceptance: "the audit doc exists with zero unclassified endpoints."

## Scope

**In:**
- Enumerate every mapped route (method + path) across all endpoint files in `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/` (including WebSocket endpoints `/sessions/{sid}/stream` and `/dictate`).
- Classify each as **Fact**, **Event**, **Verb**, or **VIOLATION** (judgment below the line), one-line rationale each; every VIOLATION names its migration phase from the plan (e.g. "wingman decide -> Phase 3").
- A deterministic inventory method (a grep/script over `Map(Get|Post|Put|Patch|Delete)` + WS maps) recorded in the doc so QA can re-derive the count.
- Commit as `docs/architecture/gateway/CONTRACT_AUDIT.md`; link it from DIRECTOR_DUMB_WRAPPER_TARGET.md's related-documents list and `docs/architecture/README.md`.

**Out:**
- Changing any endpoint behavior (this issue is documentation-only).
- The three new verbs (separate 1B issues).
- Auditing the Gateway's own API surface (Director Control API only).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/architecture/gateway/CONTRACT_AUDIT.md` exists with a table whose row count equals the scripted route inventory count, and zero rows classified "unknown/unclassified".
- [ ] Every row has: method, path, classification (Fact/Event/Verb/Violation), one-line rationale. Every Violation row names a migration phase.
- [ ] The inventory command/script is included in the doc and QA re-running it gets the same route count. (Command output proof.)
- [ ] The doc is linked from DIRECTOR_DUMB_WRAPPER_TARGET.md and docs/architecture/README.md.

## Affected Containers

Documentation only: `docs/architecture/gateway/`. No code change. (Developer commits the doc to a PR branch per CenCon 6a.)

## Proof Target

Plan 1B acceptance line (proof target): "the audit doc exists with zero unclassified endpoints."

**Cross-machine proof:** N/A - documentation-only item; the deterministic proof is the route-inventory count matching the table row count, shown in the proof report. (Stated explicitly per plan section 10 rather than inventing a synthetic cross-machine step.)

## Assumptions

- ASSUMPTION (flagged): endpoints that are pure UI conveniences served by the Director to a browser (e.g. `GET /login`, `GET /sessions/{sid}/view` HTML) are classified as Facts (they serve state/HTML deterministically) rather than getting a fourth category. If the human prefers a separate "UI surface" category, the doc structure allows it - rationale column stays.

title: [CenCon] Contract audit: classify every Director Control API endpoint as Fact, Event, or Verb in CONTRACT_AUDIT.md

body:
Epic: Phase 1 / 1A (Identity and addressing) in [docs/architecture/TARGET_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md](docs/architecture/TARGET_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md) section 3.

## Problem / Value

The #223/#224 two-way nonce handshake verifies the Director's advertised endpoint at registration time only (`GatewayClient.VerifyAsync` `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/GatewayClient.cs:381-453`; pre-registration probe `ProbeAdvertisedEndpointAsync` `GatewayClient.cs:509-525` hits `{advertised}/healthz`). A Director whose advertised name goes bad AFTER registration (lost `tailscale serve` mapping, cert expiry, firewall change) keeps heartbeating happily and is never re-flagged - the fleet believes an address that no longer answers. Plan 1A requires: "a Director that advertises an unreachable name is flagged within one heartbeat cycle."

## Scope

**In:**
- Gateway-side periodic re-verification of each registered Director's advertised endpoint, tied to the heartbeat cadence (15 s): probe `GET {advertised}/healthz` and compare directorId.
- On failure: the Director's fleet record flips to an explicit unreachable-by-name state, distinct from "heartbeat lost" (the Director is alive, its front door is not). Surfaced in Gateway `GET /directors` and on the Cockpit fleet/directors page.
- Automatic recovery when the probe succeeds again.

**Out:**
- Changing the registration-time handshake itself (#223/#224 behavior stays).
- Notifications/alerting on the state change.
- Cockpit page redesign (render the state in the existing fleet surface only).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Break the advertised endpoint of a slot-5+ test Director on SORENLAPTOP (remove its tailscale serve mapping) while its heartbeats continue: within 2 heartbeat cycles (30 s) Gateway `GET /directors` marks that Director unreachable-by-name via a named field/state. (Timed API responses proof.)
- [ ] Restore the mapping: the state auto-clears within 2 cycles, no restart of either side. (Timed API responses proof.)
- [ ] The unreachable-by-name state is visibly distinct from healthy and from heartbeat-lost on the Cockpit fleet/directors page. (Screenshot proof.)
- [ ] Unit tests for the probe state machine: healthy -> fail -> flagged; flagged -> succeed -> cleared; directorId mismatch on probe counts as failure (impostor guard).
- [ ] Probe failures are logged Gateway-side with endpoint + reason.

## Affected Containers

`CcDirector.Gateway` (DirectorRegistry / probe loop), `CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts` (status field on DirectorDto if needed - call out the contract change in the PR per plan 1.2.5), Cockpit fleet page rendering.

## Proof Target

Plan 1A acceptance line (proof target): "a tailnet endpoint that answers /healthz from the *other* machine" - this issue makes that property continuously enforced, not registration-time-only.

**Cross-machine proof:** by construction - the Gateway on SOREN_NORTH probes the SORENLAPTOP test Director's advertised endpoint. Report shows the break/flag/restore/clear timeline with timestamps and the Cockpit screenshot.

## Assumptions

- ASSUMPTION (flagged): the probe target is `GET {advertised}/healthz` (cheap, unauthenticated, already used by the registration-time probe). If a stronger nonce-based re-verification is wanted per cycle, that is a human call - healthz-probe is assumed sufficient for Phase 1.
- ASSUMPTION: probing every registered Director every 15 s is acceptable load at current fleet sizes (single-digit machines); batching/backoff tuning is the Developer's choice as long as the 30 s flag latency holds.

comments.body: CLAIM flow:in-progress by 37cea6e2/663e3482 at 2026-06-11T11:56:08Z

## Developer Agent - Implemented (ready for QA)

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-325/report.html  (PR #338, branch `issue-325-gateway-endpoint-reverify`)

### Release Notes
The Gateway now re-verifies each registered Director's advertised endpoint every heartbeat cycle (15 s), not just at registration. A Director whose advertised name goes dark while it keeps heartbeating is flagged `unreachable-by-name` on `GET /directors` within 30 s (measured: 16.1 s), with the reason and since-when; the flag auto-clears when the name answers again (measured: 14.0 s). The Cockpit Directors page shows the state as a red `UNREACHABLE BY NAME` badge, distinct from the amber fan-out `UNREACHABLE` and green `OK`.

### Changes
- NEW `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Discovery/AdvertisedEndpointMonitor.cs` - 15 s probe loop (`GET {advertised}/healthz` + directorId match impostor guard); skips FSW-discovered locals and #324 flagged no-endpoint registrations.
- `DirectorRegistry.RecordEndpointProbeResult` - the probe state machine (flag on fail, auto-clear on success), with endpoint+reason logging (transition + every 10th repeat).
- `DirectorDto` contract (additive, nullable): `advertisedEndpointState` (ok / unreachable-by-name), `advertisedEndpointCheckedAt`, `advertisedEndpointUnreachableSince`, `advertisedEndpointError`.
- `DirectorEndpointClient.GetHealthDetailedAsync` - probe with the failure reason.
- Cockpit `Directors.razor` + `app.css` - the red badge with tooltip (reason + since).
- `docs/cencon/architecture_manifest.yaml` - AdvertisedEndpointMonitor component entry.
- 8 unit tests in `AdvertisedEndpointMonitorTests` (all green; solution builds 0 warnings / 0 errors).

### How to Test
1. Build the branch and start an isolated Gateway: `CC_GATEWAY_NO_TAILSCALE=1 CC_TURNBRIEFS=0 dotnet run --project src/CcDirector.Gateway -- --port 7978`.
2. Run `python docs/cencon/proof/issue-325/harness/driver.py` - it registers synthetic Directors over the real wire contract, heartbeats them every 10 s, breaks the advertised endpoint, restores it, and writes a timestamped timeline.
3. Watch `GET /directors` (or the timeline): the named state flips and clears. Cockpit: run with `Cockpit__GatewayUrl=http://127.0.0.1:7978/` and open `/directors`.
4. Cross-machine variant (literal AC wording): break a test Director's tailscale serve mapping on another machine while it heartbeats - same state machine, only the failure string differs. The proof report documents why the agent ran the isolated equivalent instead of touching the production serve table.

### Expected Result
Flag within 30 s of the break while heartbeats continue; auto-clear within 30 s of the restore with no restarts; three visually distinct states on the Cockpit Directors page; Gateway log lines carrying endpoint + reason.

### Before/After
Before: a Director whose advertised name died after registration stayed green forever (registration-time verification only). After: flagged `unreachable-by-name` within one probe cycle, auto-clearing on recovery - see `docs/cencon/proof/issue-325/cockpit-directors-unreachable-by-name.png`.

## QA Agent - VERIFIED (all acceptance criteria met)

Independent verification of PR #338 (own worktree build of head `d0c128f`, own isolated QA Gateway on port 7975 confirmed running the PR build via its /healthz version stamp, own harness - not a re-run of the Developer's proof). Full report: [docs/cencon/proof/issue-325/qa-report.html](docs/cencon/proof/issue-325/qa-report.html)

| # | Criterion | Result |
|---|-----------|--------|
| 1 | Break advertised endpoint while heartbeats continue -> named state within 30 s | PASS - flagged `advertisedEndpointState="unreachable-by-name"` 17.1 s after the break; heartbeat counter 0 -> 3 during the flagged window (alive + flagged, distinct from heartbeat loss) |
| 2 | Restore -> auto-clear within 30 s, no restarts | PASS - cleared to `ok` 2.0 s after restore (since/error nulled); verified twice |
| 3 | Visibly distinct on Cockpit Directors page | PASS - red bold UNREACHABLE BY NAME vs amber UNREACHABLE (fan-out) vs green OK side by side ([qa-cockpit-directors.png](docs/cencon/proof/issue-325/qa-cockpit-directors.png)); tooltip carries reason + since, read off the live DOM |
| 4 | Unit tests: healthy->fail->flagged, flagged->succeed->cleared, id mismatch = failure | PASS - 8/8 AdvertisedEndpointMonitorTests green in QA's build; impostor guard ALSO exercised live (wrong-id listener flagged with "answered ... as a DIFFERENT Director (qa-impostor-process)") |
| 5 | Probe failures logged Gateway-side with endpoint + reason | PASS - QA Gateway log carries endpoint= and reason= on transition, recovery line on clear, repeat-throttled |

Regression sweep: solution builds 0 warnings/0 errors; Gateway test suite 477/478 - the single failure (DictationEndpointTests realtime clip) reproduces identically on the PR's base commit (origin/main) in a clean worktree = pre-existing, unrelated to #325. Real slot-6 Director (PR build, agent-session-isolation) answered /healthz with its directorId and rendered OK. FSW-discovered locals not probed (state null) as scoped. Existing amber UNREACHABLE and #223/#224 handshake behavior unchanged.

QA evidence committed to the PR branch: qa-report.html, qa-timeline.txt, qa-cockpit-directors.png, qa-harness/ (commit 0cf6a2b).

title: [Gateway] Re-verify each Director's advertised endpoint every heartbeat cycle - flag unreachable-by-name within 30 s

body:
Epic: Phase 1 / 1A (Identity and addressing) in [docs/architecture/TARGET_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md](docs/architecture/TARGET_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md) section 3. Architecture rule: [docs/architecture/DIRECTOR_DUMB_WRAPPER_TARGET.md](docs/architecture/DIRECTOR_DUMB_WRAPPER_TARGET.md).

## Problem / Value

Observed live on SORENLAPTOP's Director (Control API :7880, v0.6.22, 2026-06-11): the Director silently fails to advertise a tailnet endpoint. `GatewayClient.ResolveTailnetEndpoint()` (`src/CcDirector.ControlApi/GatewayClient.cs:469-500`) returns `""` when `TailscaleIdentity.TryGetMagicDnsName()` fails and no `gateway.tailnetEndpoint` config override exists, and the Director then logs "no tailnet endpoint to advertise; staying local-only" (`GatewayClient.cs:251`) and carries on. The resolved DNS name is also cached forever (`_cachedDnsName`), so a Tailscale daemon that comes up after Director start never heals the advertisement. Every cross-machine feature above this (session viewUrls, Cockpit terminal, remote restart) inherits the gap. This is the user-reported "right Tailscale names" problem, confirmed in the plan's evidence baseline (section 2).

## Scope

**In:**
- Detection order per plan 1A: Tailscale local API -> `tailscale status --json` (exists: `TailscaleIdentity.TryGetMagicDnsName`, runs status --json and parses Self.DNSName) -> explicit config override. First success wins.
- Fail loudly when none resolves: `GatewayConnectionMonitor` gets an explicit identity-failure state, surfaced in the desktop gateway indicator and FileLog with a message that names the fix (start Tailscale / set the override).
- Never advertise loopback or empty as the tailnet endpoint. Loopback remains correct for local Kestrel binding only.
- Re-resolve on the heartbeat cycle: drop the forever-cache so identity heals within one heartbeat (15 s) after Tailscale comes up, without a Director restart.

**Out:**
- Director-side population of session DTO fields (separate 1A issue).
- Continuous Gateway-side re-verification of the advertised endpoint (separate 1A issue).
- Cockpit display changes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] On a tailnet machine, after registration, Gateway `GET /directors` shows this Director with `tailnetEndpoint = https://<machine>.<tailnet>.ts.net:<port>` - never empty, never 127.0.0.1. (Control API response proof.)
- [ ] With Tailscale stopped and no config override: the Director does NOT advertise a loopback/empty endpoint as reachable; the desktop gateway indicator shows a failure state whose tooltip/text names the missing tailnet identity; FileLog contains a clear `[GatewayClient]`-tagged error naming the remediation. (Screenshot + log excerpt proof.)
- [ ] Detection order unit-tested: local API result preferred, CLI fallback used when local API unavailable, config override used when both fail; each seam stubbed.
- [ ] Healing: start the Director with Tailscale stopped, then start Tailscale; within 2 heartbeat cycles (30 s) the Gateway registration shows the real ts.net endpoint with no Director restart. (Timed log/API proof.)
- [ ] Regression test: `BuildRegistrationRequest()` never produces a registration that claims a reachable advertised endpoint while `TailnetEndpoint` is empty or loopback.

## Affected Containers

`CcDirector.ControlApi` (GatewayClient, GatewayConnectionMonitor), `CcDirector.Core` (TailscaleIdentity - add local API probe), `CcDirector.Avalonia` (indicator text only), `CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts` only if a registration status field is needed (call out any contract change explicitly in the PR per plan 1.2.5).

## Proof Target

Plan 1A acceptance line (proof target): "from the gateway box, GET /sessions (Gateway-aggregated) shows every SORENLAPTOP session with correct machine name and a tailnet endpoint that answers /healthz from the *other* machine." This issue proves the Director half:

**Cross-machine proof:** hosted on a slot-5+ test Director on SORENLAPTOP; exercised from the gateway box (SOREN_NORTH). Report must show (1) Gateway `/directors` JSON with the SORENLAPTOP director's real `*.ts.net` endpoint, (2) `curl <that-endpoint>/healthz` run FROM the gateway box returning 200 with the matching directorId, (3) the fail-loudly screenshot from the Tailscale-stopped case.

## Assumptions

- ASSUMPTION (flagged for human review): when no tailnet identity resolves, the Director still registers with the Gateway (so the fleet can see the machine exists) but in an explicit no-reachable-endpoint state - instead of today's silent "staying local-only" skip at `GatewayClient.cs:251`. The plan mandates "fail loudly" and bans the silent loopback fallback but does not say whether to register at all; register-with-flag is assumed because an invisible Director is harder to diagnose remotely.
- ASSUMPTION: "Tailscale local API" means the tailscaled LocalAPI (named pipe on Windows); if probing it is disproportionate, the Developer may implement CLI-first + config override and record the local API as deferred, provided the fail-loudly and healing criteria still pass.

comments.body: CLAIM flow:in-progress by 37cea6e2/663e3482 at 2026-06-11T10:30:11Z

﻿## Developer Agent - Implemented, ready for QA

**Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-324/report.html  (PR #337)**

### Release Notes
Director now resolves its tailnet endpoint via the plan-1A ladder (tailscaled LocalAPI -> CLI -> config override), re-resolves it every heartbeat (forever-cache deleted, heals within 15 s of Tailscale coming up, no restart), and fails loudly when unresolvable: new red NO TAILNET IDENTITY indicator state naming the fix, troubleshooter verdict, and [GatewayClient] log remediation lines. Loopback/empty is never advertised; a loopback override is refused. With no identity the Director registers FLAGGED (empty endpoint + EndpointUnreachableReason, additive optional contract field) so the fleet still sees the machine - the issue's flagged ASSUMPTION, implemented as proposed.

### Changes
See PR #337 description - 17 files, new TailnetIdentityResolver + LocalAPI probe + heartbeat re-resolve + explicit monitor/indicator state + flagged-registration Gateway acceptance + 1 additive optional contract field (called out explicitly per plan 1.2.5).

### How to Test
1. Build solution, run TailnetIdentityResolverTests (15) + Gateway.Tests #324 suites (40) - all green.
2. Slot Director on this tailnet box: log shows resolution via local-api; Gateway /directors carries the real ts.net endpoint (proof Run 1) despite the loopback override present in config.json.
3. Slot Director with CC_GATEWAY_NO_TAILSCALE=1: red NO TAILNET IDENTITY indicator with remediation text (proof Run 2 screenshot + log).

### Expected Result
GET /directors: tailnetEndpoint = https://<machine>.<tailnet>.ts.net:<port>, never empty/loopback; explicit loud failure state when unresolvable; healing within 2 heartbeat cycles without restart (timed test asserts the 30 s bound against a real GatewayHost).

### Before / After
Before: silent "staying local-only" skip at GatewayClient.cs:251, DNS name cached forever, loopback override passed through. After: ladder every heartbeat, loud failure naming the fix, loopback refused, flagged-but-visible registration. Screenshots of both indicator states are in the proof.

### Honest limitations for QA (in the report too)
- Healing (AC4) proven by the timed integration test (real register/heartbeat loop vs real in-proc GatewayHost over HTTP, probe pinned), NOT by stopping the real tailscaled - this box is the production gateway box and stopping Tailscale would sever the live tailnet (rule-0 territory).
- Cross-machine proof target executed single-box: the slot Director's ts.net front door was dialed via https://soren-north...:7897/healthz (transport-identical to a remote dial); a SORENLAPTOP-hosted rig needs a human/QA with build access on that machine.
- Live prod Gateway predates #324 and answered 400 to the flagged shape (captured in Run 2 as the designed back-compat path); new-Gateway acceptance proven by the endpoint integration tests.

I believe this is finished.

## QA Agent - VERIFIED, all acceptance criteria met (5/5 PASS)

**Proof: [docs/cencon/proof/issue-324/qa-report.html](docs/cencon/proof/issue-324/qa-report.html) (PR #337, commit cf284f6)**

Independent verification on an isolated worktree + slot-6 test Director (per-session isolation), own build (0 warnings / 0 errors), own screenshots and live Gateway captures. Developer evidence used as context only.

- AC1 PASS: slot-6 Director resolved `https://soren-north.taildb08ed.ts.net:7896` via local-api (loopback config override correctly outranked); live Gateway `/directors` carried that endpoint with `twoWayVerifiedAt` stamped; healthz via the ts.net front door answered 200 with the matching directorId.
- AC2 PASS: with `CC_GATEWAY_NO_TAILSCALE=1` the loopback override was REFUSED with a remediation message, the desktop indicator showed red NO TAILNET IDENTITY naming the fix (screenshot), FileLog carried the `[GatewayClient]` error, registration went out flagged (empty endpoint + reason) and the live pre-#324 Gateway answered 400 - the designed back-compat path, observed live. No loopback ever advertised.
- AC3 PASS: TailnetIdentityResolverTests 15/15 - local-api preferred, CLI fallback, override-when-both-fail, all seams stubbed.
- AC4 PASS: timed healing test green (15 s, inside the 30 s / 2-heartbeat bound) against a real in-proc GatewayHost; forever-cache confirmed deleted, ladder re-runs every heartbeat (code-inspected).
- AC5 PASS: BuildRegistrationRequest never-claims-reachable regression suite green; full #324 Gateway sweep 40/40.

Limitations (same as Developer recorded): cross-machine half of the proof target executed single-box (transport-identical ts.net front-door dial + the Gateway two-way callback proves reachability); AC4 proven by the timed integration test, not by stopping the production tailscaled (rule-0).

Proceeding to squash-merge per implementation-loop authorization (DECIDED D5).

title: [Director] Resolve and advertise the real MagicDNS tailnet endpoint at registration and heartbeat - fail loudly when unresolvable

body: **Child of epic #279 (Remote-first Cockpit reliability).** Same architectural gap #268 closed for the two WS legs, but for the per-session **HTTP image bytes**: the browser fetches screenshot bytes directly from the owning Director's advertised address instead of same-origin through the Gateway. ## Problem / proof Opening the Cockpit from a non-Gateway machine (screenshot: SORENLAPTOP against `https://soren-north.taildb08ed.ts.net/cockpit`, 2026-06-11): the Screenshots panel lists every file with timestamps and Insert/Copy/Del links, but **every thumbnail renders broken/black**. Root cause (`src/CcDirector.Cockpit/Components/Pages/Cockpit.razor`): - `ShotUrl(fileName)` (~line 1616) → `DirectorClient.ScreenshotUrl(DirBase, fileName)` → `{directorBase}/screenshots/file?name=...` where `DirBase => Selected.TailnetEndpoint`. - The aggregator stamps the co-located (FSW-discovered) Director's endpoint as `http://127.0.0.1:7887`, so the remote browser resolves the `` to **its own loopback** → broken image. Exactly the #268 mechanism. - The file *list* works because `LoadScreenshotsAsync` runs **server-side** on the Gateway host — only the byte fetches run in the browser. Three browser-direct sinks, all through `ShotUrl`: 1. **Thumbnails**: `` (~line 473) 2. **View full-size**: `ViewScreenshot` → `window.open(ShotUrl(fileName))` (~line 1668) 3. **Copy**: `CopyScreenshot` → `cockpit-shots.js` fetches the bytes browser-side from the same URL (~line 1680) ## Approach (decided: route through the Gateway, mirroring #268) 1. **Gateway: new per-session screenshot-bytes proxy** — `GET /sessions/{sid}/screenshots/file?name=...`: resolve the owning Director (reuse the #268 `SessionWsProxyEndpoints` resolution incl. `SessionOwnerCache` semantics: unknown sid → 404, owner offline → 503) and forward to the Director's existing `GET /screenshots/file?name=...`, streaming the image bytes + content type through. Plain HTTP forward — no WS upgrade needed; reuse the same `IHttpForwarder`/path-rewrite pattern. 2. **Cockpit: `ShotUrl` becomes same-origin** — build from `NavigationManager.BaseUri` (like `CockpitWsUrls`), path `/sessions/{sid}/screenshots/file?name=...`. All three sinks (thumb, view, copy) go through `ShotUrl`, so one change covers them. 3. Director's own `/screenshots/file` endpoint is **unchanged**. ## Scope **IN**: the Gateway proxy route, the same-origin `ShotUrl`, a unit-testable URL builder (Gateway origin + sid + escaped name, http/https, trailing slash), Gateway log lines per proxied image (open/resolved/close), unit tests for builder + proxy resolution (404 unknown / 503 offline-owner / 200 bytes round-trip). **OUT**: any change to the Director's screenshots endpoints; gallery UX changes; the Insert/Del/list paths (already server-side and working). ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `ShotUrl` output is same-origin to the Gateway (`{gateway-origin}/sessions/{sid}/screenshots/file?name=...`) and never contains a Director address; unit test asserts origin, sid path, URL-escaped name, https→https, trailing-slash safety. - [ ] `GET /sessions/{sid}/screenshots/file?name=...` on the Gateway returns the image bytes with the correct content type for a real session (prove with a self-hosted GatewayHost + a stub/real Director: bytes round-trip equals the source file). - [ ] Unknown sid → 404; cached-owner-offline → 503 (mirrors #268/#288/#291 semantics; reuse the same resolution, do not fork it). - [ ] Thumbnails render in the running Cockpit (live proof: gallery shows actual images, not broken icons), and View opens the full-size image at the same-origin URL. - [ ] Copy still works (cockpit-shots.js fetches the same-origin URL; no JS protocol change). - [ ] Build clean (0/0); existing Gateway + Cockpit tests stay green. ## Affected Containers - `CcDirector.Gateway` — new screenshot-bytes proxy route (alongside `SessionWsProxyEndpoints`). - `CcDirector.Cockpit` — `ShotUrl`/`DirectorClient.ScreenshotUrl` same-origin construction. - `CcDirector.ControlApi` — read-only (Director endpoint unchanged). Cross-machine confirmation from a second tailnet box happens post-merge as deployment verification (the supervisor handles it), so the loop's QA only needs single-machine proof.

comments.body: ## Developer Agent - Implemented, ready for QA ### Release Notes Cockpit screenshot thumbnails, View, and Copy now load same-origin through the Gateway (`GET /sessions/{sid}/screenshots/file?name=...`), which resolves the owning Director and streams the bytes through - so the gallery works from any machine, not just the Gateway host. Director endpoints unchanged. ### Changes - **Gateway**: third per-session leg on `SessionWsProxyEndpoints` - plain-HTTP forward to the Director's `/screenshots/file` via the same YARP forwarder and the SAME #268/#288/#291 resolution (`SessionOwnerCache`: unknown sid -> 404, cached-owner-offline -> 503). Open/resolved/close log lines per image. - **Contracts**: new `CockpitShotUrls.Screenshot` - pure same-origin URL builder (scheme preserved, sid path, URL-escaped name). - **Cockpit**: `ShotUrl` builds from `Nav.BaseUri`; Director-direct `DirectorClient.ScreenshotUrl` removed. `cockpit-shots.js` logic untouched. - **Tests**: `CockpitShotUrlsTests`, `ScreenshotProxyRoundTripTests` (byte-identical 200 round-trip + content type + decoded name + 404 pass-through), screenshots leg added to `SessionWsProxyEndpointsTests` 404/503/precedence theories. - **CenCon**: `architecture_manifest.yaml` component descriptions updated. ### How to Test 1. `dotnet build cc-director.sln` (0/0) and run `CcDirector.Gateway.Tests` (435 green incl. new) + `CcDirector.Cockpit.Tests` (16 green). 2. Run a dev Gateway from the branch (`CC_GATEWAY_NO_TAILSCALE=1 CC_TURNBRIEFS=0 CC_COCKPIT_PORT=7470`, `--port 7991`) + dev Cockpit (`Cockpit__GatewayUrl=http://127.0.0.1:7991/`), browse `http://127.0.0.1:7991/` -> Cockpit -> session -> SCREENSHOTS. 3. `curl` the route directly for 200/404/503 and compare bytes with the Director-direct fetch. ### Expected Result Every thumbnail `src` is `{gateway-origin}/sessions/{sid}/screenshots/file?name=...` (never a Director address); images decode (`naturalWidth > 0`); View opens the full-size image at the same-origin URL; Copy reports "copied image to clipboard"; unknown sid -> 404, cached-owner-offline -> 503. ### Before/After - Before: `` - broken/black from any remote browser (issue screenshot). - After: `` - renders everywhere; proven live with real images (see report). Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-317/report.html (PR #319)

## QA Agent - VERIFIED (independent, fresh context)

Built the PR branch myself (0 warnings / 0 errors), launched my own slot-5 Director via the scheduled task, created a real session, and drove a dev Gateway (7993) + dev Cockpit (7472) from the branch with headless Chromium talking only to the Gateway origin.

**All 6 acceptance criteria PASS with my own proof:**

1. **ShotUrl same-origin** - live DOM: all 12 `img.shot-thumb` srcs are `http://127.0.0.1:7993/sessions/{sid}/screenshots/file?name=...`; 0 references to Director ports 7887/7880. `CockpitShotUrlsTests` (origin, sid path, escaping, https->https, trailing slash, deep link, port) green.
2. **200 bytes + content type** - live fetch via the Gateway: 200 `image/png`, 182,527 bytes, **byte-identical** to the Director-direct fetch. `ScreenshotProxyRoundTripTests` (self-hosted GatewayHost + stub Director) green.
3. **404 / 503** - live: unknown sid -> 404 "session not found across any director"; after caching the owner I stopped my Director and re-fetched -> 503 "owning director offline". Same `ProxyAsync`/`SessionOwnerCache` as the WS legs (no fork); `SessionWsProxyEndpointsTests` theories include the new leg.
4. **Thumbnails render + View** - 12/12 thumbs `complete && naturalWidth>0`; clicking opened a new tab at the same-origin URL titled `file (1898x962)` with the image rendered.
5. **Copy** - "copied image to clipboard" shown; `cockpit-shots.js` diff is comment-only.
6. **Build clean, suites green** - solution 0/0; Gateway tests 435/435; Cockpit tests 16/16.

Gateway logs show open/resolved/close per image (`leg=shot`). Director endpoints untouched; architecture manifest updated.

**QA proof report:** `docs/cencon/proof/issue-317/qa-report.html` (with `qa-gallery-thumbnails.png`, `qa-view-fullsize.png`) committed to the PR branch.

Note for the supervisor: the working tree carried pre-existing/concurrent WIP not belonging to this issue (`docs/plans/cockpit-triage-2026-06-10.html`, `docs/architecture/README.md` edit + `DIRECTOR_DUMB_WRAPPER_TARGET.md`, timestamps matching other live agent sessions). QA left those untouched - they are not part of #317 and not QA's to delete.

Proceeding to squash-merge per the implementation-loop authorization. Cross-machine confirmation remains post-merge (supervisor-owned).
