body:
Parent epic: #297

## Problem / Value

#270 v1 work lists are in-memory: a Gateway restart loses every list along with its order and consumer assignment. For unattended multi-day queues this is a data-loss gap (the queue runner's whole point is running while no one watches - and the Gateway does restart, e.g. on update).

## Scope

- Persist each list (items, order, consumer claim) to disk under the gateway's data dir; reload on startup.
- Reuse the existing FileLog / storage conventions.
- Survive a restart with order and in-progress/consumer state intact.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create a list with items, restart the Gateway: the list, its order, and its consumer assignment are intact. (Proof: before/after a restart.)
- [ ] An in-progress drain resumes (or cleanly re-claims) after a restart rather than silently losing the queue. (Proof: a drain interrupted by a restart continues.)

## Affected

- `CcDirector.Gateway` (named-list store + load-on-start)

Lower priority - independent of #298 / #299 / #300; the in-memory list works for single-session runs today.

comments.body:
## Product sharpening (Definition of Ready) - settles design decisions before flow:ready-dev

### D-1. Storage shape
- One JSON file holding all named lists (items, order, consumer claim), in the Gateway's existing data dir, following the established keyvault.json precedent (plain JSON, FileLog on load/save). Suggested name: worklists.json.
- Atomic writes: write temp file then rename/replace - a crash mid-write must never corrupt or half-truncate the store.
- Write-through on every mutation (Create/Delete/AppendItem/Reorder/RemoveItem/Claim/Release and any runner-driven item mutation). Volume is tiny; no debounce/caching layer (no premature optimization, no fallback logic - a failed save is a logged error, not a silent skip).

### D-2. Reload-on-start + claim policy (settles AC2's "resumes (or cleanly re-claims)")
- On startup the Gateway loads worklists.json and restores all lists with order intact.
- A persisted CONSUMER CLAIM is, by definition, stale after a restart (the claiming runner died with the Gateway, or belongs to a session that may no longer exist). Policy: on load, persist-then-release stale claims with an explicit FileLog line naming the list and the dead consumer token. The list and its remaining items survive; a new runner re-claims and continues from the persisted order. No attempt to auto-respawn the dead runner in v1 (out of scope).
- Items already dispatched whose sessions are still alive: out of scope for v1 re-attachment; the re-claiming runner handles them per its existing duplicate-protection (#298 issue-level claim protects against re-implementing).

### D-3. Proof isolation (CRITICAL)
- ALL proof runs use an ISOLATED Gateway instance on an alternate port AND an ISOLATED data dir - the live Gateway (:7878) and its real data dir (%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\gateway) must never be read from or written to by the test instance. Use the Gateway's existing data-dir/port override mechanism (env/config); if none exists for the data dir, adding one IS in scope (it is required to make this feature testable at all).
- Restart in the proof = stop the isolated Gateway process cleanly AND ALSO prove the kill -9 equivalent (process terminated without graceful shutdown) - the persistence must survive both, which is the point of write-through + atomic writes.

### Acceptance criteria (restated, precise)
- [ ] Unit tests: store round-trip (save/load preserves lists, order, claims), atomic-write behavior, corrupt/missing-file startup behavior (missing file = empty store + log line, NOT a crash; a corrupt file = explicit error log + refusal to silently overwrite until operator action, or a documented recovery behavior - state which and why).
- [ ] Isolated-instance proof, graceful restart: create lists with ordered items + a consumer claim via REST, restart, GET shows identical lists/order and the stale claim RELEASED with the documented log line.
- [ ] Isolated-instance proof, hard-kill: same but terminate the process; after relaunch the store is intact (no corruption, no loss).
- [ ] Interrupted-drain proof: start a drain (simulated session driver per the existing test patterns / #300 IImplSessionDriver abstraction is acceptable), restart the Gateway mid-drain, re-claim, and show the remaining queue continues in order - nothing silently lost.
- [ ] Live Gateway untouched: its data dir files unmodified (hash/timestamp before vs after), :7878 never bound by the test instance, fleet PID table before/after.

### Affected (confirmed)
- CcDirector.Gateway (WorkListStore persistence + GatewayHost load-on-start), CcDirector.Gateway.Tests. No skill changes expected; DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md only if conventions change (likely "No drift").

Proof target: docs/cencon/proof/issue-301/report.html with before/after REST captures for graceful + hard-kill restarts, the interrupted-drain continuation log, live-gateway untouched evidence.

Out of scope: auto-respawning dead runners, re-attaching to still-alive dispatched sessions, multi-gateway replication, any change to the live fleet.
CLAIM flow:in-progress by SOREN_NORTH/impl-loop-kgp5jk at 2026-06-11T09:51:33Z

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-301/report.html  (PR #321)

## Developer Agent report - issue #301

**Implemented:** `WorkListStore` persistence per the Definition-of-Ready spec - one `worklists.json` in the Gateway data dir (keyvault.json precedent, D-1), atomic temp+rename writes, write-through on every mutation, reload-on-start with stale-claim release logged as `[WorkListStore] Load: released stale claim on list=<name>, deadConsumer=<token>` (D-2). Corrupt-file recovery (documented choice): quarantine to `worklists.json.corrupt-<stamp>` + explicit error log + boot empty - bytes preserved for the operator, never silently overwritten, and one bad file never takes the whole Gateway down. The store path is a required ctor arg and `GatewayHost` gained a `workListsPath` override, so tests are compile-forced onto isolated paths (D-3).

**Proof (all in the report):**
- Unit: 9 new persistence tests green (round-trip, write-through per mutation, atomic residue, missing file, corrupt quarantine, stale-claim release, interrupted-drain continuation via the #300 `IImplSessionDriver` fake).
- Graceful restart (isolated instance, port 17878, `CC_DIRECTOR_ROOT` temp dir): lists + order identical after `POST /shutdown` + relaunch; stale claim `runner-graceful-proof` released with the documented log line.
- Hard kill (`Stop-Process -Force` on the path-confirmed test pid): store intact including an item appended seconds before the kill; stale claim `runner-hardkill-proof` released; no `.tmp`/`.corrupt` residue.
- Live Gateway untouched: gateway-dir SHA256s, keyvault.json hash, :7878 owner (pid 26640) and fleet PID table identical before/after; no `worklists.json` ever appeared in the real data root; test port/process fully cleaned up.
- Build clean (0 warnings/0 errors); Gateway suite 462/463 - the 1 failure is the pre-existing environmental `DictationEndpointTests.FullPipeline_transcribes_phase0_clip2_with_realtime_provider` (live OpenAI realtime; fails identically without this change; no dictation surface touched).

I believe this is finished. Handing off to QA.

## QA Agent verdict - PASS (independent verification)

QA proof: [docs/cencon/proof/issue-301/qa-report.html](docs/cencon/proof/issue-301/qa-report.html) (raw evidence in [docs/cencon/proof/issue-301/qa-evidence/](docs/cencon/proof/issue-301/qa-evidence/)), commit bf9b9a0 on PR #321.

All 7 criteria reproduced by QA itself (developer report used as context only):

1. **Build**: clean in an isolated QA worktree - 0 warnings / 0 errors.
2. **Unit tests**: 9/9 WorkListStorePersistenceTests green; Gateway suite 462/463 - the single failure is `DictationEndpointTests.FullPipeline_transcribes_phase0_clip2_with_realtime_provider`, which QA proved fails identically on origin/main (pre-existing environmental, not this PR).
3. **Graceful restart** (own isolated instance, port 27878, isolated CC_DIRECTOR_ROOT): lists + order identical after POST /shutdown + relaunch; stale claim `qa-stale-runner-301` released with the documented `[WorkListStore] Load: released stale claim...` line.
4. **Hard kill** (`Stop-Process -Force` on the path-confirmed test pid): store intact including an item appended seconds before the kill; no .tmp/.corrupt residue.
5. **Interrupted drain**: simulated-driver test reproduced - new runner re-claims and continues 101/102/103 in persisted order, nothing lost.
6. **Code review lens**: atomic temp+rename, write-through on every mutation (code + test + live), missing-file = empty + log (live), corrupt-file = quarantine + explicit operator log + boots (live, QA corrupted the store itself), keyvault.json precedent path, no fallbacks, ASCII-only, no CenCon drift.
7. **Live fleet untouched**: before/after SHA256 + mtimes identical on the real gateway dir, real worklists.json absent throughout, :7878/:7470/pid 21128 unchanged, test port freed, isolated data dir removed.

Proceeding to squash-merge per the implementation-loop authorization (DECIDED D5).

title: [Loop] Persist named work lists across a Gateway restart

body:
Parent epic: #297

## Problem / Value

#270 adopted Option B: a work-list item is a structured `{ source, id, area? }` ref so the multi-source board is real from day one - but the v1 runner drives ONLY `source = github`; `devops` / `jira` refs are stored and displayed, not drained. To drive non-GitHub work, the queue runner needs a per-source adapter.

## Scope

- An adapter interface: given a `{ source, id }` ref - fetch the item, seed the implementation session, and read the loop's terminal state back into that source's status field.
- A `devops` adapter (Azure DevOps work items) and/or a `jira` adapter.
- Abstract the implementation-loop's GitHub-specific bits (the `flow:*` labels, `gh pr merge`) behind the adapter so the loop core is source-agnostic.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A `devops` (or `jira`) ref on a list is drained by the runner end-to-end: session seeded, work done, status written back to that source. (Proof: a non-github item driven to done.)
- [ ] The `github` path is unchanged (regression check).

## Affected

- `CcDirector.Gateway` (queue runner + adapter dispatch)
- `.claude/skills/implementation-loop` / `developer-agent` / `qa-agent` (source-agnostic flow)

Breadth item - lower priority than #298 (claim) and #299 (same-machine isolation), which harden the existing github path first.

comments.body:
## Product sharpening (Definition of Ready) - settles design decisions before flow:ready-dev

### D-1. Adapter choice: devops ONLY; jira OUT of scope
Azure DevOps is provable on this machine (az CLI + azure-devops extension installed, authenticated, org https://dev.azure.com/mindzie reachable). No Jira instance exists to prove against - a jira adapter would be untestable code, so it is OUT (the adapter CONTRACT must be documented so jira can follow the same shape later).

### D-2. Architecture: the adapter is a per-source contract, not a big C# layer
Today the Gateway runner does NOT perform tracker operations itself - the seeded Claude session does (gh for github), and the runner only (a) gates by source (WorkListRunner.RunnableSource = "github"), (b) seeds the session, (c) correlates the IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL sentinel by item id. Keep that shape:
- Gateway (C#): replace the single RunnableSource gate with a per-source adapter dispatch (an ISourceAdapter or equivalent: IsRunnable, BuildSeedPrompt(item), terminal-sentinel correlation key). github and devops are runnable; jira remains skip-with-note (regression: skip note text for jira stays).
- Session/skill side: the seeded session performs the tracker operations for its source. For devops that is az boards CLI (matches the gh-CLI precedent; az is already authenticated on the Gateway host; no new secret handling in v1 - document az login + azure-devops extension as a host prerequisite).
- The sentinel contract (DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md 7a) is source-agnostic already (issue: <id>); for devops, <id> = the work item id. No sentinel change.

### D-3. Status mapping (the flow:* equivalent for devops)
The implementation-loop skill gets a devops mode for CLAIM and WRITE-BACK only; all engineering mechanics (branch, PR, squash-merge, proof under docs/cencon/proof/) stay in the GitHub CODE repo - the work item names the target repo in its description (default thefrederiksen/cc-director in v1):
- CLAIM: work item State -> the project's in-progress state + a discussion comment "CLAIM by <director>/<session> at <utc>" (same verify-after-claim shape as #298: oldest CLAIM comment wins).
- done: State -> the project's done state + comment with the merged PR URL.
- needs-human: State stays in-progress + tag "needs-human" + comment.
- failed: comment + State back to the initial/proposed state.
- State names: v1 pins a static mapping table in the skill for the two common process templates (Basic: To Do/Doing/Done; Agile: New/Active/Closed) chosen by probing the item's CURRENT state names via az boards work-item show. No dynamic discovery beyond that.

### D-4. Proof environment
Scratch work item created by the proof in org https://dev.azure.com/mindzie, project "Z_Archive_DataWarehouse" (archived, zero traffic), title prefixed "[TEST cc-director #300]". The item describes a TRIVIAL but REAL cc-director change (a one-line docs note is acceptable) so "driven to done" is honest: runner seeds session -> loop implements -> PR squash-merged -> State written back to done with the PR link. After capturing proof screenshots, the scratch work item is DELETED (az boards work-item delete) so the archive project stays pristine; the committed report is the durable proof.
- The end-to-end run uses an ISOLATED Gateway + Director on alternate ports inside isolated worktrees (the #299 scripts/agent-session-isolation.ps1 harness; the live fleet :7878/:7470/:7887 is never touched).
- If a FULL seeded-session end-to-end is impractical to drive autonomously inside the proof, the minimum acceptable proof is: (a) runner-level test proving a devops ref is dispatched (session-seed request emitted with the devops seed prompt, sentinel correlated, write-back invoked), PLUS (b) a live az write-back demonstration on the scratch item (claim -> done transitions + comments actually visible in DevOps). State which level was achieved.

### Acceptance criteria (restated, precise)
- [ ] Gateway adapter dispatch: source=devops items are runnable (seeded, sentinel-correlated, status written back); jira items still skip with the existing note. Unit tests cover dispatch for github/devops/jira.
- [ ] devops end-to-end proof per D-4 on the scratch work item: claim transition, terminal write-back (done + PR link comment) visible in Azure DevOps screenshots.
- [ ] github path unchanged: existing WorkListRunner github tests still green; no behavior change for source=github.
- [ ] Skills (implementation-loop / developer-agent / qa-agent) + DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md document the devops mode and the adapter contract.
- [ ] Live fleet untouched during proof (before/after PID table, as in #299).

Proof target: docs/cencon/proof/issue-300/report.html with the DevOps screenshots (work item state history + comments), runner logs, test results, fleet table.

Out of scope: jira adapter implementation, PAT-based REST auth (az CLI session is the v1 transport), multi-repo routing beyond the description-named default, product-loop roles (#247/#248).
CLAIM flow:in-progress by SOREN_NORTH/impl-loop-kgp5jk at 2026-06-11T08:59:17Z

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-300/report.html  (PR #320)

## Developer Agent report

Implemented per-source adapter dispatch in the Gateway queue runner (ISourceAdapter + SourceAdapters registry): source=devops refs are now runnable - seeded with `/implementation-loop --source devops <workItemId>`, sentinel-correlated by the work item id, terminal signal recorded. jira refs keep the skip-with-note behavior; the github seed prompt is byte-identical to v1 (regression-tested).

**Proof level achieved: a + b (the D-4 minimum bar).**
- (a) Runner-level dispatch proof against the REAL GatewayHost over HTTP (DevopsAdapterDispatchProofTests): devops seeded in devops mode, sentinel correlated, signal recorded; jira skipped; github unchanged. 28/28 adapter/runner tests green.
- (b) Live az write-back demo on scratch item 7659 in Z_Archive_DataWarehouse: To Do -> In Progress (+ CLAIM comment, verify-after-claim won) -> Done (+ comment `done: merged https://github.com/thefrederiksen/cc-director/pull/320`), full az CLI JSON evidence committed; the scratch item was then deleted (code 200) and a residual query confirms the archive project is pristine.

**Spec deviation, documented:** the proof project turned out to use the Scrum template (To Do / In Progress / Done) - D-3's two-row Basic/Agile table could not express it, and the current-state-name probe is ambiguous (`To Do` opens both Basic and Scrum; DevOps rejected Basic's `Doing` live). Fix stays within D-3's static-mapping intent: the implementation-loop skill now pins Basic/Scrum/Agile and probes the work item TYPE's allowed state list (one static lookup, no dynamic state-graph discovery).

Docs/skills delivered: implementation-loop v0.8 (devops mode: claim + write-back mapping), developer-agent v0.6 + qa-agent v0.6 (devops notes), DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md Section 7b (the adapter contract).

Build: solution 0 errors. Full Gateway.Tests 433/434 - the single failure is the PRE-EXISTING real-network DictationEndpointTests realtime-provider test (OPENAI_API_KEY-gated, untouched by this change, unrelated to #300). Fleet untouched by this session; the gateway/cockpit PID change mid-proof was the Gateway's own self-update relaunch (evidence in fleet-after.txt).

QA verification: PASS - all 5 acceptance criteria independently verified.

QA proof: [docs/cencon/proof/issue-300/qa-report.html](docs/cencon/proof/issue-300/qa-report.html) (PR #320, commit 452de3a)

- Build clean in QA's own worktree: 0 warnings, 0 errors.
- Adapter dispatch + github regression: 26/26 green in QA's own run (SourceAdapterTests, WorkListRunnerTests, DevopsAdapterDispatchProofTests, WorkListRunnerEndpointProofTests). Full Gateway.Tests 432/434; both failures are pre-existing DictationEndpointTests real-network tests, proven identical on origin/main.
- Live az write-back on QA-owned scratch item 7660 (Z_Archive_DataWarehouse, Scrum process): the shipped state-probe + mapping table resolved Scrum as documented; To Do -> In Progress + CLAIM comment (verify-after-claim won) -> Done + PR-link comment; item destroyed, project-wide sweep shows zero residue.
- Static/method: ASCII-clean, sentinel parser untouched, github seed byte-identical, skills v0.6/v0.8 + DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md 7b consistent with the implementation, no CenCon drift.
- Live fleet untouched (before == after PID/port/task table).

Proof level verified: a + b per D-4. Proceeding to squash-merge per DECIDED D5.

title: [Loop] Source adapters: make devops/jira work-list refs runnable (v1 runs github only)

body:
Parent epic: #297

## Problem / Value

#270 Constraint 1: same-machine parallelism is gated by the loop's single reserved test Director (slot-5), single build output, and single Control API port. Two implementation sessions on one machine collide on all three, so the loop only parallelizes ACROSS machines. Same-machine worktree parallelism needs per-session isolation of those three resources.

## Scope

- Per-session test slot (not a fixed slot-5): allocate a free slot/port per implementation session.
- Per-session build output dir so concurrent `dotnet build` runs do not clobber each other's bin/obj.
- Per-session Control API port.
- One git worktree per session (already the model cross-machine; make it correct same-machine).

The manual pattern used to verify #198 and #199 - an isolated Gateway + Cockpit on alternate ports inside a dedicated worktree - is a hand-rolled version of exactly this. Generalize it into the loop.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Two implementation sessions run concurrently on one machine, each in its own worktree, building and proving without colliding on slot / build dir / port. (Proof: two parallel runs both reaching a clean terminal signal.)
- [ ] No shared-resource contention errors (port-in-use, locked bin, slot collision) in either run's logs.

## Affected

- `.claude/skills/developer-agent` + `qa-agent` (slot / port / build-dir allocation)
- the `cc-director-launch` scheduled-task / test-Director launch path
- `docs/cencon/DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md`

Pairs with #298 (issue-claim) - both are prerequisites for safely scaling the loop beyond one-at-a-time on a single machine.

comments.body:
## Product sharpening (Definition of Ready) - settles design decisions before flow:ready-dev

This comment makes the spec precise so the Developer Agent does not have to invent design intent. It settles the four open decisions and pins the proof bar.

### D-1. Slot allocation policy
- Slots are allocated DYNAMICALLY per implementation session from slot 6 upward. Slot 5 remains the legacy/manual default and may be in use by a human-driven session on a busy machine - the allocator NEVER assumes slot 5 is free. Slots 1-4 and the main build are absolutely off-limits (CLAUDE.md rule 0b).
- "Free" means: no running process whose image path ends in cc-director<N>.exe AND no registered scheduled task cc-director<N>-launch owned by a live session. The allocator probes both and takes the lowest free N >= 6.

### D-2. Build-output isolation comes from the worktree
- Each implementation session works in its OWN git worktree (git worktree add ... off origin/main). Building INSIDE the worktree isolates bin/obj AND the worktree's local_builds for free - no shared-tree publish race (this is the proven fix from the #290 redeploy-gateway work).
- scripts/local-build-avalonia.ps1 -Slot <N> run from the worktree root builds to <worktree>/local_builds/cc-director<N>.exe. No changes to the build script's output convention are expected; the deliverable wires the worktree+slot pairing.

### D-3. Per-session launch task
- One scheduled task per slot, named cc-director<N>-launch, registered idempotently (Register-ScheduledTask -Force) pointing at the session worktree's exe with WorkingDirectory set (Avalonia requirement). Launch via Start-ScheduledTask only (rule 0b - clean svchost parentage). The session UNREGISTERS its task at teardown.

### D-4. Control API port
- The Director already self-allocates a free Control API port at startup. The per-session deliverable is deterministic DISCOVERY, not static assignment: from the scheduled-task launch, resolve the new PID (process whose path is the session's slot exe), then read %LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\logs\director\director-<date>-<PID>.log for "Kestrel listening on http://0.0.0.0:<port>". No two sessions can collide because the OS hands out the port.

### Deliverables (Affected, made concrete)
1. A new script scripts/agent-session-isolation.ps1 with subcommands (or parameters) for: allocate (slot + worktree pairing), launch (register task + start + resolve PID/port, emitting a machine-readable session manifest), and teardown (stop ONLY the session's own PID after path confirmation, unregister task, remove worktree). ASCII-only output.
2. .claude/skills/developer-agent/skill.md + qa-agent/skill.md: replace the fixed "slot 5 + cc-director-launch" instructions with the per-session isolation script flow.
3. .claude/skills/implementation-loop/skill.md: update the concurrency-rule section (it currently claims safety BECAUSE of single slot-5; after this issue the safety claim is per-session isolation).
4. docs/cencon/DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md: Sections 6 (DoD item 3/4) updated to the per-session flow.

### Acceptance criteria - precise proof bar (interprets AC1 at mechanism level)
AC1 does NOT require two full product dev->QA loops on real issues. The contract of THIS issue is resource isolation; proof is two CONCURRENT isolated session harness runs on one machine:
- [ ] Run A and Run B execute CONCURRENTLY (overlapping in time, proven by timestamps), each: allocate distinct slot (>= 6) -> own worktree off origin/main -> build -> launch via its own per-slot scheduled task -> probe its own Control API port (GET succeeds) -> teardown (process gone, task unregistered, worktree removed).
- [ ] The two runs got DIFFERENT slots, DIFFERENT worktrees, DIFFERENT ports; neither run's log contains port-in-use, file-locked (bin/obj), or slot-collision errors.
- [ ] The LIVE fleet (Gateway :7878, Cockpit :7470, the user's Directors incl. :7887, main build + slots 1-4) is untouched: same PIDs before and after the proof run, no binds attempted on 7878/7470/7887.
- [ ] Skills + DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md updated as in Deliverables 2-4.

### Out of scope (unchanged)
- Issue-claim concurrency (#298, done), source adapters (#300), list persistence (#301), and any change to the Director's own port-allocation code (it already self-allocates; this issue is harness/script/skill work, possibly zero C# changes).

Proof target: docs/cencon/proof/issue-299/report.html showing the two concurrent run logs side by side with timestamps, slots, ports, and the before/after live-fleet PID table.
CLAIM flow:in-progress by SOREN_NORTH/impl-loop-kgp5jk at 2026-06-11T08:19:27Z

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-299/report.html  (PR #318)

## Developer Agent - ready for QA

### Release Notes
Implementation/QA sessions are per-session isolated on one machine: own slot (>= 6), own worktree build output, own per-slot scheduled task, own self-allocated Control API port. Two concurrent sessions cannot collide on slot, build dir, or port.

### Changes
- NEW `scripts/agent-session-isolation.ps1` (allocate / launch / teardown; ASCII output, JSON manifest). Slot reservation = per-slot task registration WITHOUT `-Force` (race has one winner); hard floor protects slots 1-5 and the main build; teardown stops only exact-image-path matches.
- Skills updated per Deliverables 2-4: developer-agent v0.5, qa-agent v0.5, implementation-loop v0.7, DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md Section 6 (DoD 3/4).

### Discovery during proof (mitigated in-scope)
The Director's `PortAllocator` probes a port BEFORE Kestrel binds it. Two Directors started in the same instant both allocated port 7892; the loser logged `Control API failed to start: ... address already in use` and ran with no Control API. Director C# is out of scope per the sharpened spec, so `launch` now serializes the start-to-port-bound window through a machine-wide named mutex, and a failed launch stops its own path-confirmed process (no orphans). Recommend a follow-up issue for a Director-side bind-and-hold port allocation.

### How to Test
Run two `docs/cencon/proof/issue-299/proof-runner.ps1` instances concurrently in two scratch worktrees off origin/main; check both logs for distinct slots/ports, overlapping timestamps, probe 200, and full teardown asserts; diff the fleet snapshot before/after.

### Expected Result
Both runs `result=SUCCESS`, zero contention errors, live fleet (Gateway :7878, Cockpit :7470, Director :7887, user tasks) byte-identical before/after.

### Proof run (committed)
Run A: slot 6, port 7893, PID 40124. Run B: slot 7, port 7891, PID 35984. Both Directors alive concurrently 08:41:26-08:41:54Z; both logs end with all teardown asserts. Fleet before/after unchanged (PIDs 21128 / 42928 / 65232).

I believe this is finished.

## QA Agent - VERIFIED, all acceptance criteria met (PASS)

Independent reproduction in QA-owned worktrees (Developer artifacts treated as context only). QA proof: [docs/cencon/proof/issue-299/qa-report.html](docs/cencon/proof/issue-299/qa-report.html) (PR #318), with raw logs qa-run-a.log / qa-run-b.log and fleet snapshots qa-fleet-before.txt / qa-fleet-after.txt alongside.

- AC1 (concurrent isolated lifecycle): PASS. QA-RUN-A (slot 6, port 7894, pid 44152) and QA-RUN-B (slot 7, port 7895, pid 48208) started 3 ms apart; Directors concurrently alive ~18 s; both completed allocate -> build -> launch (per-slot scheduled task) -> probe 200 -> teardown with all asserts.
- AC2 (distinct resources, zero contention): PASS. Different slots/worktrees/ports; no port-in-use, file-locked, or slot-collision errors. Bonus: run B LOST the slot-6 registration race to run A and cleanly moved to slot 7 - the atomic task-registration arbiter observed working live.
- AC3 (live fleet untouched): PASS. Gateway 42928 (:7878), Cockpit 65232 (:7470), Director 21128, :7887 owner - byte-identical before/after; test ports were 7894/7895 only; Gateway /healthz 200 after the runs.
- AC4 (deliverables 2-4): PASS. developer-agent v0.5, qa-agent v0.5, implementation-loop v0.7 concurrency rule, DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md Section 6 DoD 3/4 - all reviewed against the script behavior.

Static: ASCII-only (byte scan), PS 5.1 (0 parse errors), allocator hard floor 6 (manifest floor check blocks launch/teardown below 6 too), exact-image-path teardown safety, launch mutex + failed-launch orphan stop. Regression: dotnet build cc-director.sln clean (0 warnings / 0 errors) at the PR head. The single PR CI test failure (RecordingIngestServiceTests.Worker_TranscribesQueuedRecording_EndToEnd) is a pre-existing main flake - this PR changes zero C#; main CI is red on 5 of the last 8 merged pushes.

Proceeding to squash-merge per the implementation-loop authorization (D5).

title: [Loop] Same-machine parallelism: isolate test slot / build output / Control API port per implementation session

body:
Parent epic: #297

## Problem / Value

The implementation-loop has NO guard against two loops (or a loop + a manual run) picking up the SAME issue. #270 AC4 enforces "one consumer per LIST", but issue selection (implementation-loop Step 0: "pick the oldest open `flow:ready-dev`") has no lock. On 2026-06-10 two concurrent loops both implemented #199, producing duplicate PRs #294 and #296 plus a near-collision pushing the same branch. Wasteful at best, branch-corrupting at worst.

## Proposed approach

Claim the issue atomically at loop Step 0 via a label transition that removes it from every other loop's selection set the instant work starts:

- On select: `flow:ready-dev` -> `flow:in-progress` (new label), recording the claimant (Director id / session id) in a comment.
- The selection query (Step 0 / `--all`) picks oldest `flow:ready-dev` ONLY; an in-progress issue is invisible to other loops.
- Terminal paths release honestly: PASS -> `flow:done`; a bounce stays in the existing `flow:qa-failed` / `flow:ready-qa` cycle; needs-human / abnormal -> `flow:needs-human`.
- A crashed/abandoned claim must be reclaimable (stale-claim sweep by age, or manual) so an issue is never stranded invisible forever.
- The GitHub label set is the lock (single source of truth - already how state is carried); no new store.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Two loops started against the same `flow:ready-dev` issue: exactly one claims it; the other selects a different issue or reports none. (Proof: two runs, one IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL sentinel per issue, no duplicate PR.)
- [ ] A claimed (in-progress) issue is excluded from the no-arg / `--all` selection query. (Proof: query output.)
- [ ] Every terminal path leaves the label in a correct, non-stuck state; a claim never strands an issue invisible forever (stale-claim recovery demonstrated). (Proof: label transitions across PASS / bounce / needs-human / abnormal.)
- [ ] New label(s) added to the repo and documented in the DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md flow state machine.

## Affected

- `.claude/skills/implementation-loop/skill.md` (Step 0 claim, Step 4 release)
- `.claude/skills/developer-agent` + `qa-agent` (flow transitions)
- `docs/cencon/DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md` (flow state machine + new label)
- repo labels (`flow:in-progress` / `flow:claimed`)

## Reproduction (today)

#199: loop A -> PR #296; sibling loop B -> PR #294; both complete, same-branch contention. #296 closed as the duplicate.

comments.body: Claimed by an implementation-loop run (kicking off #298). It currently has no flow:* label so no concurrent loop will select it; implementing now. Will transition to flow:ready-qa (DEV) then flow:done (merged) per normal flow.

## Developer Agent - implemented, handing to QA

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-298/report.html  (PR #315)

### Release Notes
Adds an issue-level claim (`flow:in-progress`) so two implementation-loops can never implement the same issue - closes the #199 duplicate-PR race (#294/#296).

### Changes
- New repo label `flow:in-progress` (the claim marker).
- implementation-loop Step 0.2 claims the selected issue (`flow:ready-dev` -> `flow:in-progress`) with a timestamped `CLAIM` comment; Step 0.0 sweeps stale (>60min) claims back to `flow:ready-dev`; Step 4 claim-release gate ensures `flow:in-progress` never lingers at a terminal stop.
- Verify-after-claim re-read: oldest `CLAIM` comment wins a race, the loser backs off (honest best-effort, residual sub-second window documented - not a false atomicity claim).
- developer-agent hand-off/reject/escalate now release the claim.
- DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md: new `flow:in-progress` state, DECIDED D6, state-machine diagram, full Section 4a.

### How to Test
Run `bash docs/cencon/proof/issue-298/claim_mechanism_proof.sh` (creates + auto-closes a throwaway fixture issue; touches no real production issue). It exercises every AC and writes `docs/cencon/proof/issue-298/results.json`.

### Expected Result
`allPass: true`. AC1 two concurrent claims -> exactly one winner, loser backs off. AC2 a claimed issue is absent from the `flow:ready-dev` selection set. AC3 done/needs-human paths release the claim; an aged claim is reclaimed and reselectable; a fresh claim is protected. AC4 `flow:in-progress` exists + is documented.

### Before / After
Before: two loops both selected the oldest `flow:ready-dev` and both implemented it (#199 -> duplicate PRs). After: the first loop to select claims `flow:in-progress`; the issue leaves every other loop's selection set instantly, and the race loser deterministically backs off.

Build: docs/skill-only changeset, no C# compiled. Live fleet (Gateway 7878 / Cockpit 7470 / Director 7887) confirmed up and never touched.

I believe this is finished.

## QA Agent - PASS (independent verification)

Verified all 4 acceptance criteria independently against a FRESH throwaway fixture (#316, created + closed by QA, real queues untouched). This is a process/skill-doc change (no C# compiled), verified deterministically against the documented claim recipe.

- **AC1 - exactly one loop claims, loser backs off**: PASS. Two racing claims on #316 -> 1 winner (oldest CLAIM comment is the deterministic arbiter), 1 loser. Confirmed the oldest CLAIM comment names the winner.
- **AC2 - claimed issue excluded from selection**: PASS. A `flow:ready-dev` issue is selectable; the instant it flips to `flow:in-progress` it leaves the `flow:ready-dev` selection set (confirmed with the settle-wait for index eventual-consistency). Label state after claim is exactly `flow:in-progress` (no `flow:ready-dev`).
- **AC3 - terminal release + stale recovery**: PASS. `in-progress -> done` and `in-progress -> needs-human` both release the claim (no stuck `flow:in-progress`). Stale sweep: a fresh claim (age 0 < 60 min) is PROTECTED; an aged claim is reclaimed `in-progress -> ready-dev` and returns to the selection set (never stranded invisible).
- **AC4 - label exists + documented**: PASS. `flow:in-progress` label present on the repo; documented in DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md (label table, Section 4a, D6, state-machine diagram).

**Non-atomic race - honestly addressed (not hand-waved)**: Section 4a specifies a real verify-after-claim re-read using comment `createdAt` ordering as the arbiter and an explicit loser-back-off; the residual sub-second window is documented, not papered over. My AC1 test confirmed the back-off actually fires.

**Internal consistency**: loop Step 0.2 claim <-> developer Step 5.6 hand-off (removes BOTH in-progress + ready-dev) <-> loop Step 4 claim-release gate <-> qa-agent awareness note are consistent; the `CLAIM flow:in-progress by ` / `STALE-CLAIM SWEEP` marker strings match across loop, dev, and harness.

Live fleet (Gateway 7878 / Cockpit 7470 / Director 7887) confirmed up and untouched. Throwaway fixture #316 cleaned up (closed, no flow label). Squash-merging to main.

title: [Loop] Issue-level claim to prevent two loops implementing the same issue (duplicate-prevention)

body:
## Summary

The autonomous implementation-loop + queue runner (epic #270) shipped v1 and works end-to-end. This epic collects the robustness/breadth work that #270 explicitly DEFERRED to its "Later" roadmap and never filed as issues, plus a concurrency gap proven today. v1 is safe for ONE loop at a time; this epic makes it safe and complete for MANY.

## Why now

Running two implementation-loops concurrently against the same issue (#199) on 2026-06-10 produced TWO complete duplicate PRs (#294 and #296) and a near-collision pushing the same branch. The loop's "one consumer per LIST" guard (#270 AC4) does NOT guard at the ISSUE level. This is a live, reproduced gap - not theoretical.

## Children (dependency-ordered)

- [x] #298 - (lead) Issue-level claim / duplicate-prevention - has a concrete repro from today
- [x] #299 - Same-machine parallelism: per-session isolation of test slot / build output / Control API port
- [x] #300 - Source adapters: make devops/jira refs runnable (v1 runs github only)
- [x] #301 - List persistence across a Gateway restart

#298 and #299 harden the existing single-source github path for concurrent runs (do these first); #300 and #301 add breadth/durability and can follow.

## Out of scope

- The product-loop role layer (#247 / #248) - tracked separately.
- Wingman queue visibility (#239).

## Rollout note

These are filed WITHOUT `flow:ready-dev` on purpose: until the issue-claim mechanism (#298) exists, promoting one to ready-dev risks the very duplicate race this epic fixes. Promote ONE AT A TIME, #298 first.

comments.body:
## Loop hardening complete - all four children merged to main

All children of this epic are flow:done, each driven through the implementation-loop (sharpen -> claim -> DEV -> independent QA -> squash-merge), with proof committed under docs/cencon/proof/issue-<n>/.

| Child | PR | What shipped |
|-------|----|--------------|
| #298 issue-level claim | #315 | flow:in-progress claim + verify-after-claim + stale-claim sweep (merged before this run; relied on throughout it) |
| #299 same-machine parallelism | #318 | scripts/agent-session-isolation.ps1: dynamic slot >= 6 allocation, per-worktree build isolation, per-slot scheduled tasks, deterministic PID->log->port discovery, machine-wide launch mutex (mitigates a real PortAllocator startup race found during dev); skills + DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md updated off the fixed slot-5 pattern. Proven with two concurrent isolated runs (slots 6/7, distinct ports), live fleet untouched - twice (dev + independent QA). |
| #300 source adapters | #320 | Per-source adapter dispatch in the Gateway runner (ISourceAdapter: github + devops runnable, jira skip-with-note preserved); implementation-loop devops mode (claim/write-back via az boards, State mapping for Basic/Scrum/Agile - Scrum added after a live spec defect was caught against the real project); proof level a+b per the sharpened spec: 26-28 dispatch/regression tests green + live az claim->done write-back on scratch work items (created and deleted, zero residue in dev.azure.com/mindzie). |
| #301 list persistence | #321 | WorkListStore write-through to worklists.json (atomic temp+rename, keyvault.json conventions), reload-on-start with stale-consumer-claim release (logged); survives graceful restart AND hard kill; interrupted drain continues in order. Proven on isolated instances (alternate port + isolated data dir), live gateway hash-verified untouched. |

### Notes for the record
- Every child was sharpened with a Definition-of-Ready comment settling design decisions BEFORE flow:ready-dev (the binding spec each DEV/QA phase verified against).
- Zero QA bounces across all three issues driven in this run.
- All work happened in isolated worktrees off origin/main; the live fleet (Gateway :7878, Cockpit :7470, Director :7887) stayed up throughout (the one Gateway/Cockpit PID change observed mid-run traces to the concurrent #317 gateway-proxy deploy by another session, not this loop).
- Leave-clean: no loop branches, worktrees, PRs, scheduled tasks, test processes, or DevOps scratch items remain. The shared checkout currently carries UNCOMMITTED foreign WIP (session-type/sidebar files) belonging to another live session - untouched by this loop, noted here so it is not attributed to it.

Epic complete. Closing.
All four children merged and verified; see closing summary above.

title: [Loop] EPIC: Loop hardening - safe concurrent runs, same-machine parallelism, source adapters, list persistence

body:
## Symptom

"Copy Handover Info" (session rail context menu) copies a Control API URL that only works on the machine that produced it:

```
Name: bpmnStudio - research
Session ID: be3ee447-ef39-462a-85ab-2b067df85c1c
Repo: D:\ReposMindzie\mindzieWeb
Director ID: 37cea6e2-da6d-418c-a5d5-7eeb417da297
Machine: SOREN_NORTH
Version: 0.6.22.0
Control API: http://127.0.0.1:7887      <-- useless from any other machine
```

The whole point of this block is to hand a session to **another** agent/machine so it can locate and talk to the session over the Control API. A `127.0.0.1` URL is unreachable from anywhere but the originating box. When this Director is on a tailnet (the default), the copied block should advertise the **tailnet front door** the session is actually reachable at, e.g. `https://soren_north.<tailnet>.ts.net:7887` -- the same address the Director already advertises to the Gateway when it registers.

This is the policy the app already commits to elsewhere ("CC Director never opens a localhost URL", `MainWindow.axaml.cs:1424-1448`), but two UI surfaces still leak loopback. User's framing: **if Tailscale is set up and not explicitly turned off, Tailscale is the default and we should never surface a localhost URL anywhere a remote consumer will read it.**

## Reproduction

1. Run a Director on a Tailscale-connected machine (Tailscale up, logged in).
2. Right-click a session in the rail -> "Copy Handover Info".
3. Paste. The `Control API:` line reads `http://127.0.0.1:<port>`.
4. From a different tailnet machine, try to use that URL -> connection refused / unreachable.

Expected: the `Control API:` line is the tailnet-reachable URL (`https://<magicdns>:<port>`). Only when Tailscale is genuinely unavailable should a loopback value appear, and it must be clearly labelled local-only.

## Root cause

The handover-copy builder hardcodes loopback instead of resolving this node's tailnet identity:

`src/CcDirector.Avalonia/MainWindow.axaml.cs:1142-1144`
```csharp
var port = app?.ControlApiHost?.Port;
if (port is > 0)
    lines.Add($"Control API: http://127.0.0.1:{port}");
```

The infrastructure to do this correctly already exists and is used by the registration path:

- `src/CcDirector.Core/Network/TailscaleIdentity.cs` -- `TryGetMagicDnsName()` returns this node's MagicDNS name (e.g. `soren_north.tailXXXX.ts.net`) or `null` when Tailscale is unavailable.
- `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/GatewayClient.cs:469-500` -- `ResolveTailnetEndpoint()` already builds exactly the right value: `https://<magicdns>:<port>` (the Tailscale Serve front door; the Director binds Kestrel to loopback only, and the Gateway's `tailscale serve --https=<port> http://localhost:<port>` maps the public HTTPS port to it). It returns `""` when there is no tailnet identity and refuses to advertise loopback. The "Control API" value in the handover block is **the same address** and should be derived the same way.

So the handover-copy and Help dialog re-implement endpoint formatting by hand and diverge from the one place that gets it right.

## Audit -- other loopback usages (the "go further" sweep)

I swept all of `src/**/*.cs`, `src/**/*.razor`, `src/**/wwwroot/*.js` for `127.0.0.1` / `localhost`. Only **two** surfaces actually leak loopback to a remote consumer. Everything else is correctly loopback (same-process / same-desktop) and must NOT be touched.

### Defects to fix (loopback handed to a remote consumer)

1. **`src/CcDirector.Avalonia/MainWindow.axaml.cs:1144`** -- "Copy Handover Info" clipboard block (the reported bug).
2. **`src/CcDirector.Avalonia/HelpDialog.axaml.cs:69`** -- the About/Help dialog's "Control endpoint" row:
   ```csharp
   controlEndpoint = host.Port > 0 ? $"http://127.0.0.1:{host.Port}" : "(no port)";
   ```
   This dialog has a "Copy" button (`_copyText`, line 45) explicitly built so the block can be pasted/shared, so the same leak applies.

### Confirmed CORRECT loopback -- do NOT change (documented so the developer doesn't "fix" them)

These are loopback by design because the URL never leaves the machine, or because the code already refuses loopback for remote callers:

- `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/GatewayClient.cs:469-500` `ResolveTailnetEndpoint()` -- already advertises MagicDNS, never loopback. **This is the reference implementation.**
- `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/ControlApiHost.cs` -- Control API binds to loopback ONLY by design; remote access is via Tailscale Serve. Correct.
- `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/InstanceRegistration.cs:51` `ControlEndpoint = http://127.0.0.1:<port>` -- written to a local `instances/<id>.json` consumed only by the **same-machine** Gateway via FileSystemWatcher; HTTP-registered Directors override with the tailnet endpoint (`DirectorRegistry`). Stays on the box. Correct.
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Api/GatewayEndpoints.cs` `DeriveDirectorBaseUrl()` (~1351-1374) -- has a `callerIsLoopback` guard that already refuses to hand a loopback URL to a non-loopback caller. Correct.
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Tailscale/TailscaleServeProvisioner.cs` -- `http://localhost:<port>` is the **backend target** of a `tailscale serve` mapping (must be loopback). Correct.
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Cockpit/CockpitSupervisor.cs:127` -- binds Cockpit Kestrel to loopback (`ASPNETCORE_URLS`); fronted by Serve. Correct.
- `src/CcDirector.GatewayApp/*` (GatewayTrayController, SettingsWindow, Program) -- same-process health/shutdown probes and "open settings in the browser on THIS desktop". Correct.
- `src/CcDirector.Avalonia/MainWindow.axaml.cs:1785` (`/sessions/{id}/view` "Open in Browser") and the in-process dictation roundtrip -- open on the local desktop only. Correct.
- `src/CcDirector.Core/Browser/WorkflowRunner.cs` -- in-process calls to the local browser daemon. Correct.
- `src/CcDirector.Cockpit/Program.cs:12`, `appsettings.json`, `launchSettings.json` -- dev defaults / config, overridable. Correct.
- All `*.Tests` loopback usages. Correct.

## Fix plan (executable cold)

**Goal:** the two remote-facing surfaces emit the tailnet endpoint when Tailscale is up, and only a clearly-labelled local value otherwise. Centralise the policy so it can't drift again.

1. **Add one shared resolver** for "the address this Director's Control API is reachable at", so the policy lives in ONE place (the user's "spread that across the whole application"). Put it next to the existing identity helper:
   - In `src/CcDirector.Core/Network/TailscaleIdentity.cs`, add:
     ```csharp
     /// The Control API endpoint to advertise for a given local port: the Tailscale
     /// Serve front door (https://<magicdns>:<port>) when this node has a tailnet
     /// identity, else null (no remotely-reachable URL exists).
     public static string? TryGetControlApiEndpointForPort(int port)
     {
         var dns = TryGetMagicDnsName();
         return dns is null ? null : $"https://{dns}:{port}";
     }
     ```
     (This is exactly the shape `GatewayClient.ResolveTailnetEndpoint()` produces at line 482.)
   - Optional but recommended: refactor `GatewayClient.ResolveTailnetEndpoint()` to call this helper for the MagicDNS branch, so registration and the UI share one definition. Keep its existing `_config.TailnetEndpoint` override fallback. Do not change its `""`-means-local-only contract.

2. **Fix the handover copy** -- `MainWindow.axaml.cs` `CopySessionNameAndId` (around line 1142-1144). Resolving MagicDNS shells the tailscale CLI (up to ~5s), so do it off the UI thread. The method is already `async`:
   ```csharp
   var port = app?.ControlApiHost?.Port;
   if (port is > 0)
   {
       var endpoint = await Task.Run(() =>
           TailscaleIdentity.TryGetControlApiEndpointForPort(port.Value));
       lines.Add(endpoint is not null
           ? $"Control API: {endpoint}"
           : $"Control API: http://127.0.0.1:{port} (local only - Tailscale unavailable)");
   }
   ```
   Add a `using CcDirector.Core.Network;` if not present.

3. **Fix the Help/About dialog** -- `HelpDialog.axaml.cs`. `BuildInfoRows()` is static and called synchronously from the dialog's load path; per the Responsive-UI rule it must not block on the CLI. Make the dialog populate rows asynchronously (show the dialog immediately, fill the "Control endpoint" row when resolution returns), or resolve the endpoint in the dialog's `Loaded`/`OnOpened` via `await Task.Run(...)` and update the row + `_copyText`. The value:
   ```csharp
   var endpoint = TailscaleIdentity.TryGetControlApiEndpointForPort(host.Port);
   controlEndpoint = host.Port <= 0 ? "(no port)"
       : endpoint ?? $"http://127.0.0.1:{host.Port} (local only - Tailscale unavailable)";
   ```

4. **Tests** (`src/CcDirector.Core.Tests/Network/TailscaleIdentityTests.cs` already exists -- extend it):
   - `TryGetControlApiEndpointForPort_NoTailscale_ReturnsNull` (mock/seam the MagicDNS resolver to null -- mirror how `GatewayClient` tests pin `MagicDnsResolver`; may require extracting the resolver as an injectable `Func<string?>` or testing a pure formatter that takes the dns name + port).
   - `TryGetControlApiEndpointForPort_WithMagicDns_ReturnsHttpsFrontDoor` -> asserts `https://<name>:<port>`.
   - A formatter-level test asserting the handover/help "local only" fallback string is used when the endpoint is null.

5. **Acceptance:**
   - On a tailnet machine: "Copy Handover Info" and the Help dialog "Control endpoint" both read `https://<magicdns>:<port>` (matches what the Gateway shows for this Director).
   - With Tailscale stopped: both read `http://127.0.0.1:<port> (local only - Tailscale unavailable)`.
   - Paste the tailnet value on a second tailnet machine and confirm `GET <endpoint>/healthz` answers.

## Out of scope / notes

- Do NOT change any of the "Confirmed CORRECT" list above -- those loopback uses are required.
- This does not add a network listener; the Director still binds Kestrel to loopback. We are only correcting the *advertised* address strings to match the Serve front door that already exists.

comments.body:
## Developer Agent - Implemented, ready for QA

**Proof:** docs/cencon/proof/issue-293/report.html  (PR #295)

### Release Notes
"Copy Handover Info" and the About/Help dialog now advertise the tailnet front door `https://<magicdns>:<port>` when Tailscale is up (the same address the Director advertises to the Gateway), and a clearly-labelled `http://127.0.0.1:<port> (local only - Tailscale unavailable)` only when Tailscale is genuinely unavailable.

### Changes
- `TailscaleIdentity`: `ResolveAdvertisedControlApiEndpoint(port)` (single policy) + pure `FormatAdvertisedControlApiEndpoint(frontDoor, port)`. **Design note:** reused the existing `TryGetFrontDoorUrlForPort` rather than adding the proposed `TryGetControlApiEndpointForPort` - that helper already returns `https://<magicdns>:<port>`/null, so adding another would duplicate the front-door policy (the opposite of the issue's "policy in ONE place" goal). Behavior is identical to the plan.
- `MainWindow.CopySessionNameAndId` - resolves off the UI thread, emits endpoint.
- `HelpDialog` - shows immediately with a `(resolving...)` placeholder, fills the Control endpoint row + Copy text in `Opened` (Responsive-UI).
- 4 new formatter unit tests. None of the "Confirmed CORRECT" loopback uses were touched.

### How to Test
1. Tailnet machine: right-click a session -> "Copy Handover Info"; paste -> `Control API: https://<magicdns>:<port>`.
2. About CC Director -> "Control endpoint" row shows the same.
3. From another tailnet node: `GET <endpoint>/healthz` -> 200.
4. Tailscale stopped: both read `http://127.0.0.1:<port> (local only - Tailscale unavailable)`.

### Expected Result
A tailnet-reachable URL when Tailscale is up; a labelled local-only value otherwise.

### Before / After
- Before: `Control API: http://127.0.0.1:7887` (unreachable off-box)
- After (verified live on SOREN_NORTH): `Control API: https://soren-north.taildb08ed.ts.net:7886` - `healthz` over that front door answered **200**.

Build clean (0 warnings/errors); 24/24 TailscaleIdentity tests pass.

## QA Agent - FAILED (flow:qa-failed)

The #293 **feature code is correct and independently verified** (details below), but the **handoff artifact is defective**: PR #295 is not isolated to issue #293 - it bundles unmerged issue #198 work. This violates the CenCon hard gate (DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md Section 2: "every code change traces back to exactly one GitHub issue") and Developer Step 4 ("work on a feature branch off main").

### Blocking defect: PR #295 carries two issues

The branch was cut from `bugfix/198-terminal-pane-resilience` instead of `main`, so PR #295 -> `main` contains three commits:

```
git log origin/main..HEAD
8dd1d34 fix(#293): advertise tailnet front door in handover copy + Help dialog   <- the only #293 commit
968d5a8 docs(198): attach live-verification screenshots                          <- issue #198
4a791a0 fix(cockpit): terminal pane recovers + shows state (#198)                <- issue #198
```

There is **no open PR for #198**, so PR #295 is currently the only path those #198 commits (`TerminalPane.razor`, `cockpit-terminal.js`, 5 `198-terminal-pane` proof PNGs) would reach `main`. If a human merges PR #295 they would silently pull issue #198 into `main` under a "#293" PR, with no #198 review/QA. QA cannot pass a PR that would contaminate `main` with a second, unreviewed issue.

**Expected:** PR #295 contains only the #293 change (commit `8dd1d34`).
**Actual:** PR #295 contains `8dd1d34` + two #198 commits.

### Fix (re-isolate the branch off main, then re-label flow:ready-qa)

```bash
git fetch origin
git checkout issue-293-advertise-tailnet-control-endpoint
git reset --hard 8dd1d34            # ensure HEAD is the #293 commit
git rebase --onto origin/main 968d5a8   # drop the two #198 commits; OR:
#   git checkout -B issue-293-advertise-tailnet-control-endpoint origin/main && git cherry-pick 8dd1d34
git push --force-with-lease
# confirm: git log origin/main..HEAD  -> only 8dd1d34
```

(If #198 is meant to land first, it needs its own PR merged to `main` before #293 is rebased on top - either way PR #295 must show only the #293 commit when handed back.)

### Independent verification of the #293 behavior (this part PASSES - code is fine)

Built the branch to slot 5, launched via `cc-director-launch`, Control API on port 7886 (Kestrel bound to `127.0.0.1` loopback only). On SOREN_NORTH (Tailscale up, MagicDNS `soren-north.taildb08ed.ts.net`):

| Criterion | Expected | Actual | Result |
|-----------|----------|--------|--------|
| Tailnet up -> front door | `https://<magicdns>:<port>` | `ResolveAdvertisedControlApiEndpoint(7886)` = `https://soren-north.taildb08ed.ts.net:7886` (the exact method both UI surfaces call) | PASS |
| Tailscale down -> labelled loopback | `http://127.0.0.1:<port> (local only - Tailscale unavailable)` | `FormatAdvertisedControlApiEndpoint(null,7886)` = `http://127.0.0.1:7886 (local only - Tailscale unavailable)` | PASS |
| Front door reachable | `GET <endpoint>/healthz` 2xx | `https://soren-north.taildb08ed.ts.net:7886/healthz` -> **200** | PASS |
| Confirmed-CORRECT loopback untouched | only the 2 surfaces change | #293 commit `8dd1d34` touches only HelpDialog/MainWindow/TailscaleIdentity(+tests); `MainWindow:1795` `/sessions/{id}/view` loopback (on the do-not-touch list) left as-is | PASS |

Tests: `TailscaleIdentityTests` 24/24 pass. (UI-surface screenshots were attempted but Avalonia's menu/rail did not drive reliably via UIA among many open windows; the in-process run of the exact shipping method + the front-door healthz 200 prove the behavior the two thin call sites emit.)

**Net:** the code is ready; only the branch needs re-isolating. Fix the branch so PR #295 shows just `8dd1d34`, then re-label `flow:ready-qa`.

## QA Agent - PASS (flow:done) - re-run after branch re-cut

The bounce reason is resolved and all acceptance criteria are independently verified on the re-cut branch.

**Proof:** docs/cencon/proof/issue-293/qa-report.html  (PR #295)

### Blocking defect (first pass) - FIXED
PR #295 now contains a single commit isolated to #293; the two issue #198 commits are gone:
```
git log origin/main..HEAD
8cd7662 fix(#293): advertise tailnet front door in handover copy + Help dialog   <- only commit
```

### Acceptance criteria (independently reproduced, live on SOREN_NORTH)
| Criterion | Expected | Actual | Result |
|-----------|----------|--------|--------|
| Tailnet up -> front door | `https://<magicdns>:<port>` | `ResolveAdvertisedControlApiEndpoint(7886)` = `https://soren-north.taildb08ed.ts.net:7886` | PASS |
| Tailscale down -> labelled loopback | `http://127.0.0.1:<port> (local only - Tailscale unavailable)` | exact match | PASS |
| Front door reachable | `GET <endpoint>/healthz` 2xx | `https://soren-north.taildb08ed.ts.net:7886/healthz` -> **200** | PASS |
| Confirmed-CORRECT loopback untouched | only 2 surfaces change | `MainWindow:1795` `/sessions/{id}/view` loopback left as-is | PASS |

### Regression / method
- Full solution builds clean off the re-cut branch (0 warnings / 0 errors) - dropping #198's changes broke nothing.
- `TailscaleIdentityTests` 24/24 pass.
- No CenCon drift, no new listener (Kestrel still loopback-only); PR isolated to one issue.

**Verdict: VERIFIED.** Standalone QA does not merge - **PR #295 is left open for you to squash-merge** (merge-on-pass applies only inside the implementation-loop).

title: Copy Handover Info (and Help dialog) leak http://127.0.0.1 Control API URL; should advertise tailnet front door when Tailscale is up

body:
Follow-up from #288 (PR #289).

## Finding
#289 added `SessionOwnerCache` so the per-session WS proxy answers **503** ("owning director offline") instead of 404 when live ownership resolution fails but the session is in the cache. That correctly fixes the *offline-Director* case (#268 AC4).

But the same path also fires for a session that simply **EXITED** while its Director is still up and reachable:
- The Director is online, the session just ended.
- Live resolution (`GetSessionAsync`) returns null (session gone), so the WS proxy falls to the cache.
- The cache still holds `sid -> directorId` (the session was seen earlier), so the proxy returns **503 "owning director offline"** — but the Director is NOT offline; the session is gone.

Arguably this should be **404** (or **410 Gone**), not 503. It is noted in the #289 code comments (`SessionOwnerCache` / `SessionWsProxyEndpoints`).

## Impact
Low. The Cockpit does not normally open a per-session WS for a session it knows has exited, and the loud-failure UX (#268 AC5) renders the same regardless (the browser can't read 404 vs 503 vs 410 off a failed upgrade). This is about HTTP-status precision and cache hygiene.

## Options
1. **Prune on exit:** when the aggregator observes a session as `Exited`, drop it from `SessionOwnerCache` (so it reverts to 404). Cheap; the aggregator already iterates sessions.
2. **Distinguish in the proxy:** when the cached owner IS currently reachable but reports no such session, return 404/410 (session gone) rather than 503 (owner offline) — i.e. only 503 when the cached owner itself is unreachable.
3. Accept 503 as "Director cannot serve this session" and document it.

Option 1 is the simplest correctness fix.

comments.body:
## Acceptance Criteria (sharpened for ready-dev)

- [ ] When the fleet aggregator (`GET /sessions`) observes a session as `Exited` on a **reachable** Director — or the session disappears from a Director that is currently reachable — that session is removed from `SessionOwnerCache`.
- [ ] A per-session WS request (`/sessions/{sid}/stream` or `/dictate`) for an **exited** session whose owning Director is **online** returns **404** (not 503).
- [ ] An **offline-owner** session (cached owner, Director unreachable) still returns **503** — #288 behavior must not regress.
- [ ] Unit tests cover both: (a) exited-then-pruned session → 404; (b) offline owner → still 503. Existing `SessionWsProxyEndpointsTests` still pass.
- [ ] Build clean (0/0).

Decided approach: option 1 (prune the owner cache on observed exit).

## Developer Agent - ready for QA

Implemented the decided approach (option 1: prune the owner cache on observed exit).

**Release Notes:** The per-session WS proxy now returns 404 for a session that has exited while its owning Director is still online (was 503 after #289). Offline-owner sessions still return 503 - #288 preserved.

**Changes:**
- `SessionOwnerCache.Forget(sessionId)` + `RetainForDirector(directorId, liveSessionIds)` - reconcile drops cached entries for a reachable Director whose session is no longer live; entries owned by a different (offline) Director are never touched.
- `GET /sessions` aggregator reconciles the owner cache against each reachable Director's live (non-Exited) session ids.

**How to Test:** `dotnet test src/CcDirector.Gateway.Tests`
- `SessionsAggregationTests.Aggregator_prunes_owner_cache_for_session_no_longer_live_on_reachable_director_then_ws_proxy_is_404`
- `SessionsAggregationTests.Aggregator_prune_does_not_touch_a_session_owned_by_a_different_offline_director`
- `SessionOwnerCacheTests.*` (6) and existing `SessionWsProxyEndpointsTests.*` (all pass)

**Expected Result:** exited + online owner -> 404; offline owner -> 503 (unchanged). Build clean (0/0). 28/28 targeted tests pass.

**Before:** exited session whose Director is online -> 503 "owning director offline" (stale cache).
**After:** same case -> 404. Offline owner -> still 503.

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-291/report.html  (PR #312)

@
## QA Agent - VERIFIED (flow:done)

Independent verification of PR #312 against the sharpened acceptance criteria. This is a Gateway-side resolution-logic change (no UI surface), so QA proof is a self-hosted `GatewayHost` driven over real HTTP, not a screenshot.

| # | Criterion | Result |
|---|-----------|--------|
| 1 | Aggregator prunes `SessionOwnerCache` for a session no longer live on a **reachable** Director | PASS - `OwnerOf("gone")` is null after one `GET /sessions` |
| 2 | WS request for **exited** session, **online** owner -> **404** (not 503) | PASS - real `/sessions/gone/stream` returns 404 |
| 3 | **Offline-owner** session -> still **503** (#288 no regression) | PASS - real `/sessions/offline-owned/stream` returns 503; entry preserved |
| 4 | Unit tests cover both; existing `SessionWsProxyEndpointsTests` pass | PASS - 6 SessionOwnerCacheTests + 2 #291 aggregation tests; SessionWsProxyEndpointsTests 7/7; full Gateway project 415/415 |
| 5 | Build clean (0/0) | PASS - full solution: 0 Warning(s), 0 Error(s) |

**How I verified (independent):**
- Full `cc-director.sln` build -> Build succeeded, 0/0.
- `dotnet test` SessionsAggregationTests + SessionOwnerCacheTests + SessionWsProxyEndpointsTests -> 28/28 pass; both #291 integration tests (which drive the actual WS-proxy endpoints over HTTP and read the status code) green.
- Full Gateway test project -> 415/415 pass (regression sweep).
- Code-read the shared `SessionOwners` instance wiring in `GatewayHost` (aggregator `RetainForDirector` and WS proxy `OwnerOf` use the same cache), and confirmed `liveIds` excludes `Exited` rows so an Exited row cannot keep an entry alive.

Proof report: `docs/cencon/proof/issue-291/qa-report.html`

All 5 acceptance criteria met. Squash-merging to main inside the implementation-loop.
@

title: [Gateway] Per-session WS proxy: an EXITED session returns 503 (cached owner) where 404/410 may be more correct

body:
## Finding
Surfaced while deploying the #288 fix to a remote machine. `scripts/redeploy-gateway.ps1` publishes the Gateway through a **shared working tree / stage directory**. When two redeploys run against the same checkout around the same window (or the working tree is on a different branch than intended), one can clobber the other and the Gateway comes up running an **older build than `main`** — while `GET /cockpit` still returns **HTTP 200**.

Concretely, during the #288 live verification:
- A redeploy reported success and `/cockpit` → 200, but the running Gateway was actually the **pre-#289 build** (it returned 404 where #289 returns 503).
- This was only caught by inspecting a build-specific **log marker** (`-> 404 (… none cached)` vs the old `-> 404 (no owning director across the fleet)`), not by the 200.

## Why it matters
HTTP 200 proves *a* Gateway is up, **not which build**. Any verification (including automated QA) that trusts 200 after a redeploy can validate against stale code. It nearly invalidated a "no regression" check in the #288 work.

## Options
1. **Isolate per deploy:** publish from a fresh `git worktree` pinned to the target commit (the #288 verification ultimately did exactly this to get a trustworthy build), so concurrent deploys can't share state.
2. **Post-deploy build-marker assertion:** stamp the published build with the commit SHA and have the script assert the *running* Gateway reports that SHA (e.g. via a `/version` field) before declaring success — fail loudly otherwise.
3. Serialize redeploys with a lockfile so two can't interleave on the shared tree.

(1)+(2) together would make redeploy both race-safe and self-verifying.

## Severity
Medium — silent stale deploys undermine any post-deploy verification; the fix is mechanical.

comments.body:
## Acceptance Criteria (sharpened for ready-dev)

- [ ] `scripts/redeploy-gateway.ps1` publishes the Gateway from a source **isolated from the shared working tree** (e.g. a temporary `git worktree` pinned to the resolved HEAD commit, or a clean export), so a concurrent branch switch or edit in the main checkout cannot change what gets published.
- [ ] After swap + relaunch, the script reads the **running** Gateway's reported build identity (version + commit SHA) and asserts it matches the commit it just published; on mismatch it exits non-zero with a clear message naming expected vs actual. (If the Gateway exposes no SHA-bearing field, add a minimal one — `AppVersion.Full` already carries the SHA per its comment — or stamp+read a build marker.)
- [ ] Two redeploys cannot corrupt each other's staged output (per-run stage dir or a lock).
- [ ] Existing behavior preserved: graceful `POST /shutdown`, file swap, relaunch `--managed`, and the `GET /cockpit` smoke check still run and still gate success.
- [ ] Proof: a run log showing publish-from-isolated-source + the post-deploy SHA assertion passing, plus a demonstration (or test) that a stale/mismatched build is caught and fails loudly.

Decided approach: option 1 (isolated worktree) + option 2 (post-deploy SHA assertion) together.

## Developer Agent - ready for QA

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-290/report.html  (PR #311)

### Release Notes
`scripts/redeploy-gateway.ps1` can no longer silently ship a stale Gateway build. It publishes from an isolated git worktree pinned to the resolved HEAD commit and, after relaunch, asserts the running Gateway reports exactly that commit.

### Changes
- Isolated-source publish: `git worktree --detach` at HEAD's commit -> publish into a per-run GUID stage dir -> remove the worktree. A concurrent branch switch or second redeploy cannot change what this run ships.
- Post-deploy SHA assertion: after the existing shutdown/swap/relaunch `--managed`/`GET /cockpit` gate, read `GET /healthz` `version` (`AppVersion.Full`, carries `+<sha>`) and assert its SHA matches the published commit; throw `STALE BUILD` + exit non-zero on mismatch. No new endpoint (the SHA was already on `/healthz`).
- Fixed a latent `NETSDK1150` the clean-worktree publish surfaced (`-p:SelfContained=false`).

### How to Test
```
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\test\test-redeploy-gateway-sha.ps1
```
(A) SHA units, (B) real isolated-worktree publish, (C) the real `Assert-RunningGatewaySha` vs a self-hosted Gateway on a free loopback port. Never touches the live Gateway (7878) or production.

### Expected Result
Exit 0, `RESULT: PASS`. Section C proves the match path passes AND `Assert-RunningGatewaySha FAILS LOUD on a mismatched (stale) commit`. Full solution build: 0 warnings, 0 errors.

### Before / After
- Before: shared tree + shared stage dir -> a stale build could ship while `/cockpit` returned 200.
- After: per-commit isolated worktree + per-run stage dir; success is declared only after the running Gateway is proven to BE the published commit.

CenCon impact: none (deploy script + test; reuses existing `/healthz`; no architecture/security drift).

## QA Agent - PASS (flow:done)

Independently verified in a fresh context (never saw the Developer's work). This is a deploy-script + build-identity change; no production Gateway is installed here, so verification was done by inspecting the script, running the real isolated-worktree publish, and standing up a self-hosted GatewayHost on a free loopback port for the SHA assertion's match + fail-loud paths.

**5/5 acceptance criteria PASS:**
1. Isolated publish - `git worktree --detach` pinned to HEAD `ce29ba0`, per-run GUID temp dir, removed afterward (worktree count before=1 after=1, no leak). Commit pinned before publish.
2. Post-deploy SHA assertion - running Gateway reported `version = 0.6.22+ce29ba042cc3a7a7e2af2a6054c764113cd98ea8`; match path returned ok, **mismatch threw `STALE BUILD: published commit feedface... but the RUNNING Gateway reports ce29ba0 ... refusing to declare success`** and exits non-zero. Reproduced in both the dev test and my own independent script.
3. Per-run GUID stage dir (`local_builds\_gw-deploy-<GUID>`) + per-run worktree temp dir; finally removes only this run's dir. No shared mutable path.
4. Existing gates preserved (POST /shutdown, swap, relaunch --managed, GET /cockpit) - all run before the additive SHA gate.
5. Proof: dev test `RESULT: PASS` (A/B/C) + my independent self-hosted-Gateway run reproducing match and STALE BUILD.

Full solution build: **0 Warnings, 0 Errors.** No CenCon drift (reuses existing `/healthz`).

QA proof report: `docs/cencon/proof/issue-290/qa-report.html`

Squash-merging to main (implementation-loop).

title: [Gateway] redeploy-gateway.ps1 shared build/stage tree can silently ship a stale build

body:
Follow-up from #268 two-box QA.

## Finding
Issue #268's AC4 specifies: *unknown session → 404; **a session whose owning Director is offline → 503** within a few seconds (no hang).* In practice the per-session WS proxy returns **404** for an offline Director, never 503.

## Why
`SessionWsProxyEndpoints.ProxyAsync` resolves the owning Director by calling `GetSessionAsync` on each registered Director (`LocateOwningDirectorAsync`) **before** forwarding. Resolution therefore requires the owning Director to be reachable. The 503 branch only fires when a Director *resolves* but its `ForwardDestination` (TailnetEndpoint-preferred) is a **different, unreachable** endpoint.

In the live fleet every Director's resolve endpoint == forward endpoint:
- `37cea6e2`: ControlEndpoint `http://127.0.0.1:7887`, TailnetEndpoint `None`
- `a0d9f4a3`: ControlEndpoint == TailnetEndpoint == `https://sorenlaptop…:7880`

So an offline Director fails at resolution → 404. The 503 path is effectively unreachable in normal operation.

## Also
`SessionWsProxyEndpointsTests` does **not** cover the 503 response (its comment defers it to "the cross-machine proof run"); the cross-machine proof run found it unreachable as above.

## Options
1. **Align the spec** to reality: offline owning Director → 404, and adjust the Cockpit loud-failure copy if it should distinguish unknown-session from unreachable-Director.
2. **Resolve from the registry's session→director map** (which the aggregator already maintains) instead of live `GetSessionAsync`, then 503 when the forward to the resolved Director fails. This makes the 503 reachable and matches the AC.
3. Add a unit test that actually asserts the 503 (resolved-owner + dead ForwardDestination), whichever path is chosen.

Low severity — the loud-failure UX (#268 AC5) still fires correctly because the browser can't read 404 vs 503 off a failed WS upgrade; this is about the proxy's HTTP status precision and the AC wording.

comments.body:
## AC4-503 verified live (cross-machine)

Confirmed working on **SOREN_NORTH** after redeploying the Gateway from `main` `91bcd07` (PR #289), with the WS request originating from **SORENLAPTOP** (`100.89.217.71`).

**Setup:** stood up a throwaway slot-5 test Director (`ce7e7143`), created session `e74b86e3-d34e-4c8b-ab09-b3e28bf217d7`, primed the Gateway's `SessionOwnerCache` via `GET /sessions`, then killed **only** that test Director.

**Result:**

| Request (from SORENLAPTOP) | Status |
|---|---|
| `GET /sessions/e74b86e3-…/stream` (known session, owner offline) | **503** |
| `GET /sessions/00000000-…-000000000000/stream` (never observed) | **404** |

**Gateway log (`[SessionWsProxy]`):**

```
2026-06-10 18:15:11.796 [SessionWsProxy] open leg=stream sid=e74b86e3-d34e-4c8b-ab09-b3e28bf217d7 client=100.89.217.71
2026-06-10 18:15:11.800 [SessionWsProxy] leg=stream sid=e74b86e3-d34e-4c8b-ab09-b3e28bf217d7 -> 503 (known owner ce7e7143-30c8-4b78-beff-a1637936528f offline/unreachable)
2026-06-10 18:15:11.991 [SessionWsProxy] leg=stream sid=00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 -> 404 (no owning director across the fleet, none cached)
```

So an offline owning Director now returns **503** for a known session and **404** only for a never-observed one — exactly the #268 AC4 behavior. Unit coverage `SessionWsProxyEndpointsTests.Known_session_with_offline_owner_returns_503_not_404` + `Unknown_session_returns_404_not_the_cockpit_interstitial` is green.

Two-box QA report updated to reflect the PASS: `docs/cencon/proof/issue-268/two-box-qa-report.html` (commit `50c9a95` on `main`).

title: [Gateway] Per-session WS proxy returns 404 (not the spec'd 503) for an offline owning Director

body:
## EPIC - Cockpit optimistic-UI sweep

> **Umbrella epic** (like #270, #279). Not itself a `flow:ready-dev` item; it groups and tracks the
> children below. Each child carries its own `flow:*` label and is independently shippable.

## Problem / Value

CLAUDE.md rule 1 is mandatory: **every user action must give visible feedback in <100ms.** The
optimistic-UI audit that followed the #217 explain-button fix (commit 137b289) found a cluster of
Cockpit actions that violate this - the only feedback waits on a Director network round-trip or the
2-second roster poll, so a working action reads as a broken one and invites double-clicks. This epic
is the batch fix for that cluster.

## The canonical pattern (all children copy it)

Commit **137b289** (#217) is the in-repo reference: stamp optimistic local state in the **same render
as the click, before the POST**; a stale-guard timer reconciles against the polled truth; a failed
POST rolls back loudly. Blazor renders at the first `await`, so setting the flag/label before the
network call and calling `StateHasChanged` gives instant feedback for free.

## Children (independent - all parallelizable)

| Issue | Action with no feedback | Kind |
|-------|------------------------|------|
| **#227** | Answer-option buttons on the reply path give zero feedback | bug |
| **#228** | Rail kebab actions (Close/Hold/Wingman/New-Issue) show no in-progress state | enhancement |
| **#229** | "+ New session" Create button stays "Create" during the multi-second spawn | enhancement |
| **#230** | Composer image upload shows nothing until the upload finishes | enhancement |

These have no ordering between them; each is sharpened to `flow:ready-dev` and can be picked up in
any order or in parallel.

## Scope of the epic

**IN** - give immediate, honest, in-progress feedback for the four actions above, using the 137b289
pattern. **OUT** - any visual *redesign* of the Cockpit (layout/nav/theming); the remote-first WS
reliability work (epic #279); the rail GUID-vs-port label (#237, separate). This epic does not change
*what* the actions do, only the feedback they give while doing it.

## Definition of Done for the epic

All four children reach `flow:done`: each of the four actions shows feedback within one render of the
click (Expected vs Actual proven per child), with no regression to the success path.

comments.body:

DoD was "all four children reach flow:done". All four are closed and squash-merged:

| Child | Action | Closed |
|-------|--------|--------|
| #227 | Answer-option buttons feedback (reply path) | 2026-06-10 |
| #228 | Rail kebab actions in-progress state | 2026-06-11 |
| #229 | New session Create button spawn feedback | 2026-06-11 |
| #230 | Composer image upload progress | 2026-06-11 |

Each child shipped with its own QA proof under docs/cencon/proof/. No open issue references this epic. Nothing to extract.

title: [Cockpit] EPIC: Optimistic-UI sweep - every user action gives feedback before the round-trip

body:
## EPIC - Remote-first reliability for the Cockpit

> This is an **umbrella epic** (like #270). It is not itself a `flow:ready-dev` work item; it
> tracks and orders the child issues below. Each child carries its own `flow:*` label.

## Problem / Value

The Cockpit is the Gateway's web dashboard, but it is **not usable from a machine that is not the
Gateway host**. Opening it from this laptop (which is *not* the Gateway) fails on the most important
interactions - voice dictation and the live terminal - and the failures are opaque. The reason is a
single architectural gap, not a pile of unrelated bugs:

**Per-session WebSocket legs go browser -> Director directly, using the Director's own advertised
address.** The Gateway already reverse-proxies HTTP `/sessions/{sid}/*` calls and the Blazor circuit
WS to the owning Director (`src/CcDirector.Gateway/Cockpit/CockpitProxy.cs`,
`src/CcDirector.Gateway/Api/GatewayEndpoints.cs`), but the two **raw per-session WebSocket legs are
not proxied**:

- dictation: `cockpit-dictate.js` connects to `DictateWsUrl(DirBase)` where
  `DirBase => Selected.TailnetEndpoint` (`Cockpit.razor:1434`, `:1258`);
- terminal: `TerminalPane.BuildWsUrl(DirectorBase, sid)` over the same base
  (`TerminalPane.razor:63-65`).

When the owning Director advertised a **loopback** endpoint (`http://127.0.0.1:7887`), a remote
browser resolves that to *its own* loopback and the socket never connects (#268). When the Director
is tailnet-reachable, the long-lived WS still drops mid-stream and the dialog only says
"Connection closed." with no diagnosis (#226). The same leg leaves the Terminal tab blank (#198).
Because the Cockpit's own logs are discarded (#199), none of it is diagnosable from the remote side.

The fix is to make the **browser only ever talk same-origin to the Gateway**, and have the Gateway
