proxy the per-session WS legs to the owning Director - exactly as it already does for HTTP. That one
backbone change makes voice, terminal, and every future per-session WS feature work from any machine
on the tailnet, and removes the Cockpit's dependence on each Director advertising a correct address.

## Scope of the epic

**IN**
- Make the Cockpit fully usable from a non-Gateway machine for the two per-session WS features that
  are broken today: **voice dictation** and the **live terminal stream**.
- The backbone: route those WS legs through the Gateway (child: #268, re-sharpened below).
- Honest failure + diagnosability so remote breakage is explainable (#226 error UX, #199 logging).
- Verify the dependent symptoms are resolved by the backbone (#198 terminal-blank, #170 Director
  shows yellow).

**OUT (explicitly deferred)**
- Any **visual / UX redesign** of the Cockpit (layout, navigation, theming). This epic is reliability
  only. The optimistic-UI sweep (#227-#230) and rail-clarity (#237) are tracked separately and are
  not part of this epic.
- Same-machine worktree parallelism, the work-list queue runner (#270), and any non-WS feature.

## Child issues and dependency order

| Order | Issue | Role in this epic | State |
|-------|-------|-------------------|-------|
| 1 | **#268** (backbone) | Route per-session WS legs (dictate + terminal stream) through the Gateway | re-sharpened to `flow:ready-dev` |
| 1 | **#199** | Persisted Cockpit logging (diagnosability enabler; independent of the backbone) | already `flow:ready-dev` |
| 1 | **#267** | Open-in-Cockpit toolbar button honors the configured gateway URL | already `flow:ready-dev` |
| 2 | **#226** | Dictation error UX: read WS close code/reason, typed Director error, retry-with-audio | depends on #268; see issue |
| 3 | **#198** | Terminal tab blank cross-machine - verify resolved by #268, fix any residual | depends on #268 |
| 3 | **#170** | Director shows yellow / "not connected" in the Cockpit - re-test after #268 | depends on #268 |

Order-1 items can be picked up now. Order-2/3 items are gated on the backbone (#268) landing first,
because the backbone changes the URL the browser connects to; verifying them before it lands would
test the old path. The Product Agent labels each `flow:ready-dev` when its predecessor reaches
`flow:done`.

## Definition of Done for the epic (human-verifiable)

The epic is done when, **from this laptop (a non-Gateway machine), over the tailnet**:
1. Opening the Cockpit, selecting a session on a *remote* Director, and clicking **Speak** records
   and transcribes successfully (no "WebSocket connection failed." / "Connection closed.").
2. The **Terminal** tab for that same remote session shows live output within a few seconds.
3. When something does fail, the dialog/tab names a real cause, not a generic message.
4. No browser WebSocket is ever pointed at a `127.0.0.1`/`localhost` address for a remote session.

## Assumptions

- The approved approach is **route-through-Gateway** ("Option B" from #268), chosen by the product
  owner on 2026-06-10 over "make every Director advertise a tailnet endpoint." Rationale: it removes
  the per-Director-address dependency entirely instead of making it correct in one more place.
- This epic is **reliability only**; the Cockpit visual redesign is a separate future epic.

comments.body:
## Epic audit 2026-06-11: implementation scope shipped - closing with two extractions

All implementation children are flow:done and squash-merged:

| Order | Child | Closed |
|-------|-------|--------|
| 1 | #268 per-session WS legs (dictate + terminal) routed through the Gateway | 2026-06-10 |
| 1 | #199 persisted Cockpit logging | 2026-06-10 |
| 1 | #267 Open-in-Cockpit uses configured gateway URL | 2026-06-10 |
| 2 | #226 dictation failure UX (real close cause + retry with audio) | 2026-06-11 |
| 3 | #198 terminal tab blank cross-machine | 2026-06-10 |
| 3 | #170 Director-yellow root cause | closed today: research was delivered 2026-06-05; the cause (Gateway-side Serve provisioning cannot reach remote nodes) was fixed by #197 + #223/#224; verified live today that both Directors are two-way verified green |

Extracted before closing:
- #323 - the epic-level acceptance run. Children passed QA individually, but the epic DoD (Speak + live Terminal from a non-Gateway machine, honest failures, no loopback WS) was never executed as one pass; the remote-DIRECTOR round-trip and dictation Insert/Send last inch remain unexercised. Verification-only.
- #322 - found live during this audit: Gateway.Tests runs can write real instance files, painting phantom amber "unreachable" Directors in the production Cockpit. Distinct cause from #170; test-pollution bug.

With the backbone shipped and the residuals tracked as their own items, this umbrella is done.

title: [Cockpit] EPIC: Remote-first reliability - the Cockpit works fully from any non-gateway machine

body:
## Parent epic

Child 5 of 5 for #270 (autonomous work-item queue runner). **BLOCKED BY child 2 (named lists REST).** The Product Agent will label this `flow:ready-dev` once child 2 reaches `flow:done`. Until then no `flow:*` label.

## Problem / Value

#270's producer side: when the Product Agent marks an item ready for dev, it should be able to **append that item to a chosen named work list over REST**, so the item enters an ordered queue the Gateway runner can drain - instead of relying on `flow:ready-dev` creation order alone. This child adds that single producer call to the product skill. It is small and deliberately last: it is only useful once the list object (child 2) exists, and only fully useful once the runner (child 3) drains lists - but the append itself is testable against child 2 alone.

## Scope

**IN**
- Extend the `product-agent` skill (`.claude/skills/product-agent`) so that, when it labels an issue `flow:ready-dev`, it can ALSO append that issue to a named work list via child 2's `POST /lists/{name}/items`, sending the structured ref `{ "source": "github", "id": "<issue#>", "area": "<optional>" }`.
- Since this skill files GitHub issues, it always emits `source = "github"`; `id` is the issue number as a string. `area` is optional (the skill may set it from the issue's area/label, or omit it).
- The list name is chosen explicitly (skill argument or a documented default); the skill never invents a list.
- The list assignment is additive to the existing flow: the `flow:ready-dev` label remains the single source of truth for status; the list append only sets ORDER/queue membership.

**OUT**
- The list object/REST (child 2), the runner (child 3), the Cockpit (child 4).
- Any change to the Definition of Ready or the label state machine - this only adds an optional list-append step.
- Making list-append mandatory: an item with no list still works exactly as today (`flow:ready-dev` only). The append is opt-in.

## Acceptance criteria

1. The `product-agent` skill, after labeling an issue `flow:ready-dev`, can append that issue to a named list via `POST /lists/{name}/items` as `{ "source": "github", "id": "<issue#>", "area"? }`, and `GET /lists/{name}` then shows the issue in order with `source = github`. (Proof: run the skill path for a test issue, show the list contains the structured ref.)
2. The skill never creates or guesses a list name - if no list is specified and no documented default applies, it does not append (and says so), rather than inventing one. (Proof: skill run with no list specified leaves the item label-only.)
3. Appending to a list does not change or duplicate the item's status: the `flow:ready-dev` label is unchanged and the list stores only the ref/order (rides child 2 criterion 7). (Proof: issue still shows `flow:ready-dev`; list payload has no status field.)
4. An item that is label-only (never appended) still flows through the existing method unchanged (the append is opt-in, not required). (Proof: a `flow:ready-dev` item with no list entry is still pickable by the loop's no-arg selection.)

## Affected containers

- `.claude/skills/product-agent` (the new REST append step; documentation of the list-name argument/default).
- (The same producer call is intended to be reused by the product skill in OTHER repos per #270, but THIS issue ships only the cc-director `product-agent` skill change; cross-repo rollout is out of scope here.)

## Proof target

- A captured run of the product-agent path for a test issue: label set to `flow:ready-dev`, then `POST /lists/{name}/items`, then `GET /lists/{name}` showing the issue in order.
- The no-list-specified run leaving the item label-only (criterion 2).
- The issue still showing `flow:ready-dev` and the list payload carrying no status (criterion 3).
- HTML proof report: Expected vs Actual per criterion.

## Note to the Developer Agent

This is a skill change (no .NET build artifact) plus a REST call - prove it by exercising the skill path against a running Gateway with child 2's endpoints, not by `dotnet build`. Do not reject for "no compiled change."

## Assumptions

- The list-name source (explicit skill argument vs a documented default list) is an implementation choice for the Developer Agent to settle and document; the skill must NOT invent a list silently (criterion 2 enforces this). Flagged.
- Uses child 2's final `POST /lists/{name}/items` structured-ref contract (`{ source, id, area? }`, Option B).



---

## Blocker cleared 2026-06-10 - child 2 (#273) shipped to main

#273 reached `flow:done` and merged. The append endpoint this child calls now EXISTS on `main`. The final, shipped contract (build against this, no re-derivation needed):

- `POST /lists/{name}/items` - body `{ "source": "...", "id": "...", "area"?: "..." }` -> `{ "name", "appended": <ref> }`. Returns 400 if `source` or `id` is empty/missing; 404 if the named list does not exist (the skill must target a list that exists - it does not create one, criterion 2).
- `GET /lists/{name}` -> `WorkListDto` = `{ "name": string, "items": [{ "source", "id", "area"? }, ...], "consumer": string|null }` - used to prove the appended ref landed in order.

This skill always sends `{ "source": "github", "id": "<issue#>", "area"?: "<area>" }`. The list payload carries no status field, so criterion 3 (append does not duplicate status) is structurally guaranteed by the shipped contract. Endpoint: `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Api/WorkListEndpoints.cs`; contracts: `src/CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts/WorkListItemRef.cs`. Per the existing Note to the Developer Agent, prove this by exercising the skill path against a running Gateway - not by `dotnet build`.

comments.body: Blocked by #273 (named lists REST). No `flow:*` label until #273 reaches `flow:done`; the Product Agent will then label this `flow:ready-dev`. Parent epic: #270.

Product Agent: blocker cleared. Child 2 (#273, named work lists REST) reached flow:done and merged to main, so the only thing gating this child is now resolved. Verified the shipped POST /lists/{name}/items append endpoint on main accepts the structured ref { source, id, area? } exactly as this issue specifies, and that the list payload carries no status field - so criterion 3 (append does not duplicate status) is structurally guaranteed. Ran the Definition of Ready (Section 5) check: 7/7 PASS. Body sharpened with the final shipped contract. Per the existing Note to the Developer Agent, this is a skill + REST change proven against a running Gateway, not dotnet build. Labeled flow:ready-dev.

## Developer Agent - Ready for QA

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-276/report.html  (PR #285)

### Release Notes
The product-agent skill can now optionally append a `flow:ready-dev` issue to an explicitly-named work list over REST, so the item enters the ordered queue the #274 runner drains. Opt-in and additive - the label stays the single source of status truth.

### Changes
- `.claude/skills/product-agent/skill.md`: new opt-in Step 4 (append via shipped #273 `POST /lists/{name}/items` with `{ source: "github", id, area? }`), report moved to Step 5, documented "no default list / never auto-create". Bumped to v0.2.
- `docs/cencon/proof/issue-276/`: HTML report + raw REST transcript.

### How to Test
Run a Gateway from main (has `/lists`), create a named list, then exercise the skill's REST path: `POST /lists/{name}/items {"source":"github","id":"276","area":"CenCon"}` then `GET /lists/{name}`.

### Expected Result (all proven Expected == Actual on a live Gateway 0.6.22+05e11c2)
1. Append lands; `GET` shows refs in append order, `source=github`. PASS
2. Append to a non-existent list -> 404, list NOT auto-created; no list specified -> label-only (skill has no default list). PASS
3. List/item payload carries only `source/id/area` - no status field; label untouched. PASS
4. Label-only item never appears in a list and stays status-selectable (append is opt-in). PASS

### Before / After
- Before: Product Agent only set `flow:ready-dev`; queue order relied on label creation order.
- After: Product Agent can additionally append the item to a chosen ordered queue without changing how status works.

Skill + REST change (no .NET artifact) per the issue's Note to the Developer Agent; `dotnet build cc-director.sln` is clean. CenCon: no drift.

## QA Agent - VERIFIED (flow:done)

Independent verification - QA did NOT reuse the Developer Agent's binary, port (7950), or transcript. QA built the shipped #273 named-work-list surface (`WorkListEndpoints` + `WorkListStore`) directly from the PR-branch source and booted ONLY that surface on a fresh loopback port (`http://127.0.0.1:7951`, no auth, fresh in-memory store) - the exact code production maps via `GatewayHost.StartAsync() -> WorkListEndpoints.Map`. QA then drove the documented product-agent skill append path with its own real curl HTTP calls.

### Acceptance criteria - 4/4 PASS

1. **PASS** - After `flow:ready-dev`, `POST /lists/{name}/items {source:"github", id, area?}` -> 200 `{name, appended}`; `GET /lists/{name}` shows refs in append order with `source=github` (276 then 311).
2. **PASS** - Append to a non-existent list -> 404 `no such list`; `GET /lists` stays `{"lists":[]}` (NOT auto-created). Skill text forbids `POST /lists` auto-create and documents no default list.
3. **PASS** - List payload keys = `[consumer, items, name]`; each item = `[area, id, source]`; no `status` field anywhere. `area` is optional (omitted -> null). Append touches no GitHub label.
4. **PASS** - A label-only item (id 999, never appended) is absent from the list; nothing forces an item in. Append is opt-in/additive.

### Contract conformance
The skill references ONLY the shipped endpoints (`POST /lists/{name}/items`, `GET /lists/{name}`) - no invented endpoint. The documented contract (200 `{name, appended}`; 400 on empty source/id; 404 on missing list with no create; GET -> `WorkListDto {name, items[], consumer}` with no status field) matches `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Api/WorkListEndpoints.cs` exactly. `dotnet build` of the Gateway project is clean (0 errors, 0 warnings). No CenCon drift (skill-only change reusing an existing surface).

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-276/qa-report.html (committed to PR #285).

VERIFIED - all acceptance criteria met.

title: [CenCon] product-agent skill appends a ready-for-dev item to a named work list over REST

body:
## Parent epic

Child 4 of 5 for #270 (autonomous work-item queue runner). **BLOCKED BY child 2 (named lists REST).** The Product Agent will label this `flow:ready-dev` once child 2 reaches `flow:done`. Until then no `flow:*` label so the loop does not pick it up. (Does NOT require child 1 or child 3 - it is a pure client of child 2's list object; live "running" status appears once child 3 exists, but the create/reorder/view surface is testable against child 2 alone.)

## Problem / Value

#270 insists the Cockpit is **a client of the list API, not the owner** - the list is identical whether reached from the Cockpit or the product skill. This child adds the Cockpit surface to create a named list, reorder its items, and watch per-item status. It must read and write the SAME Gateway list object (child 2); it must not keep its own copy or its own ordering. This is the human's window into the queues.

## Canonical UI target

Build to this mockup (committed in the repo) - it is the approved layout for this page, not a sketch:

- **Mockup:** [docs/plans/queue-runner-270/lists-ui-mockup.html](docs/plans/queue-runner-270/lists-ui-mockup.html)
- **Overview + UI-to-issue map:** [docs/plans/queue-runner-270/how-it-works.html](docs/plans/queue-runner-270/how-it-works.html)

The page is a left **list-card column** (named priority lists: name + RUNNING/IDLE tag + "N items / claimed by machine-X") and a right **items table**. Each item row shows: drag handle (reorder), priority number, **source badge** (`GH` / `DO` / `JIRA`), **id** (`#262`, `1203`, `CCD-44`), title, an **area chip** (Gateway / Core / Installer / Cockpit), and a **status badge**. A list mixes sources and areas - it is not sliced by either. A header progress strip shows "N done / N running / N queued".

> **Decision 2026-06-10 (Option B):** child 2's item ref is the structured `{ source, id, area? }` object, so the source badge and area chip are **real, rendered from the ref** - not hard-coded. In v1 only `source = github` items are runnable (child 3); `devops`/`jira` rows render with their badge and stay **queued** (the UI must not imply they will run yet).

## Scope

**IN**
- A Cockpit control to create a named list and append/remove/reorder its item references, calling child 2's REST surface directly.
- The two-column layout above: list-card column + items table, matching the canonical mockup and `docs/VisualStyle.md`.
- Each row renders the structured ref: **source badge** (`github`/`devops`/`jira`), **id**, **area chip**, drag-handle reorder, and a status badge.
- A live per-item status view showing each item as queued / running / done / needs-human / failed.
- Item status is READ from the GitHub `flow:*` labels (single source of truth), NOT from the list object and NOT cached separately in the Cockpit. (Non-github items have no `flow:*` label and render as **queued**.)

**OUT**
- The list object/REST (child 2 - consumed, not built).
- The queue runner (child 3) - until it exists, items show queued/done/needs-human/failed from labels but never "running"; that is acceptable and the view must not break when no runner is active.
- Any orchestration, session start, or merge logic in the Cockpit (it is a client only).

## Status mapping (ASSUMPTION D1 - flagged)

The Cockpit derives the per-item badge from the item's GitHub `flow:*` label:
- no flow label / `flow:ready-dev` -> **queued**
- session active for that item (from child 3's runner state, when present) -> **running**
- `flow:done` -> **done**
- `flow:needs-human` -> **needs-human**
- `flow:qa-failed` mid-loop is transient and shown as **running** (the loop is still on it), not failed.
- a runner-recorded `failed` signal (child 3) -> **failed**

Flagged: the exact "running" source depends on child 3's recorded state; before child 3 exists the Cockpit shows queued/done/needs-human only. Overridable by the human.

## Acceptance criteria

1. From the Cockpit a user can create a named list; it then appears via `GET /lists` (proving the Cockpit wrote to the shared Gateway object, not a local copy). (Proof: create in UI, then show the same list returned by the Gateway REST.)
2. From the Cockpit a user can append a structured item ref (`{ source, id, area? }`) to a list and reorder items; the new order is reflected in `GET /lists/{name}` exactly (the Cockpit issued the child-2 PATCH, did not reorder locally only).
3. A list created via the product skill / raw REST appears in the Cockpit identically (same name, same order, same per-item source/id/area) - proving the Cockpit is a client of the one shared object. (Proof: create via REST, open Cockpit, show it.)
4. Each row renders the ref's **source badge**, **id**, and **area chip** from the list object, and a **status badge** derived from the item's GitHub `flow:*` label per the mapping above; changing an item's label and refreshing updates the badge. A mixed-source list (github + devops + jira) renders all three with the correct badges. (Proof: screenshots of a mixed list + before/after a label change.)
5. The Cockpit does not store or display a status that conflicts with the item's `flow:*` label (no separate cached status) - verified by inspection + by criterion 4 always following the label.
6. The list view renders correctly with no active runner (no item in "running") and does not error when child 3 is absent. (Proof: screenshot with a list whose items are queued/done only.)
7. The page visually matches the canonical mockup (`docs/plans/queue-runner-270/lists-ui-mockup.html`) and complies with `docs/VisualStyle.md`. (Proof: side-by-side screenshot of the built page vs the mockup.)

## Affected containers

- `src/CcDirector.Cockpit/wwwroot` (the list create/reorder control and the per-item status view, as a client of child 2).
- `src/CcDirector.Cockpit` (any Razor/page wiring for the new view).

Must comply with `docs/VisualStyle.md` (UI change).

## Proof target

- Screenshots of: creating a list in the Cockpit; the same list returned by `GET /lists` (proving shared object); reorder reflected in REST; a mixed-source list rendering github/devops/jira badges + area chips; per-item status badges following a `flow:*` label change (before/after); the view with no active runner; the built page side-by-side with the mockup.
- Inspection note confirming no separate status cache in the Cockpit (criterion 5).
- HTML proof report: Expected vs Actual per criterion, screenshots inline.

## Assumptions

- D1 status mapping (flow:* label -> badge) above - flagged, overridable.
- The Cockpit calls child 2's REST surface as defined there; if child 2's paths changed during its build, this child uses the final paths.



---

## Blocker cleared 2026-06-10 - child 2 (#273) shipped to main

#273 reached `flow:done` and merged. The REST surface this child consumes now EXISTS on `main`; the "if child 2's paths changed during its build" conditional is resolved. The Developer Agent builds against this final, shipped contract (no need to re-derive it):

- `POST /lists` - body `{ "name": "..." }` -> `{ "name": "..." }` (409 if the name exists).
- `GET /lists` -> `{ "lists": [ WorkListDto, ... ] }`.
- `GET /lists/{name}` -> a `WorkListDto` (404 if absent).
- `POST /lists/{name}/items` - body `{ "source": "...", "id": "...", "area"?: "..." }` -> `{ "name", "appended": <ref> }` (400 if source/id empty; 404 if no such list).
- `PATCH /lists/{name}/items` - body = full ordered array of `{ source, id, area? }` -> `{ "name", "items": [...] }` (reorder).
- `DELETE /lists/{name}/items/{source}/{id}` -> `{ "name", "source", "id", "removed": <bool> }` (404 only if the list is absent).
- `POST /lists/{name}/consumer` / `DELETE /lists/{name}/consumer` - single-consumer claim (claim/release). Not required by this client view, but available.

`WorkListDto` = `{ "name": string, "items": WorkListItemRef[], "consumer": string|null }`. `WorkListItemRef` = `{ "source": string, "id": string, "area": string|null }`. The list object carries NO status field (confirms criteria 4-5: the Cockpit must derive status from GitHub `flow:*` labels, never from the list). Contracts: `src/CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts/WorkListDto.cs` and `WorkListItemRef.cs`; endpoints: `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Api/WorkListEndpoints.cs`.

comments.body: Blocked by #273 (named lists REST). No `flow:*` label until #273 reaches `flow:done`; the Product Agent will then label this `flow:ready-dev`. Parent epic: #270.

Product Agent: blocker cleared. Child 2 (#273, named work lists REST) reached flow:done and merged to main, so the only thing gating this child is now resolved. Verified the shipped REST surface on main matches what this issue consumes (POST/GET /lists, GET /lists/{name}, POST/PATCH /lists/{name}/items, DELETE /lists/{name}/items/{source}/{id}) and that WorkListDto carries NO status field - confirming criteria 4-5 (Cockpit derives per-item status from GitHub flow:* labels, never from the list object). Ran the Definition of Ready (Section 5) check: 7/7 PASS. Body sharpened with the final shipped contract (the prior 'if child 2 paths changed' conditional is resolved). Labeled flow:ready-dev.

## Developer Agent - Implemented, handing to QA

The Cockpit Lists page is built as a pure CLIENT of the #273 `/lists` REST object - it reads and writes the same Gateway list (never a local copy or local ordering), and derives per-item status live from each github item's `flow:*` label (the list object carries no status).

**PR:** #284
**Proof:** docs/cencon/proof/issue-275/report.html (PR #284)

### How to Test
1. Run a Gateway + a Cockpit pointed at it; open `/lists`.
2. Create a list in the UI -> verify `GET /lists`.
3. Append a `{source,id,area?}` item, reorder via the arrows/drag handle -> verify `GET /lists/{name}` order.
4. Seed a mixed github/devops/jira list via raw REST -> open the Cockpit, confirm GH/DO/JIRA badges + area chips + per-item status.
5. Change an item's `flow:*` label, refresh -> the badge follows.

### Expected Result
All 7 acceptance criteria pass (Expected-vs-Actual table + screenshots in the report). Renders with no active runner; does not error when #274 is absent.

### Before / After
New page - no "before". After screenshots in the proof folder; side-by-side with the canonical mockup included.

Build: `dotnet build cc-director.sln` clean (0 warnings, 0 errors). 12 new Cockpit unit tests pass. No CenCon drift (pure client UI consuming the existing #273 surface).

## QA Agent - VERIFIED, all 7 acceptance criteria met

Verified **independently** (not trusting the developer's report/screenshots): built the full solution from this PR branch, ran a CLEAN dev Gateway holding the shipped #273 `/lists` object plus a Cockpit pointed at it, and drove the Cockpit UI with Playwright. The user's own Gateway/Cockpit were left untouched.

| # | Criterion | Result |
|---|-----------|--------|
| 1 | Create a list in the UI; appears in `GET /lists` | PASS - created "QA Verify List" in the modal; `GET /lists` returned it from the shared object |
| 2 | Append + reorder reflected in `GET /lists/{name}` | PASS - "Move up" on #273 changed `GET /lists/Today` to `273,275,270,1203,CCD-44` (PATCH to shared object, not local-only) |
| 3 | A REST-created list appears in the Cockpit identically | PASS - "Today"/"Release 0.7" seeded via raw REST render with identical name/order/source/id/area |
| 4 | Source badge + id + area chip + status badge; mixed list; badge follows label change | PASS - GH/DO/JIRA badges, Cockpit/Gateway/Installer/Core chips, real titles; #275=RUNNING (real flow:ready-qa), #273=DONE; adding `flow:done` to #270 flipped QUEUED->DONE on refresh (reverted after) |
| 5 | No separate cached status conflicting with the label | PASS - DTOs carry no status field; status recomputed from GitHub each refresh; badge tracks the live label |
| 6 | Renders with no active runner; no error when #274 absent | PASS - empty list shows empty-state; queued/done "Today" renders cleanly; clean WS circuit, no console errors |
| 7 | Visually matches the canonical mockup + VisualStyle.md | PASS - same nav rail, two-column layout, table columns, badge/chip colors, progress strip; shared cockpit dark-theme tokens |

**Build:** `dotnet build cc-director.sln` clean (0 warnings, 0 errors). **Tests:** 12 Cockpit + 28 Gateway WorkList pass. No CenCon drift (pure client of the existing #273 surface + public GitHub metadata).

**QA proof (my own screenshots):** [docs/cencon/proof/issue-275/qa-report.html](docs/cencon/proof/issue-275/qa-report.html)

Labeling `flow:done` and merging PR #284.

title: [Cockpit] Work-list client: create/reorder a named list and watch per-item status

body:
## Parent epic

Child 3 of 5 for #270 (autonomous work-item queue runner). **Both blockers are now cleared:** child 1 (terminal signal, #272) and child 2 (named lists REST, #273) have reached `flow:done` and merged to `main`. The concrete contracts this runner consumes are now fixed and are pinned below.

> **Decision 2026-06-10 (Option B):** child 2's item ref is the structured `{ source, id, area? }` object. This runner reads `item.source` and `item.id`. In v1 it **drives only `source = github` items** (seeds `/implementation-loop <id>`); `devops` / `jira` refs are **skipped** (left queued/displayed) until a per-source adapter exists - a later phase. Overview: [docs/plans/queue-runner-270/how-it-works.html](docs/plans/queue-runner-270/how-it-works.html).

## Contracts this runner consumes (now shipped to `main` - pinned, not assumed)

**Terminal signal (child 1, #272 - `main`):** the `implementation-loop` skill prints, as its final transcript output for an issue, a single fenced sentinel block (the transport is the **session transcript**, the surface the Gateway/Wingman already capture - no new transport). Spec: `docs/cencon/DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md` Section 7a. Exact shape:

```
IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL
issue: <N>
signal: done | needs-human | failed
pr: <pr number or none>
merged: yes | no
reason: <one line - why this terminal state>
```

The runner correlates by the `issue` field and reads exactly one of the three `signal` values per run. There are exactly three values; `merged: yes` only on `done`.

**Named-list REST (child 2, #273 - `main`):** the Gateway hosts these routes (`src/CcDirector.Gateway/Api/WorkListEndpoints.cs`):

| Method | Path | Used by this runner for |
|--------|------|--------------------------|
| GET | `/lists/{name}` | read the ordered item refs to drain |
| POST | `/lists/{name}/consumer` | claim the single draining consumer (mints/returns `{ name, consumer: <token> }`; a second claim while held returns **HTTP 409 Conflict**) |
| DELETE | `/lists/{name}/consumer` | release the claim when the list is drained |

Item-ref JSON shape (`CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts.WorkListItemRef`): `{ "source": "github", "id": "262", "area": "Gateway" }`. `id` is a **string**. The list carries **no item-status field**; this runner does not write status back to the list.

## Problem / Value

This is the heart of #270: the thin **Gateway queue runner** that turns a named work list into unattended, ordered runs. For each item in a list it starts one implementation session on a Director, seeds it with `/implementation-loop <N>`, watches it to the terminal signal (child 1), then advances to the next item - exactly one item in flight per list, never starting the next until the current reaches a terminal signal. This is the piece that makes the working single-issue loop run hands-off and in order. The Director stays dumb; all orchestration lives here at the Gateway.

## Scope

**IN**
- A per-list consumer (the runner) that, once it holds the list's consumer claim (`POST /lists/{name}/consumer`, child 2), drains the list in `GET /lists/{name}` order.
- For each **`source = github`** item: start one implementation session on a Director, seed the session with `/implementation-loop <item.id>`.
- **Source gating (v1):** when the next item's `source` is not `github`, the runner does NOT start a session for it - it leaves the item in place (queued/displayed) and either skips to the next runnable item or holds, per a documented, deterministic rule. No devops/jira adapter is built here.
- Watch a started session until it emits child 1's `IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL` sentinel and read its `signal` (`done` / `needs-human` / `failed`).
- On the terminal signal: record which of the three signals the item ended on, then advance to the next item in the list.
- Single-consumer enforcement at the runner: only the claim-holder drains; a second drainer is refused (rides child 2's `POST /lists/{name}/consumer` 409).
- Cross-machine: the runner can target a Director on another machine (one list per machine in v1).

**OUT**
- The list object and its REST CRUD (child 2 - reused here).
- The terminal-signal emission (child 1 - read here, not defined here).
- **Driving non-github items.** `devops` / `jira` refs are accepted by the store (child 2) but this runner does not resolve or start them in v1 - that needs a per-source adapter (later phase).
- The Cockpit progress view (child 4) and product-skill producer (child 5).
- Same-machine parallel draining of two lists sharing one slot-5 test Director - explicitly OUT (the loop's single test slot/build-output/port would collide; see #270 Constraint 1). v1 parallelism is across machines only.
- Retry/auto-fix of a `failed` or `needs-human` item - the runner records the signal and advances (or stops the list); it does not re-drive a stuck item.

## Acceptance criteria

1. Given a list with two `source = github` items, the runner (holding the consumer claim from `POST /lists/{name}/consumer`) starts an implementation session for the first item seeded with `/implementation-loop <first.id>`, and does NOT start the second item's session until the first emits an `IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL` sentinel. (Proof: timeline/log showing session-1 start, terminal signal, then session-2 start - never overlapping within one list.)
2. The runner reads the terminal signal by the child-1 contract (parses the `IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL` block's `signal` field, correlated by its `issue` field) and records, per item, which of `done` / `needs-human` / `failed` it ended on. (Proof: the runner's recorded per-item result matches the sentinel the session emitted.)
3. **Source gating:** given a list containing a `devops` or `jira` item, the runner does NOT start a session for it (no `/implementation-loop` is seeded for a non-github ref); it applies the documented skip/hold rule and the non-github item is left untouched in the list. (Proof: log/timeline showing the non-github item was never started, and the documented rule for whether the runner skipped past it or held.)
4. After the last item reaches a terminal signal, the runner stops draining that list and releases its consumer claim via `DELETE /lists/{name}/consumer` (or marks it complete). (Proof: the release request/response, and a `GET /lists/{name}` showing the claim cleared.)
5. A second attempt to drain the same list while the runner holds the claim is refused: a second `POST /lists/{name}/consumer` returns HTTP 409 while the claim is held (rides child 2 criterion 6). (Proof: the 409 response captured.)
6. Two lists on two different machines drain at the same time, one implementation session each, without interfering. (Proof: two concurrent runners, each its own Director, both advancing.)
7. The Director hosting the session contains no list or runner logic - the runner and list live at the Gateway/fleet level (verified by inspection: the new code is under `src/CcDirector.Gateway`, none under the Director/Avalonia host).
8. The runner never starts a same-machine second draining session against the shared slot-5 Director (v1 guard): an attempt to drain a second list on a machine already draining one is refused or queued, not run concurrently. (Proof: documented guard + a test showing the refusal/queue.)

## Affected containers

- `src/CcDirector.Gateway` (the queue runner - the new logic; consumes child 2's `WorkListStore` / `/lists` surface and child 1's transcript sentinel).
- `src/CcDirector.Core` Wingman, if used as the done-sensor read path (see open question) - otherwise the runner polls the session transcript for child 1's `IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL` sentinel directly.
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway.Tests` (runner sequencing + single-machine guard tests).

## Proof target

- A run against a 2-item list on one machine: the timeline showing item-1 session start -> `IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL` signal -> item-2 session start, with the recorded per-item signal for each.
- A cross-machine run: two lists, two Directors, both draining concurrently, screenshot or log.
- A source-gating run: a list with a `devops`/`jira` item showing the runner never seeded `/implementation-loop` for it (criterion 3).
- The single-machine second-list guard exercised (criterion 8).
- The double-claim 409 (criterion 5) and the consumer release (criterion 4) captured.
- Inspection note + file paths proving no runner logic landed in the Director host (criterion 7).
- HTML proof report: Expected vs Actual per criterion.

## Assumptions

- The terminal signal is read per child 1's **shipped** contract: the `IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL` sentinel block on the session transcript, `docs/cencon/DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md` Section 7a (no longer conditional - #272 settled the transport as the transcript sentinel).
- "Start an implementation session on a Director seeded with `/implementation-loop <N>`" uses the Gateway's existing session-create + seed-prompt path (the same one the Cockpit/Director already use to create sessions) - flagged; the Developer Agent confirms the exact existing API.
- Open (from #270, non-blocking): whether the runner uses Wingman's existing transcript read or polls the session for the sentinel. The Developer Agent picks the simpler working path and documents it; this is an implementation choice to be recorded in the proof, not invented design intent.

comments.body: Blocked by #272 (terminal signal) and #273 (named lists REST). No `flow:*` label until both reach `flow:done`; the Product Agent will then label this `flow:ready-dev`. Parent epic: #270.

Both blockers are cleared and merged to `main`, so this child is now `flow:ready-dev`:

- Child 1 (#272, terminal signal) -> `flow:done`, merged (7073b85). The `IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL` sentinel block on the session transcript is now the fixed contract; spec in `docs/cencon/DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md` Section 7a.
- Child 2 (#273, named lists REST) -> `flow:done`, merged (b2b8725). The `/lists` surface (incl. `GET /lists/{name}`, `POST`/`DELETE /lists/{name}/consumer` with HTTP 409 on double-claim) and the structured `{ source, id, area? }` item ref (`CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts.WorkListItemRef`, string `id`) are now fixed.

Sharpened the issue body to pin those shipped contracts rather than leave them conditional:
- Added a "Contracts this runner consumes (now shipped to `main`)" section with the exact sentinel block shape and the exact consumer-claim REST (mint/return token, 409 on second claim, DELETE to release).
- Criteria 1, 2, 4, 5 now reference the concrete `IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL` sentinel and the concrete `POST`/`DELETE /lists/{name}/consumer` behavior (was "rides child 2 criterion 6" only).
- Resolved the stale assumption that the terminal-signal transport "may be re-pointed if child 1 changed the transport" - #272 settled it as the transcript sentinel.

Definition of Ready: 7/7 PASS. The remaining open item (Wingman read vs. transcript poll for the sentinel) is correctly left as a documented implementation choice for the Developer Agent, not invented design intent.

## Developer Agent - Ready for QA

**PR:** #282 (branch `issue-274-queue-runner`)
**Proof:** docs/cencon/proof/issue-274/report.html (report.png screenshot + live-gateway-http.txt)

### Release Notes
The thin Gateway queue runner (child 3 of #270) now drains a named work list (#273) hands-off and in order: claim the list's single-consumer claim, drain in `GET /lists/{name}` order **one github item in flight at a time**, start one implementation session per item seeded with `/implementation-loop <id>`, watch it to child 1's `IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL` sentinel (#272), record the per-item signal, advance, then release the claim. All orchestration lives at the Gateway; the Director stays dumb.

### Changes
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Running/ImplLoopTerminalSignal.cs` - parses the sentinel block, correlated by `issue` (Section 7a).
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Running/WorkListRunner.cs` + `WorkListRunResult.cs` - ordered single-in-flight drain, source-gating, per-item record, claim release, per-item timeout guard.
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Running/WorkListRunnerManager.cs` - v1 same-machine single-drain guard.
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Running/IImplSessionDriver.cs` + `DirectorImplSessionDriver.cs` - seam over the existing CreateSession + PrePrompt seed + buffer read (no new Director surface).
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Api/WorkListRunnerEndpoints.cs` + `GatewayHost.cs` - `POST /lists/{name}/run`.
- `docs/cencon/architecture_manifest.yaml` - runner recorded under the Gateway container.

### How to Test
- `dotnet test src/CcDirector.Gateway.Tests` - 19 unit tests + 1 end-to-end-over-HTTP proof test (`WorkListRunnerEndpointProofTests`) that boots the real `GatewayHost`, registers a stub Director answering `POST /sessions` + `GET /sessions/{sid}/buffer`, and drives `POST /lists/{name}/run` over real HTTP for every criterion.
- Live: the real `CcDirector.Gateway.exe` dev console host served the route (`[WorkListRunnerEndpoints] mapped /lists/{name}/run route`; 404 unknown director/list; 409 double-claim) - see `live-gateway-http.txt`.

### Expected Result
All 8 acceptance criteria PASS - see the Expected-vs-Actual table in report.html / report.png.

### Implementation choice (recorded, not invented design)
The done-sensor reads the session transcript directly via `GET /sessions/{sid}/buffer` and scans for the `IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL` sentinel (the simpler working path; no Wingman dependency) - per the issue's open implementation choice.

### Source-gating rule (criterion 3, documented)
Only `source = github` is runnable in v1. A non-github (devops/jira) item is **skipped** (never started, no `/implementation-loop` seeded) and the runner advances to the next runnable item, leaving the non-github item untouched in the list. It does not hold the whole list on the first non-github ref.

### Note for QA (pre-existing foreign working-tree files)
My commit touched **zero** `tools/` files. The working tree carries three unrelated `tools/cc-director-setup*` files (issue #251 Gateway-update role-detection work from a co-resident session) that predate this branch; I deliberately did not absorb, delete, or stash another session's WIP. All #274 work is committed to the PR branch and pushed.

I believe this is finished.

## QA Agent - VERIFIED (PASS)

Independent verification complete - I did NOT trust the Developer Agent's report or screenshots. I checked out PR #282 (branch `issue-274-queue-runner`), built the full solution myself, and ran the tests myself, generating my own proof.

**Proof:** [docs/cencon/proof/issue-274/qa-report.html](docs/cencon/proof/issue-274/qa-report.html)

### Result: 8/8 acceptance criteria PASS

| # | Criterion | QA observed | Result |
|---|-----------|-------------|--------|
| 1 | Two github items drain in order, one in flight, never overlapping | `start order=262,263; seeds=/implementation-loop 262 \| /implementation-loop 263; overlap=False` | PASS |
| 2 | Per-item signal parsed from IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL, correlated by issue, recorded | `262=Done; 263=NeedsHuman` | PASS |
| 3 | Source gating: devops/jira never started, left untouched in list | `start order=262 only; outcomes=SkippedNonGithub,Ran,SkippedNonGithub; list still=1203,262,CCD-44` | PASS |
| 4 | Consumer claim released after last item; GET shows cleared | `consumerReleased=True; GET consumer=null` | PASS |
| 5 | Second claim while held returns HTTP 409 | `409` | PASS |
| 6 | Two machines drain concurrently, no interference | `m1 100->Done; m2 200->Done; both OK` | PASS |
| 7 | No runner logic in Director host | `Gateway/Running=6 files; Avalonia/ControlApi=0` (my own grep) | PASS |
| 8 | Same-machine second drain refused (v1 guard) | `409` | PASS |

### How verified
- `dotnet build cc-director.sln` -> **Build succeeded. 0 Warning(s) 0 Error(s)**.
- 19/19 #274 tests pass, including `WorkListRunnerEndpointProofTests` which boots the real `GatewayHost` and drives `POST /lists/{name}/run` over real HTTP (Bearer-authed) for every criterion. My own run wrote "Overall: ALL CRITERIA PASS".

### Regression / method
- Adjacent #273 consumer-claim surface intact (exercised by the e2e test).
- Full Gateway.Tests: 385 passed / 1 failed. The lone failure is `DictationEndpointTests.FullPipeline_transcribes_phase0_clip2_with_realtime_provider` - a live realtime-STT WebSocket timing test, NOT touched by this branch, a pre-existing flake, NOT a regression from #274.
- CenCon `architecture_manifest.yaml` updated (runner recorded under the Gateway container); no security drift; route behind host-wide auth.

Labeling `flow:done` and squash-merging PR #282 to main (clean-build gate) per the implementation-loop.

title: [Gateway] Queue runner drains one named list, one implementation session per item

body:
## Parent epic

Child 2 of 5 for #270 (autonomous work-item queue runner). Independent of the terminal-signal child - can be built in parallel. Children 3, 4 and 5 all depend on THIS one (it defines the shared list object).

> **Decision 2026-06-10 (Option B - item-ref model promoted now):** the stored item reference is a **structured `{ source, id, area? }`** object, not a bare integer. This makes the multi-source Cockpit board ([docs/plans/queue-runner-270/lists-ui-mockup.html](docs/plans/queue-runner-270/lists-ui-mockup.html)) real from day one. `source = github` is the only **runnable** source in v1 (#274 drives only GitHub items); `devops` / `jira` refs are **accepted, stored, ordered, and displayed** but not yet drained. Overview + UI-to-issue map: [docs/plans/queue-runner-270/how-it-works.html](docs/plans/queue-runner-270/how-it-works.html). This supersedes the old "bare GitHub issue number" assumption B2 below.

## Problem / Value

#270 needs a shared, addressable definition of "what to implement next, in what order." Today that does not exist - work order lives only in a human's head and the `flow:ready-dev` queue is just creation order. This child adds **named work lists** as a first-class REST object at the Gateway: an ordered list of structured work-item references `{ source, id, area? }`, CRUD-able over REST, with a single-consumer claim. It is the object the product skill writes to, the Cockpit views, and the queue runner drains - so it must exist before any of those. This child delivers ONLY the object and its REST surface; no draining, no sessions.

## Scope

**IN**
- A named work list object at the Gateway/fleet level: a name + an ordered list of **structured item references**. Each ref is `{ source, id, area? }`:
  - `source` - a string enum: `github` (the only **runnable** source in v1), with `devops` and `jira` accepted and stored as reserved/non-runnable sources.
  - `id` - a **string** identifier within that source (e.g. `"262"` for github, `"1203"` for devops, `"CCD-44"` for jira). String, not int, so it holds non-numeric keys.
  - `area` - optional free-text grouping label for display (e.g. `Gateway` / `Core` / `Installer` / `Cockpit` in the board). Pure metadata; the store does not interpret it.
- REST CRUD surface (see table). The list stores **order + the structured refs + single-consumer assignment ONLY**. It never stores item status.
- Mixed-source items coexist in one list (a list is a named priority list, not sliced by source or area); order is preserved across sources.
- Single-consumer claim semantics: a list has at most one active consumer; claiming an already-claimed list is refused.
- In-memory store is acceptable for v1 (persistence across Gateway restart is explicitly OUT - see #270 roadmap).

**OUT**
- The queue runner that drains a list (child 3). (Note: child 3's v1 runner drives only `source = github` items; non-github refs stay queued/displayed.)
- Any session start, `/implementation-loop` seeding, or Wingman read (child 3).
- The Cockpit UI (child 4) and the product-skill producer call (child 5) - though both consume this API.
- Reading/echoing item status from GitHub labels (this object deliberately does not duplicate status; consumers read labels themselves).
- **Resolving** a devops/jira ref to a runnable action - the model carries them, but no source adapter exists in v1 (later phase).
- List persistence across Gateway restart.

## REST surface (this child delivers exactly this)

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| POST | `/lists` | Create a named list (body: name) |
| GET | `/lists` | List all named lists with their ordered item refs |
| GET | `/lists/{name}` | Read one list's ordered item refs |
| POST | `/lists/{name}/items` | Append a structured item ref (body: `{ source, id, area? }`) |
| PATCH | `/lists/{name}/items` | Reorder items (body: full ordered ref array) |
| DELETE | `/lists/{name}/items/{source}/{id}` | Remove one item (addressed by source + id) |
| POST | `/lists/{name}/consumer` | Claim the single draining consumer for this list |
| DELETE | `/lists/{name}/consumer` | Release the consumer claim |

Item-ref JSON shape (v1):

```json
{ "source": "github", "id": "262", "area": "Gateway" }
```

**ASSUMPTION B1 (flagged):** the endpoints live on the Gateway's existing API surface (the same host/auth the Cockpit and Directors already use), not a brand-new service. **DECISION B2 (Option B, settled):** an item reference is the structured `{ source, id, area? }` object above - `id` is a string, `source` is `github` (runnable) / `devops` / `jira` (stored-only in v1). No repo qualifier yet (single-repo assumption for v1, matching #270); a repo field can be added later without breaking the shape. B1 remains overridable; B2 is decided per the epic's Option B.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `POST /lists` creates a named list; `GET /lists` then returns it. (Proof: Control API request/response pair.)
2. `POST /lists/{name}/items` with a structured ref `{ source, id, area? }` appends it; `GET /lists/{name}` shows it in order with `source`, `id`, and `area` round-tripped exactly. Appending three items yields them in append order.
3. A list accepts and stores **mixed-source** refs in one ordered list: appending `{github,"262"}`, `{devops,"1203"}`, and `{jira,"CCD-44"}` yields all three, in order, each with its source preserved (no source is rejected at the store layer; runnability is child 3's concern, not the store's).
4. `PATCH /lists/{name}/items` with a reordered array reorders the list; a subsequent `GET` reflects the new order exactly.
5. `DELETE /lists/{name}/items/{source}/{id}` removes that one item; `GET` no longer shows it; other items keep their relative order.
6. `POST /lists/{name}/consumer` succeeds the first time and returns a claim; a second `POST` while the claim is held is refused (HTTP 409 or equivalent documented status). `DELETE /lists/{name}/consumer` releases it, after which a new claim succeeds.
7. The stored list contains only name, ordered structured refs (`source`/`id`/`area`), and consumer assignment - it has **no item-status field** (verified by inspecting the GET payload: no `status`/`flow` field present).
8. The endpoints are reachable cross-machine via the Gateway (not bound to a single Director) - verified by a request from outside the Gateway process.

## Affected containers

- `src/CcDirector.Gateway` (the named-list store + claim semantics, the new logic).
- `src/CcDirector.ControlApi` or the Gateway's API surface (the `/lists` endpoints - whichever hosts Gateway-level REST; name the chosen one in the proof).
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts` (DTOs for list + the structured `{ source, id, area? }` item ref + claim, if Gateway DTOs live here).
- `src/CcDirector.Gateway.Tests` (unit tests for store, mixed-source append/round-trip, and claim refusal).

## Proof target

- A captured sequence of Control API requests/responses exercising criteria 1-8 (create -> append github/devops/jira -> reorder -> delete by source+id -> claim -> double-claim-refused -> release -> re-claim), each with the JSON shown.
- A GET payload showing a mixed-source list with `source`/`id`/`area` round-tripped and visibly **lacking any status field** (criteria 2, 3, 7).
- One request issued from outside the Gateway process proving cross-machine reachability (criterion 8).
- Unit tests green for the store, mixed-source round-trip, and the single-consumer refusal.
- HTML proof report: Expected vs Actual per criterion.

## Assumptions

- B1 (endpoints on the existing Gateway API surface) - flagged, overridable.
- B2 (structured `{ source, id, area? }` item ref; `github` runnable, `devops`/`jira` stored-only; single-repo v1) - **decided** per the epic's Option B, not merely flagged.
- In-memory store is sufficient for v1 (persistence is OUT per #270 roadmap) - taken as decided by #270.

comments.body:
## Developer Agent - Ready for QA

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-273/report.html  (PR #278)

### Release Notes
Named work lists are now a first-class Gateway REST resource: an ordered list of structured item refs `{ source, id, area? }` with a single-consumer claim. This is the shared object epic #270's queue runner (#274), Cockpit board (#275), and product producer (#276) will build on. In-memory for v1 (persistence is OUT per #270). github is the runnable source; devops/jira are accepted, stored, ordered, and displayed but not drained.

### Changes
- `CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts`: `WorkListItemRef` (`{ source, id(string), area? }`) and `WorkListDto` (`name` + ordered `items` + single `consumer`; **no status/flow field**).
- `CcDirector.Gateway/WorkListStore.cs`: lock-guarded in-memory fleet store - Create / ListAll / Get / AppendItem / Reorder / RemoveItem(by source+id) / Claim / Release. Stores order + refs + claim only; never item status; never rejects a source. Defensive copies on read; FileLog on every public method.
- `CcDirector.Gateway/Api/WorkListEndpoints.cs`: the 8 routes from the issue table.
- `CcDirector.Gateway/GatewayHost.cs`: wires the routes **after** the host-wide auth middleware (alongside VaultEndpoints), so `/lists` inherits Bearer-token auth and is reachable cross-machine via the Gateway - not bound to a single Director (ASSUMPTION B1 honored).
- Tests: `WorkListStoreTests` (10) + `WorkListEndpointsTests` (9), all green.
- `docs/cencon/architecture_manifest.yaml`: WorkListStore + WorkListEndpoints added to the Gateway container.

### How to Test
Boot a Gateway (auth on), then drive the sequence: create -> append github/devops/jira -> reorder (PATCH) -> delete by source+id -> claim -> double-claim (expect 409) -> release -> re-claim, and GET to inspect payloads. The full captured request/response transcript is at `docs/cencon/proof/issue-273/rest-transcript.txt`; the reproducible harness is `proof-harness.cs`.

### Expected Result
All 8 acceptance criteria pass. Notable wire responses: double-claim returns **409 Conflict**; GET payload carries only `name`/`items`/`consumer` (and per-item `source`/`id`/`area`) with **no status/flow field**; mixed-source refs round-trip in order with no source rejected.

### Before / After
Before: no shared, addressable definition of "what to implement next, in what order" - work order lived only in a human's head. After: a named, ordered, CRUD-able work-list object at the Gateway with single-consumer claim semantics, behind the existing auth, ready for #274/#275/#276 to consume.

### Verification
- `dotnet build cc-director.sln` -> Build succeeded, 0 Warning(s), 0 Error(s).
- 19 unit tests green (store + HTTP wire).
- Criteria 1-8 proven over real HTTP against a real `GatewayHost` (auth ON, Tailscale OFF); criterion 8 issued from a **separate OS process** (powershell.exe, distinct pid from the Gateway).
- Slot-5 build v0.6.22 launched via the `cc-director-launch` scheduled task and answered `/healthz`.

I believe this is finished.

## QA Agent - VERIFIED (flow:done)

Independent verification by the CenCon QA Agent. I did NOT reuse the Developer Agent's binary or transcript - I built the solution from this PR branch (clean: 0 warnings, 0 errors), booted the **real `GatewayHost`** (full wiring: host-wide Bearer-token auth middleware + routing + `WorkListEndpoints`) with auth ON on a loopback port, and drove every criterion over real HTTP with my own harness.

**All 8 acceptance criteria PASS:**

| # | Criterion | Observed |
|---|-----------|----------|
| 1 | POST /lists creates; GET /lists returns it | POST 200; GET shows `backlog` |
| 2 | Append round-trips source/id/area in order | ids=[262,263,264]; areas=[Gateway,Core,null]; source preserved |
| 3 | Mixed-source refs accepted + ordered | github/262, devops/1203, jira/CCD-44 all stored, in order, none rejected |
| 4 | PATCH reorders | after PATCH order = [CCD-44, 262, 1203] |
| 5 | DELETE by source+id removes one; rest keep order | DELETE github/262 -> removed; remaining [CCD-44, 1203] |
| 6 | Single-consumer claim | 1st=200, 2nd(held)=**409 Conflict**, release=200, re-claim=200 |
| 7 | No item-status field | top-level=[name,items,consumer]; per-item=[source,id,area]; no status/flow |
| 8 | Cross-machine via Gateway, from outside the process | **separate OS process** curl.exe (PID 20148, distinct from host PID 31404): GET /lists with token=200; without token=401 |

**Auth guard:** /lists is mapped after the host-wide auth middleware; GET /lists with no Bearer token returns 401. No new unauthenticated surface.

**Method/regression checks:**
- `dotnet build cc-director.sln` -> Build succeeded, 0 Warning(s), 0 Error(s).
- 19 work-list unit tests green.
- Full Gateway suite: 366/367. The one failure (`DictationEndpointTests.FullPipeline_transcribes_phase0_clip2_with_realtime_provider`) is a **pre-existing environmental** failure - it opens a live OpenAI realtime transcription websocket and gets 0 partials without network/API key. Not in this PR's changed files (last touched by unrelated commit d82db15), zero coupling to work-list code. Not a regression from #273.
- `architecture_manifest.yaml` updated with WorkListStore + WorkListEndpoints.
- New source is ASCII-only; no fallback programming; FileLog on every public store method.

Proof report (Expected vs Actual per criterion) + full request/response transcript:
- `docs/cencon/proof/issue-273/qa-report.html`
- `docs/cencon/proof/issue-273/qa-rest-transcript.txt`

title: [Gateway] Named work lists as a first-class REST resource (CRUD + ordering + single-consumer claim)

body:
## Parent epic

Child 1 of 5 for #270 (autonomous work-item queue runner). **This is the keystone: every other child depends on the contract defined here.**

## Problem / Value

The `implementation-loop` supervisor already reaches exactly one of three terminal outcomes per issue - MERGED (`flow:done`), PARKED (`flow:needs-human`), ESCALATED (`flow:needs-human`) - but it only writes them as a human-readable one-line report into its own context (skill Step 4). There is no machine-readable signal an external watcher (the future Gateway queue runner, #270) can read without parsing prose. Without an unambiguous terminal signal, nothing can know when one loop run has finished so the next item can start. This child defines and emits that signal. It is a contract change, not a feature - but it is the single thing the whole #270 feature hinges on.

## Scope

**IN**
- Define a single, machine-readable terminal-signal contract with exactly three values: `done`, `needs-human`, `failed`.
- Map the loop's existing outcomes onto the three values: MERGED -> `done`; PARKED or ESCALATED -> `needs-human`; loop could not complete (pre-flight dirty-tree stop, crash, build-tool failure, any abnormal exit) -> `failed`.
- Emit the signal as the loop's final action on EVERY terminal path, as a single fenced sentinel block printed to the session transcript (the surface the Gateway already captures), in addition to (not replacing) the existing human one-line report.
- Document the contract in `docs/cencon/DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md` (or a linked contract file) so the Gateway child (#270 / child 3) can be built against a stable spec.

**OUT**
- The Gateway-side reader/parser of the signal (that is child 3, the queue runner).
- Any REST endpoint, list object, or Cockpit surface (other children).
- Changing the loop's actual outcomes or merge authority - only adding the signal emission.

## Proposed sentinel format (ASSUMPTION - flagged for human/Developer; this is the one genuinely new contract)

As the loop's last line on every terminal path:

```
IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL
issue: <N>
signal: done | needs-human | failed
pr: <pr number or none>
merged: yes | no
reason: <one line - why this terminal state>
```

**Assumption A1:** the signal travels on the **session transcript** (matching #270's own diagram: `"FINISHED: done|needs-human|failed"` flowing from the session up to the queue runner), NOT as a new GitHub issue comment or a new REST call. Rationale: the Gateway/Wingman already capture the session transcript, so this adds no new transport. If the human/Developer prefers the signal as a GitHub issue comment or a Gateway POST, that overrides this assumption - but the acceptance criteria below are written against the transcript-sentinel form and must be re-pointed if the transport changes.

**Assumption A2:** the literal token block above is the format. Any single stable, greppable, key/value block is acceptable; the exact keys can change as long as `signal` carries one of the three values and `issue` identifies the item.

## Acceptance criteria

1. On a MERGED outcome the loop prints, as its final transcript output, a sentinel block whose `signal:` value is exactly `done`, with the merged PR number and `merged: yes`.
2. On a PARKED or ESCALATED outcome the loop prints a sentinel block whose `signal:` value is exactly `needs-human`, with `merged: no` and a one-line `reason`.
3. On an abnormal stop (pre-flight dirty-tree stop per skill Step 0a, or any path where the loop cannot complete) the loop prints a sentinel block whose `signal:` value is exactly `failed`, with `merged: no` and a `reason`.
4. The sentinel block is emitted IN ADDITION to the existing human-readable one-line report (the report is unchanged); the two do not conflict.
5. Exactly one sentinel block is emitted per issue per run (a watcher reading the last sentinel block gets an unambiguous answer).
6. The contract (the three values, their mapping to loop outcomes, and the block format) is written into `docs/cencon/DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md` or a linked contract doc, so child 3 can be built against it.

## Affected containers

- `.claude/skills/implementation-loop` (emit the sentinel as the final step of Step 4 on every terminal path; document the three values).
- `docs/cencon/DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md` (record the terminal-signal contract).

## Proof target

Because this is a skill/contract change with **no .NET build artifact**, the Developer Agent proves it by **exercising the loop**, not by `dotnet build`/slot-5 screenshot:
- A captured transcript (or log) excerpt showing the exact sentinel block for each of the three signals (`done`, `needs-human`, `failed`) - the `done` case from a real or stubbed merge path, the `needs-human` case from a parked/escalated path, the `failed` case from a forced pre-flight dirty-tree stop.
- The diff of `implementation-loop/skill.md` and the contract doc.
- The HTML proof report states Expected vs Actual for each of the 6 acceptance criteria, quoting the emitted sentinel lines.

## Note to the Developer Agent

This child has no compiled output. Do NOT reject it for "no .NET change" - the deliverable is a skill-contract change proven by exercising the loop and capturing the emitted sentinel. If you believe the transport assumption (A1: transcript sentinel) is wrong, that is a design decision for the human - label `flow:rejected` ONLY if an acceptance criterion is untestable as written, not because you would have chosen a different transport.

## Assumptions

- A1 (transport = session transcript) and A2 (block format) above - both flagged, both overridable by the human.
- The three-value vocabulary (`done | needs-human | failed`) is taken directly from #270 and treated as decided by the human who wrote #270.

comments.body:
## Developer Agent - Ready for QA

Implemented. The implementation-loop skill now emits a single machine-readable `IMPL-LOOP-TERMINAL` sentinel block as its final output on every terminal path, in addition to the unchanged human one-line report. Contract recorded in DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md Section 7a.

### Signal mapping
- MERGED -> `signal: done`, `merged: yes`
- PARKED / ESCALATED / merge-conflict / dirty-post-merge-build -> `signal: needs-human`, `merged: no`
- pre-flight dirty-tree stop (Step 0a) / abnormal exit -> `signal: failed`, `merged: no`

### Acceptance criteria
All 6 met (Expected vs Actual in the report). The `failed` signal was reproduced genuinely (forced pre-flight dirty tree halted Step 0a); `needs-human` and `done` shown from stubbed park/merge paths (permitted by the proof target). Captured sentinel blocks are quoted in the report and in sentinel-transcripts.txt.

### CenCon impact
Updates DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md (where criterion 6 requires the contract) - intended, not drift. No architecture_manifest / security_profile change (signal rides the existing session transcript; no new transport).

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-272/report.html  (PR #281)

## QA Agent - VERIFIED (flow:done)

Independent verification on branch `issue-272-terminal-signal` (PR #281). I read the actual committed source (not the Developer's report) and confirmed the sentinel on every terminal path the issue enumerates.

All 6 acceptance criteria PASS:
1. MERGED -> `signal: done`, merged PR, `merged: yes` (skill.md Step 4 mapping + example block).
2. PARKED/ESCALATED/conflict -> `signal: needs-human`, `merged: no`, reason.
3. Abnormal stop (Step 0a dirty-tree / leftover-stash / wrong-base, plus dirty Step-4 gate / build-tool failure / crash) -> `signal: failed`, `merged: no`, reason.
4. Sentinel emitted IN ADDITION to the unchanged one-line report (printed after it).
5. Exactly one sentinel per issue per run; a FAIL bounce is non-terminal and emits none.
6. Contract recorded in DEVELOPMENT_METHOD.md Section 7a - block shape matches skill.md byte-for-byte.

Method sweep: only skill + contract + proof files changed (no .NET source, clean-build gate N/A); both edited docs are ASCII-only (no-unicode); no guards removed; no architecture_manifest/security_profile change needed (signal rides the existing transcript transport).

QA proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-272/qa-report.html

title: [CenCon] implementation-loop emits a machine-readable terminal signal (done | needs-human | failed)

body:
> **Sliced + decided (2026-06-10):** broken into this epic + 5 children #272-#276 (dependency-ordered). **Option B adopted for the item-ref model:** a list item is a structured `{ source, id, area? }` ref (not a bare issue number), so the multi-source Cockpit board is real from day one - but the v1 runner drives only `source = github` items; `devops`/`jira` refs are stored/displayed, not drained, until a per-source adapter exists (later phase). Design overview + UI: [docs/plans/queue-runner-270/how-it-works.html](docs/plans/queue-runner-270/how-it-works.html), mockup [docs/plans/queue-runner-270/lists-ui-mockup.html](docs/plans/queue-runner-270/lists-ui-mockup.html). ## Summary Add **named work lists** as a first-class object in the **REST API**, and an autonomous **queue runner** in the **Gateway** that drains them. A list is a clear definition of **implementation order** for a stream of work. The **product skill** (which will live in every repo) appends ready-for-dev items to a list over REST; the **Cockpit** is just another client that views and edits the same lists. For each item in a list the Gateway starts one implementation session, seeds it with `/implementation-loop `, watches it to a clear terminal signal, then advances. Multiple lists run in parallel, one implementation session per list, across separate machines (and later git worktrees). The **Director stays dumb** (it only hosts the session); the lists and the watching live at the Gateway/fleet level. ## Problem and value Today `/implementation-loop ` drives a single issue from DEV through QA to a squash-merge, but a human has to start it, watch it, and start the next one. There is no shared, addressable definition of what to implement next, and no way to run several streams at once. This issue adds the thin orchestration layer that turns the working single-issue loop into unattended, ordered, parallelizable runs, without putting any orchestration logic into the Director. ## Core idea 1. A **named work list** is a first-class REST object: an ordered list of structured work-item references `{ source, id, area? }` (Option B). `source = github` is runnable in v1; `devops`/`jira` are stored/displayed only. 2. The **product skill** appends an item to a chosen list over REST whenever it marks that item ready for dev. The list owns **order plus queue assignment only**; the item's status stays in the GitHub `flow:*` labels (single source of truth). 3. The **Gateway queue runner** drains a list: for each item it starts one implementation session, seeds `/implementation-loop `, watches it (reusing Wingman as the done-sensor) until a terminal signal, then advances. 4. **Multiple lists** run in parallel, one draining session per list, like two developers each working their own queue. Parallelism is across separate machines first; same-machine git worktrees come later (see Constraints). 5. The **Cockpit** is one client of the list API (create/view/reorder lists, watch progress). It is not the owner. ## Flow ```mermaid flowchart TD P["Product skill (in any repo)"] -->|"REST: add item to list"| L U["You (Cockpit)"] -->|"REST: create/reorder/watch"| L subgraph GW["GATEWAY / fleet level"] L["Named work lists (REST objects)
list A: [#101, #102] list B: [#205]"] QR["Queue runner: one consumer per list"] L --> QR end QR -->|"start session, seed /implementation-loop N"| D1["Director (dumb host) - machine 1"] QR -->|"start session, seed /implementation-loop M"| D2["Director (dumb host) - machine 2"] D1 --> S1["Impl session: DEV -> QA -> merge to main"] D2 --> S2["Impl session: DEV -> QA -> merge to main"] S1 -->|"terminal: done | needs-human | failed"| QR S2 -->|"terminal: done | needs-human | failed"| QR QR -->|"advance each list"| L ``` ASCII view (single list, single consumer): ``` PRODUCT SKILL (any repo) YOU (Cockpit) | REST: add item | REST: create / reorder / watch v v +-----------------------------------------------------+ | GATEWAY / fleet level | | | | NAMED WORK LISTS (REST objects) | | list A: [#101, #102, #103] <- one consumer | | list B: [#205, #206] <- one consumer | | | | QUEUE RUNNER (per list): | | 1. start session, seed "/implementation-loop N" | | 2. watch via Wingman: done? stuck? | | 3. on terminal signal -> next item | +-----------------------------------------------------+ | start / monitor ^ "FINISHED: done|needs-human|failed" v | +-----------------------------------------------------+ | DIRECTOR (dumb host) | | +---------------------------------------------+ | | | IMPLEMENTATION SESSION | | | | /implementation-loop #N | | | | DEV -> QA -> (bounce) -> merge to main | | | +---------------------------------------------+ | +-----------------------------------------------------+ ``` ## Components and responsibilities | Component | Role in this feature | New or existing | |-----------|----------------------|-----------------| | Named work lists (REST) | First-class ordered lists of work-item references. The shared definition of implementation order. | New | | Product skill | Producer. Appends ready-for-dev items to a list over REST. | Existing skill, new REST call | | Cockpit | A client: create/reorder lists, submit, watch per-item progress. | Existing surface, new control | | Gateway queue runner | Drains each list; one consumer per list; starts a session per item, watches to terminal signal, advances. | New, thin | | Wingman | The done-sensor. Answers "is this session finished or stuck." | Existing, reused | | Director | Dumb host for the session. No list logic, no orchestration. | Existing, unchanged | | Implementation session | Runs `/implementation-loop ` (existing DEV -> QA -> merge loop). | Existing, unchanged | ## REST surface (minimal sketch) | Method | Path | Purpose | |--------|------|---------| | POST | `/lists` | Create a named list | | GET | `/lists` and `/lists/{name}` | Read lists and their ordered items | | POST | `/lists/{name}/items` | Append a structured ref `{ source, id, area? }` (used by the product skill) | | PATCH | `/lists/{name}/items` | Reorder items | | DELETE | `/lists/{name}/items/{source}/{id}` | Remove an item | | POST | `/lists/{name}/consumer` | Assign/claim the single draining session for this list | (The list stores order plus queue assignment only. Item status is read from GitHub labels, never duplicated here.) ## The contract this feature depends on The whole feature hinges on the implementation-loop emitting an **unambiguous terminal signal** the Gateway can read without interpreting prose: - `done` (merged to main, flow:done) - `needs-human` (escalated: weak spec, 3-strike, conflict, or dirty build) - `failed` (loop could not complete) Defining and emitting that signal is part of this issue. ## Constraints (must respect) 1. **Same-machine parallelism is gated by the loop's single test slot.** The developer-agent and qa-agent build and prove on a single reserved test Director ("slot 5") with one build output and one Control API port, and require strictly sequential sub-agents. So two implementation sessions on one machine, even in separate worktrees, would collide on slot 5, the build output, and the port. Cross-machine parallelism is free (each machine has its own slot 5). Same-machine worktree parallelism requires per-session isolation of test slot, build output, and port, which does not exist today and is a later phase. 2. **One consumer per list.** A list has at most one active draining session, enforced via the consumer claim, so two drainers never race the same items. 3. **Single source of truth for item state.** Item status lives in the GitHub `flow:*` labels. The list owns order and assignment only. 4. **Parallel merges to main raise conflict escalations.** Independent sessions all squash-merge to the same `main`; near-simultaneous finishes will hit conflicts more often. This is safe because the loop never force-merges (it escalates `needs-human`), but expect the escalation rate to rise with parallelism. 5. **Lists live at the Gateway/fleet level**, reachable cross-machine and by the product skill in any repo, not inside a single Director. ## Acceptance criteria 1. Named work lists are CRUD-able over the REST API: create a list, add an item, reorder, remove, read. 2. The product skill can append a ready-for-dev work item to a named list via REST, and the item appears in that list's order. 3. The Gateway queue runner drains a list, starting exactly one implementation session per item (seeded with `/implementation-loop `), and never starts the next item in that list until the current one reaches a terminal signal. 4. A list enforces a single consumer: a second attempt to drain the same list is refused while one is active. 5. The runner advances on any of the three terminal signals (`done`, `needs-human`, `failed`) and records which one per item. 6. The Cockpit is a client only: it can create/reorder a list and show live per-item status (queued / running / done / needs-human / failed). The list is identical whether reached from the Cockpit or the product skill. 7. Item state is read from GitHub labels; the list never stores a conflicting status. 8. The Director contains no list or orchestration logic for this feature (verified by inspection: the queue runner and lists live at the Gateway/fleet level). ## Affected containers - `src/CcDirector.Gateway` (named lists store + queue runner, the new logic) - `src/CcDirector.ControlApi` or the Gateway's API surface (the `/lists` REST endpoints) - `src/CcDirector.Cockpit/wwwroot` (list create/reorder control and progress view, as a client) - `src/CcDirector.Core/Wingman` (reused as the done-sensor; may need a clean terminal-signal read) - `.claude/skills/implementation-loop` (emit the unambiguous terminal signal) - the product skill (new REST call to append items to a list) - to be added per repo ## Roadmap - **v1:** named lists over REST with the structured `{ source, id, area? }` item ref (Option B); GitHub the only **runnable** source; product skill and Cockpit as clients (Cockpit renders source/area for all refs); cross-machine parallelism (one list per machine); the terminal-signal contract. - **Later:** same-machine worktree parallelism (after slot/build/port isolation is solved); **source adapters** that make `devops` / `jira` refs runnable (the model already carries them); list persistence across a Gateway restart. ## Open questions (not blocking v1) - **Product-skill pass:** this spec should be run through the Director's product skill to reach Definition of Ready before it goes to `flow:ready-dev`. (Intentionally deferred per the request that created this issue.) - **Where exactly the watcher reads the terminal state:** confirm whether Wingman already exposes a clean "terminal" read or whether the queue runner polls session state plus the loop's signal. - **List persistence:** whether an in-progress list must survive a Gateway restart, or best-effort in-memory is acceptable for v1. ## Notes This is the unattended, fleet-level wrapper around the already-working single-issue `/implementation-loop`. The Director stays dumb, the Gateway gains named lists plus a thin queue runner, the product skill and Cockpit are clients of the same lists, and the only genuinely new contract is the loop's terminal signal.

comments.body:
## Product Agent refinement (2026-06-10)

Reviewed against the CenCon Definition of Ready. This issue is the right design, but it is an **epic spanning 5-6 containers**, not a single `flow:ready-dev` item - the `implementation-loop` drives ONE issue DEV -> QA -> merge, so this is kept as the tracking epic (no `flow:*` label) and sliced into five children, each meeting the full DoR. Dependency order matters (signal-first), so children are released to `flow:ready-dev` only as their blockers reach `flow:done`.

### Children (dependency order)

- [ ] **#272 `[CenCon]` implementation-loop emits a machine-readable terminal signal** (`done | needs-human | failed`) - **keystone, no deps. `flow:ready-dev` now.** Everything hinges on this contract.
- [ ] **#273 `[Gateway]` Named work lists as a REST resource** (CRUD + ordering + single-consumer claim) - independent of #272, **`flow:ready-dev` now.**
- [ ] **#274 `[Gateway]` Queue runner drains one list, one session per item** - **blocked by #272 + #273.**
- [ ] **#275 `[Cockpit]` Work-list client** (create/reorder + per-item status) - **blocked by #273.**
- [ ] **#276 `[CenCon]` product-agent skill appends an item to a list over REST** - **blocked by #273.**

Cross-machine parallelism falls out of #273 + #274 for free. Same-machine worktree parallelism stays OUT (the loop's single slot-5 / build-output / port collide - Constraint 1 here).

### DoR gaps that drove the slice (recorded for the audit trail)

- **Title** lacked an area prefix - retitled `[Gateway]`.
- **Scope** had no explicit IN/OUT - the Roadmap/Constraints implied it; now made explicit per child.
- **Acceptance criteria** depended on the terminal-signal contract that was named but unspecified - that contract is now its own child (#272) and is concrete there.
- **Proof Target** was missing - each child now states exactly what its screenshot/HTML report must show.
