- AC2: poisoned installed version logs loudly, does NOT report UpToDate, prefers exe FileVersion.
- AC3: normal install/update still completes only when the exe was actually swapped.

**Before/After:** Before, the test wrote `gateway 9.9.9` into production `installed.json` -> UpToDate -> swap skipped. After, the test writes only to an isolated root, and the planner rejects the anomalous version.

Note: the full live `test-gateway-selfupdate.ps1` end-to-end run (builds + spawns gateways, binds ports) was NOT run on this busy shared machine to protect the live fleet (7878/7470/7887 confirmed 200). Its isolation mechanism is proven hermetically by `SelfUpdateIsolationTests`.

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-176/report.html  (PR #306)

## QA Agent - PASS (independently verified)

All three acceptance criteria verified by QA on a fresh checkout of PR #306, with QA's own build and test run (the Developer's binaries/output were not reused). Live fleet (7878/7470/7887) confirmed 200 throughout; production setup state kept byte-identical.

| AC | Criterion | Result |
|----|-----------|--------|
| 1 | Self-update test leaves production `installed.json`/`update-pins.json` byte-identical | PASS |
| 2 | Poisoned (installed > release) version -> loud WARNING, NOT UpToDate, prefers exe FileVersion | PASS |
| 3 | Normal install/update still completes; UpToDate only when truly current | PASS |

**AC1** - QA hashed the production setup files at baseline, after the build + full 153-test run, and after the production-root refusal run: all identical (`installed.json` BFDBD7E2..., `update-pins.json` E3566B3A...). The `RecordingFakeVersions_UnderIsolatedRoot_LeavesProductionSetupStateUntouched` test (which performs the real `InstalledManifest.Set(gateway,9.9.9).Save()` + `PinStore.Pin` under `CC_DIRECTOR_ROOT`) passed in QA's run. QA also exercised the script's production-root refusal: `-Root %LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director` exited 2 with a refusal message before any build or spawn.

**AC2** - QA ran the guard tests: `PoisonedInstalledVersion_DoesNotReportUpToDate_PrefersExeFileVersion` (exact #176 case), `..._NoReadableExeStamp_ReappliesInsteadOfUpToDate`, `..._ExeAlreadyAtRelease_ReportsUpToDateFromExeStamp` - all green. Loud `EngineLog.Write` WARNING confirmed in `UpdatePlanner.Plan`. Guard fires only on `present && IsNewer(recorded, release) && recorded != fileVersion`; `VersionUtil.IsNewer` returns false on unparseable input, so legitimate states are never misflagged.

**AC3** - `NormalInstalledVersion_NotTreatedAsPoisoned` + existing independent-versioning/pinning tests green.

**QA build/test:** `dotnet build cc-director.sln` -> Build succeeded, 0 Warning(s), 0 Error(s). `dotnet test` setup-engine -> 153/153 passed.

**Regression/CenCon:** Change confined to `CcDirector.Setup.Engine`; no architecture/security/DT-rule drift; no `docs/cencon/` update needed.

Proof: `docs/cencon/proof/issue-176/qa-report.html`. Merging to main on a clean build.

title: [Installer] Self-update test harness pollutes production installed.json - silent half-installs

body:
## Problem / Value

When the setup CLI installs the tray Gateway, `GatewayTrayInstaller.InstallAsync` starts the long-lived tray process with `UseShellExecute=false`, so the Gateway inherits the CLI's stdout/stderr handles. Any caller that **pipes** the CLI (`... | Select-Object`, `... | tail`) then hangs forever after the CLI exits, because the Gateway keeps the inherited pipe open for its whole lifetime. Hit live during the first v0.6.6 gateway install (a 90-minute phantom hang). The WPF wizard escapes this by tailing a `--log-file` instead of piping, but agents and scripts calling the CLI directly are blocked.

## Root cause

`tools/cc-director-setup-engine/GatewayTrayInstaller.cs` - the `ProcessStartInfo` in step 4 launches the tray with `UseShellExecute=false` (inherits stdio). `GatewayApp Program.cs` already fixed the identical problem in the self-update relaunch path with `UseShellExecute=true` and documents it in a comment - this path needs the same one-line fix.

## Scope

**IN**
- Set `UseShellExecute=true` (and ensure no `RedirectStandard*` is set) on the tray-launch `ProcessStartInfo` in `GatewayTrayInstaller.cs` step 4, matching the `GatewayApp` self-update precedent.
- Add/confirm a code comment referencing this issue and that precedent.

**OUT**
- The WPF wizard `--log-file` tailing path (already correct).
- Any other installer step or process launch.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Piping the setup CLI's gateway-install output (e.g. `... | Select-Object -First 5`, `... | tail`) returns to the prompt after the CLI exits instead of hanging. (Proof: terminal capture of the piped command completing.)
- [ ] The tray Gateway still launches and registers after install. (Proof: install log + `GET /healthz` returns 200.)
- [ ] The tray-launch `ProcessStartInfo` sets `UseShellExecute=true`. (Proof: the diff.)

## Affected Containers

- `CcDirector.Setup.Engine` - `tools/cc-director-setup-engine/GatewayTrayInstaller.cs`.

## Proof Target

`docs/cencon/proof/issue-175/`: a piped CLI gateway-install completing (not hanging) + the Gateway healthy (`/healthz` 200) after install.

## Assumptions

- Mirrors the proven `GatewayApp Program.cs` self-update fix; no detached-process side effects beyond what that path already established. None invented.

comments.body: Claimed by an implementation-loop run. Manual duplicate-prevention (the issue-level claim mechanism #298 is not built yet): removing flow:ready-dev so no concurrent loop selects this issue while it is being implemented. It will transition to flow:ready-qa (DEV done) then flow:done (merged) per the normal flow; if the run aborts, flow:ready-dev is restored.

## Developer Agent - ready for QA

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-175/report.html  (PR #302)

### Release Notes
Piped callers of the setup CLI's Gateway install no longer hang. The tray Gateway is launched with `UseShellExecute=true` so it does not inherit the CLI's stdio.

### Changes
- `GatewayTrayInstaller.cs` step 4: launch via new static seam `BuildTrayLaunchInfo` with `UseShellExecute=true` (no `RedirectStandard*`), mirroring the `GatewayApp` self-update relaunch precedent. Comment cites #175.
- `GatewayAndPinsTests.cs`: deterministic end-to-end test reproducing the inherited-handle hang and proving the fix, plus a config assertion.

### How to Test
`dotnet test tools/cc-director-setup-engine.Tests/CcDirector.Setup.Engine.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~GatewayTrayInstallerTests"`

### Expected Result
4/4 GatewayTrayInstaller tests pass; full setup-engine suite 147/147; solution builds clean (0 warnings, 0 errors, Debug + Release).

- [x] Piped CLI gateway-install returns instead of hanging -- `PipedCaller_ReturnsWithFix_AndWouldHangWithoutIt`.
- [x] Tray Gateway still launches/registers -- unchanged exe/args/working-dir/health-wait; `BuildTrayLaunchInfo_UsesShellExecute_NoRedirection`. Live fleet left healthy (7878/7470/7887 all 200).
- [x] Tray-launch `ProcessStartInfo` sets `UseShellExecute=true` -- the diff + assertion.

### Before / After
Before: `UseShellExecute=false` inherits caller stdio -> piped CLI hangs forever. After: `UseShellExecute=true` -> no inherited handle -> piped caller returns.

## QA Agent - PASS (independent verification)

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-175/qa-report.html (PR #302)

Verified the PR branch fresh, without reusing the Developer's binaries or screenshots.

- [x] **Piped CLI returns instead of hanging** - ran `PipedCaller_ReturnsWithFix_AndWouldHangWithoutIt` myself. It reproduces the hang at the OS-handle level (OLD `UseShellExecute=false` inherits an inheritable pipe write handle -> reader never reaches EOF within 3s, `oldReachedEof==false`) AND proves the fix (`BuildTrayLaunchInfo` -> EOF reached while child still alive, `newReachedEof==true`). PASS.
- [x] **Tray Gateway still launches/registers** - only the `UseShellExecute` flag changed inside `InstallAsync`; exe/args/working-dir/health-wait unchanged. Live fleet Gateway `GET /healthz` (7878) -> 200. PASS.
- [x] **`ProcessStartInfo` sets `UseShellExecute=true`** - confirmed in source (`GatewayTrayInstaller.cs`), no `RedirectStandard*`; asserted by `BuildTrayLaunchInfo_UsesShellExecute_NoRedirection`. PASS.

Precedent claim verified: `GatewayApp/Program.cs startGateway` independently confirmed to use `UseShellExecute=true` with the same rationale.

Regression: 4/4 targeted tests, full setup-engine suite 147/147, `dotnet build cc-director.sln` clean (0 warnings, 0 errors). Live fleet (7878/7470/7887) healthy and untouched.

VERIFIED - all acceptance criteria met. Merging to main.

title: [Installer] GatewayTrayInstaller inherits stdio - piped CLI callers hang forever

body:
﻿## What

The Python tools install step reports **"Installing 141/184 packages"** during setup (v0.6.5 wizard, python-tools component, ~334 MB bundle). 184 wheels for ~26 cc-* tools seems excessive.

## Ask

Audit the shared-venv wheelhouse and answer:

1. **Which top-level dependencies pull in the heaviest transitive trees?** (e.g. document converters, pandas/numpy stacks, browser automation)
2. **Are any deps only needed by optional paths** that could move to extras installed on demand (the way cc-vault already has a `[full]` extra)?
3. **Are there overlapping libraries doing the same job** across tools (multiple HTTP clients, multiple markdown parsers, etc.) that could be standardized to shrink the set?
4. Quick wins: deps declared but no longer imported anywhere.

## Why it matters

- Bigger bundle = slower install/update for every machine (the bundle re-downloads on every release until the hash-skip optimization from docs/architecture/VERSIONING.md lands)
- More packages = more supply-chain surface and more version-conflict risk in the shared venv

## Context

- The shared venv replaced 26 PyInstaller exes (~1GB) in v0.5.0, so 334 MB is already a big improvement - this is about the next pass.
- Wheelhouse is built by scripts/build-python-bundle.ps1 / .sh from the tools/ pyproject files.
- Not urgent; filed to track the investigation.

comments.body:
## Wheelhouse audit results

Measured against the actual wheelhouse build at `build/python-bundle` (the v0.6.x bundle contents): **184 wheels, 331.6 MB uncompressed** (24 of those are our own cc-* wheels). Method: parsed every wheel's `Requires-Dist` metadata, evaluated markers for the bundle target (win_amd64 / cp312), and computed the full dependency closure per top-level requirement in `thirdparty.in`, priced with real wheel sizes. "Unique" = wheels reachable from that requirement and from no other, i.e. exactly what disappears if it is removed.

### 1. Which top-level deps pull in the heaviest trees?

| Top-level dep | Used by | Closure | Unique wheels | Unique MB |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|
| crawl4ai | cc-crawl4ai | 95 pkgs / 185.3 MB | 46 | 72.3 |
| diagrams | cc-docgen | 14 pkgs / 43.4 MB | 10 | 43.1 |
| opencv-python | cc-video, cc-transcribe | 52.5 MB | 1 | 40.2 |
| google-api-python-client | cc-gmail | 20 pkgs / 21.6 MB | 6 | 16.3 |
| scikit-image | cc-video, cc-transcribe | 9 pkgs / 70.6 MB | 4 | 12.5 |
| pillow-heif | cc-photos | 12.6 MB | 1 | 5.5 |
| yt-dlp | cc-youtube-info | 3.3 MB | 1 | 3.3 |

**crawl4ai is the whale.** Its closure is 95 of the 160 third-party packages - more than half the package count of the entire bundle, for one tool. Standouts inside it:
- It depends on **both `playwright` AND `patchright`** (a 37.9 MB playwright fork) - two complete browser-automation stacks.
- `unclecode-litellm` (18.0 MB litellm fork) which drags in `tokenizers` + `huggingface-hub` + `hf-xet` (~7.5 MB of HuggingFace stack).
- `nltk` + `snowballstemmer` + `rank-bm25` (NLP), `alphashape` -> `shapely`/`trimesh`/`rtree` (computational geometry), `aiohttp`, `pyopenssl`, `brotli`, `lark`...
- Via `alphashape` it also reaches **scipy (36.6 MB)**, which is why scipy shows as "shared" rather than unique to scikit-image.

**diagrams (cc-docgen)** is 34.5 MB of bundled provider icon assets, plus an upstream packaging bug: `diagrams 0.25.x` declares **`pre-commit` as a runtime dependency**, which drags `pre-commit + virtualenv + distlib + nodeenv + identify + cfgv + platformdirs + python-discovery` (~8.4 MB of pure dev tooling) into every end-user install.

**google-api-python-client** is 15.3 MB because it ships static discovery JSON for every Google API; cc-gmail uses one of them. No slim official package exists; shrinking this means a thin REST client rewrite. Not worth it now.

### 2. What could move to on-demand extras?

The installer already installs per-tool dists from the wheelhouse via `tools-manifest.json`, so the natural unit is the tool:

| Scenario | Wheels removed | MB removed |
|---|---:|---:|
| cc-crawl4ai out of default bundle | -46 | -72.3 |
| cc-docgen (diagrams) out of default bundle | -10 | -43.1 |
| scikit-image dropped (see overlap below) | -4 | -12.5 |
| cc-crawl4ai + scikit-image together | -53 | **-123.5** (scipy, networkx finally drop - they are shared between exactly these two) |
| all three | **-66** | **-166.7** |

All three together takes the bundle from 184 wheels / 331.6 MB to **118 wheels / ~165 MB** - a 50% cut - while keeping every "daily driver" tool (pdf/html/word/excel/powerpoint, gmail/outlook, playwright, reddit/spotify, image/photos, voice/whisper/video/transcribe, vault, comm-queue) fully working.

### 3. Overlapping libraries doing the same job

- **playwright vs patchright** (~76 MB pair): patchright comes ONLY from crawl4ai; cc-playwright needs the real playwright. Removing/making cc-crawl4ai optional collapses this to one stack.
- **SSIM duplication**: scikit-image is imported for exactly ONE function - `skimage.metrics.structural_similarity` - in `cc-video/src/screenshots.py:10` and `cc-transcribe/src/screenshots.py:9`, while opencv is already a dependency of both tools. SSIM is ~15 lines of cv2/numpy (GaussianBlur formulation). Replacing it drops scikit-image + imageio + tifffile + lazy-loader, and (combined with the crawl4ai move) scipy + networkx.
- **HTTP clients**: requests (cc-image) vs httpx (cc-reddit, cc-spotify, cc-personresearch, openai) vs aiohttp (crawl4ai). Standardizing tool code on httpx is good hygiene but removes zero wheels - requests and aiohttp stay in transitively (O365, yt-dlp, google stack, crawl4ai).
- **Fuzzy matching**: cc-vault declares both jellyfish and rapidfuzz; both are genuinely imported (different algorithms). Fine as is.

### 4. Quick wins: declared but not imported (and one real bug)

- **BUG (fixed in this audit's working tree)**: `cc-vault/src/cli.py:1880` did `from thefuzz import fuzz` - `thefuzz` is neither declared nor in the wheelhouse, so `cc-vault contacts search <name> --company X` (name + filter combo) crashed with ModuleNotFoundError in the shared venv. Changed to `from rapidfuzz import fuzz` (already a declared dep; `fuzz.token_set_ratio` is API-compatible, verified).
- **pygments** is declared by cc-pdf, cc-html, cc-word but never imported by any tool source (highlighting goes through markdown-it without a pygments highlighter). Removing the declarations is hygiene only - rich requires pygments, so the wheel stays regardless.
- **linkify-it-py** looks unused by grep but IS required: cc_shared's parser uses the `MarkdownIt("gfm-like")` preset, which imports linkify-it lazily. Keep.
- **python-docx / python-pptx / pymupdf / openpyxl** appear twice in `thirdparty.in` (once from the doc tools, once from cc-vault[full]) - harmless, the resolver dedupes.
- **backports-tarfile** wheel is in the wheelhouse but unreachable by any closure on py3.12 (its marker is `python_version < "3.12"`); `pip download` grabbed it anyway. 0.03 MB - cosmetic.
- The **diagrams -> pre-commit chain** (8.4 MB) is an upstream packaging bug. Note: simply deleting those wheels breaks `pip install` metadata checks at install time, so the clean fixes are (a) move cc-docgen out of the default bundle, (b) pin/patch diagrams when upstream fixes it, or (c) a uv override - in that order of preference.

### Recommended sequence

1. Done now: thefuzz -> rapidfuzz fix in cc-vault.
2. Decide whether cc-crawl4ai and cc-docgen stay in the default bundle. This is 90% of the available win (-115 MB, -56 wheels) and is purely an installer/manifest change - the engine already installs per-tool from the manifest, so an "extras" tier (second zip or on-demand install) is the natural shape.
3. Replace the skimage SSIM call with a cv2 implementation in cc-video + cc-transcribe (-12.5 MB alone, -51 MB combined with step 2).
4. Leave google-api-python-client, pymupdf, opencv, pillow-heif as the cost of doing business.

Audit tooling: `build/python-bundle/audit_174.py` (offline, runs against any wheelhouse build) with full closure data in `audit_closures.json`.

## Step 2 implemented: cc-crawl4ai + cc-docgen moved to an on-demand EXTRAS tier

Built and live-verified locally (uncommitted). The default Python tools download drops from ~313 MB to **203.6 MB** (-110 MB / -35%), and from 184 to **121 wheels**.

### What changed

- **registry.json**: the two tools carry `"tier": "extras"`; everything else is core.
- **Build scripts** (ps1 + sh): both tiers still resolve from ONE combined lock (no version conflicts possible), then `scripts/split-python-wheelhouse.py` partitions the wheelhouse and the build emits a third asset per OS: `cc-tools-pyenv-extras-win-x64.zip` (108.9 MB, 63 wheels) / `cc-tools-pyenv-extras-macos-arm64.tar.gz`.
- **Engine**: `PythonToolsInstaller.InstallExtrasAsync` installs the extras tools into the EXISTING shared venv offline (pip resolves shared deps from what core already installed). Tracked as `python-tools-extras` in installed.json. A core bundle rebuild (every release update) wipes the venv - the installer removes the extras record at reset time (truthful state) and automatically reinstalls the extras tier from the same release afterwards. ToolUpdater needs no change (tiers version in lockstep).
- **CLI**: `cc-director-setup-cli install-extras` - idempotent; fails with the exact fix if no venv exists.
- **release.yml**: ships and REQUIRES the two extras assets (a missing extras asset would strand machines that have the tier installed).
- **Docs**: tools overview, cli-reference, installer handover updated.

### Partition correctness (the interesting bug)

First implementation used conservative marker handling (ignore platform/python-version markers) for the core closure. That attributed `importlib-metadata` + `zipp` to core - a core package references them only under `python_version < 3.x` - so pip never installed them, and the extras install failed unable to find them. The splitter now does REAL PEP 508 marker evaluation per target platform and moves exactly `closure(extras) - closure(core)`. The header of `split-python-wheelhouse.py` documents the lesson.

### Verification

- 111 engine tests + 9 CLI tests green, including 2 new fast gate tests (missing asset / missing venv).
- Live opt-in integration test (CC_PYBUNDLE_DIR against the real local build) PASSED in 5m17s, covering: core install with NO crawl4ai leak, extras install (scripts + shims + record), idempotent re-run, and a forced core rebuild that auto-restores the extras tier.

### Numbers

| Asset | Before | After |
|---|---:|---:|
| cc-tools-pyenv-win-x64.zip | ~313 MB / 184 wheels | 203.6 MB / 121 wheels |
| cc-tools-pyenv-extras-win-x64.zip | - | 108.9 MB / 63 wheels (on demand) |
| cc-python-win-x64.zip | 23 MB | 23 MB (unchanged) |

Remaining from the audit: step 3 (replace the single skimage SSIM call in cc-video/cc-transcribe with cv2) is now worth ~12.5 MB; scipy stays core either way until that lands.

## Step 3 implemented: skimage SSIM replaced with cv2 - core bundle down to 155.3 MB

Built and live-verified locally (uncommitted). With steps 2+3 together, the default Python tools download is now **155.3 MB / 114 wheels**, down from ~313 MB / 184 wheels at the start of this issue (**-51%**).

### What changed

- `cc-video/src/screenshots.py` + `cc-transcribe/src/screenshots.py`: the single `from skimage.metrics import structural_similarity` import is replaced by a ~25-line `ssim()` implemented with cv2 + numpy (boxFilter formulation matching skimage's defaults: uniform 7x7 window, unbiased covariance, border-cropped mean).
- `scikit-image` removed from both pyprojects (and from the legacy PyInstaller .spec files).
- One stale cc-transcribe test mock fixed (`create_audio_file` did not accept the `bitrate` kwarg production code passes - pre-existing, unrelated failure).

### Equivalence is exact, not approximate

Tested against `skimage.metrics.structural_similarity` on synthetic frames (identical, noise, small/large content change, natural gradients) and 5 random-frame trials through the actual changed modules: **max delta 3e-14** - floating-point noise. Zero disagreements at the 0.92 dedup threshold. Functional test: a synthetic 3-scene video through the real `extract_screenshots` yields exactly 3 screenshots at the scene boundaries.

### Bundle effect

| | Before step 3 | After |
|---|---:|---:|
| Lock | 158 third-party deps | 154 (scikit-image, imageio, tifffile, lazy-loader gone) |
| Core zip | 203.6 MB / 121 wheels | **155.3 MB / 114 wheels** |
| Extras zip | 108.9 MB / 63 wheels | 145.4 MB / 66 wheels |

scipy + networkx did not disappear from the release - they moved to the **extras** zip, because crawl4ai still reaches them via alphashape. That is the partition working as designed: only cc-crawl4ai users pay for scipy now. If crawl4ai ever drops alphashape upstream, they vanish entirely with no work on our side.

### Verification

- cc-video tests 24/24, cc-transcribe tests 33/33 (in a fresh venv built offline from the new bundle - proves no hidden skimage dependency)
- Live opt-in install integration test PASSED (7m18s): core install, extras install (scipy now arriving from the extras wheelhouse), idempotent re-run, forced rebuild with automatic extras restore

### Issue scorecard

| Audit recommendation | Status |
|---|---|
| 1. thefuzz bug fix | DONE (2d95d0b) |
| 2. extras tier for cc-crawl4ai + cc-docgen | DONE (2d95d0b) |
| 3. skimage -> cv2 SSIM | DONE (this change) |
| 4. accept google-api-python-client / pymupdf / opencv / pillow-heif | accepted as cost of doing business |

Default download at the start: ~336 MB (python + tools). Now: **~178 MB**. The remaining big rocks (opencv 40 MB, pymupdf 19 MB, google discovery JSON 15 MB) are all genuinely used.

All four audit recommendations are done and shipped: (1) cc-vault thefuzz crash fixed + (2) extras tier for cc-crawl4ai/cc-docgen in 2d95d0b, (3) skimage -> cv2 SSIM in bb1b91d, (4) remaining heavy deps accepted as genuinely used. Default Python tools bundle: ~313 MB / 184 wheels -> 155.3 MB / 114 wheels (-51%). Extras install on demand via 'cc-director-setup-cli install-extras' and auto-restore after every core rebuild. Full reports in the comments above.

title: Python tools bundle installs 184 packages - audit and reduce the wheelhouse

body:
## Problem / Value

A long-lived Director's log goes silent after midnight. Observed on SORENLAPTOP: `director-2026-06-04-6524.log` = 727 KB (last write 23:59:57); `director-2026-06-05-6524.log` created 00:00:09 at **0 bytes**, never written again across 12+ busy hours. Day-2+ Director processes therefore produce **no diagnostics at all** - the norm on long-running machines, and it crippled the #170 investigation (which only worked because the prior day's log happened to hold the evidence).

## Root cause area

`src/CcDirector.Core/Utilities/FileLog.cs`, `WriterLoop`: the day rollover is handled inside the dequeue loop (`if (today != currentDate)` -> dispose old writer, create `new StreamWriter(path, append:true){ AutoFlush=false }`). The new file is created (hence the 0-byte file) but subsequent writes never land. Two suspects the Developer must confirm: (a) an exception thrown in `WriterLoop` after the rollover terminates the background writer thread (the entire `foreach` is inside one `try`), so all logging stops; (b) `AutoFlush=false` combined with "flush only when `_queue.Count == 0`" never flushes while a busy Director keeps the queue non-empty.

## Scope

**IN**
- Writes after a date rollover reliably land in the new day's file for the life of the process.
- The writer thread must not die silently on a transient write/rollover exception - log and continue, never exit the loop.
- Make the rollover decision unit-testable (e.g. an injectable clock or extracting the date/path logic).

**OUT**
- Changing the log file naming scheme, directory, or line format.
- Gateway/Cockpit logging (Cockpit logging is #199).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After a simulated date rollover, a subsequent `FileLog.Write` lands in the new day's file (non-zero, contains the line). (Proof: a unit/integration test driving the rollover via the injectable clock, asserting the new file's content.)
- [ ] A transient exception in the write path does not terminate the writer thread; logging continues afterward. (Proof: a test that forces one write to throw and asserts the next write still lands.)
- [ ] Writes flush to disk within a bounded interval even while the queue stays non-empty (no indefinite buffering). (Proof: test or a documented periodic-flush trigger.)

## Affected Containers

- `CcDirector.Core` - `Utilities/FileLog.cs`; `CcDirector.Core.Tests`.

## Proof Target

`docs/cencon/proof/issue-171/`: green rollover + exception-resilience test output, plus a one-line note of the confirmed root cause.

## Assumptions

- Rollover happens at local midnight per `DateTime.Now` (existing behavior, kept). Adding a clock seam to make `WriterLoop` testable is acceptable (developer's call). None invented.

comments.body:
## Developer Agent - Ready for QA

Implemented and proven. PR #277 (branch `issue-171-filelog-rollover`).

**Confirmed root cause:** both suspects in the issue. (a) The whole consuming `foreach` was inside one `try`, so any exception after the day rollover permanently killed the writer thread - all logging stopped, new day's file stayed 0 bytes. (b) `AutoFlush=false` + flush-only-when-`_queue.Count==0` never flushed on a busy long-running Director.

### Release Notes
A long-lived Director's log no longer goes silent after midnight.

### Changes
- `src/CcDirector.Core/Utilities/FileLogWriter.cs` (new, internal): rollover/flush engine with injectable clock; per-line try/catch keeps the thread alive (logged, not swallowed); bounded 1s periodic flush; `FileShare.Read` so live viewers can read.
- `src/CcDirector.Core/Utilities/FileLog.cs`: thin static facade, public API unchanged.
- `src/CcDirector.Core.Tests/Utilities/FileLogWriterTests.cs` (new): 4 regression tests.

### How to Test
`dotnet test src/CcDirector.Core.Tests --filter FileLogWriterTests`

### Expected Result
4/4 green; `dotnet build cc-director.sln` clean (0/0). Also verified live on a slot-5 Director: its log grew 0 -> 16,684 bytes and flushed under startup load.

### Before / After
Before: new day's file created at 0 bytes, never written. After: post-rollover writes land; one bad write no longer kills the thread; bounded flush.

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-171/report.html  (PR #277)

## QA Agent - VERIFIED (PASS)

Independently verified in a fresh context (did not reuse the Developer Agent's binary, output, or screenshots).

**Independent QA run:** `dotnet test src/CcDirector.Core.Tests --filter FileLogWriterTests` -> Passed! 4 Passed, 0 Failed.
**Full solution build:** `dotnet build cc-director.sln` -> Build succeeded, 0 Warning(s), 0 Error(s).

### Acceptance criteria (3/3 + supporting)
1. PASS - rollover writes land in the new day's file (non-zero) -- `Write_AfterDateRollover_LandsInNewDaysFile`.
2. PASS - transient write exception does not kill the writer thread; logging continues -- `Write_WhenWritePathThrowsOnce_WriterSurvivesAndKeepsLogging`.
3. PASS - bounded flush while the queue stays non-empty -- `Write_WhileQueueStaysBusy_FlushesWithinBoundedInterval`.
(+ `ComputeLogPath_UsesDateAndProcessId`.)

Each test is non-vacuous: diffed against the pre-fix code (`ccbc879:.../FileLog.cs`), which had a hardcoded clock, a single `try` around the whole `foreach`, and a flush-only-when-`Count==0` path -- the three confirmed root causes. Public API unchanged; out-of-scope (naming/dir/format, Gateway/Cockpit) untouched; no DT security rule affected.

Proof: docs/cencon/proof/issue-171/qa-report.html

VERIFIED - all acceptance criteria met.

title: [Logging] FileLog midnight rollover loses all writes (new day's file stays 0 bytes)

body:
## Symptom

In the Cockpit, one of the Directors' sessions renders **yellow**, meaning the Cockpit considers that Director not connected / not reachable through the Gateway (screenshot taken 2026-06-05 06:55, Soren's machine, several Directors running).

## Questions to answer (research, not fix)

1. How exactly does a Director announce itself to the Gateway — file-based discovery vs HTTP register — and which path was in play here?
2. What is the heartbeat contract (interval, timeout, who sweeps stale entries, what happens on 410)?
3. How does the Gateway decide a Director is unreachable (probe failures, circuit breaker, eviction), and how does that state flow to the Cockpit?
4. What does the Cockpit's yellow state actually key off (registry staleness? machineErrors envelope? failed direct WebSocket?)?
5. Root cause for the observed yellow: heartbeat dying, tailnet endpoint unreachable (Tailscale Serve mapping?), port mismatch, or Cockpit-side staleness?

## Outcome

A written explanation of the Director->Gateway->Cockpit liveness pipeline plus the concrete root cause for the yellow state, with log evidence. Fix to be scoped separately.

comments.body:
## Root cause found (research only - no fix applied)

**TL;DR: The heartbeat is NOT the problem. The Gateway evicts the laptop's Director every ~3.5 minutes because it cannot probe the Director's advertised HTTPS endpoint - the Tailscale Serve mapping that endpoint depends on is supposed to be provisioned by the Gateway's `TailscaleServeProvisioner`, but `tailscale serve` only configures the LOCAL node, so it can never provision a mapping on a remote Director's machine. Cross-machine Directors are unreachable by design-gap, loop register->evict->410->re-register forever, and the Cockpit renders them as the amber/yellow "unreachable" row.**

### How the liveness pipeline works

**Director -> Gateway (announce + heartbeat)** - `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/GatewayClient.cs`
1. Two ingress paths into `DirectorRegistry`: (a) filesystem watch on `%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\config\director\instances\` for SAME-machine Directors, (b) HTTP `POST /directors/register` + heartbeat every **15 s** + DELETE on stop, for Directors with `gateway.url` configured (cross-machine).
2. The Director advertises `TailnetEndpoint` from `ResolveTailnetEndpoint()`: MagicDNS always wins -> `https://<magicdns>:<own port>`; an explicit `gateway.tailnetEndpoint` override is honored only when there is no Tailscale identity; with neither it stays local-only.
3. On heartbeat `410 Gone` the client re-registers automatically.

**Gateway side (liveness verdicts)** - `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Discovery/DirectorRegistry.cs`
- HTTP entries are swept if no heartbeat for **60 s** (`HttpHeartbeatTimeout`).
- Separately, the fleet aggregator (`GET /sessions`) PROBES each Director's advertised endpoint. **3 consecutive probe failures** open a circuit (`MaxConsecutiveFailures`) -> 30 s cooldown (`UnreachableCooldown`) -> the Director is skipped and surfaced in the `machineErrors` envelope.
- A Director continuously unreachable for **3 min** (`UnreachableEvictAfter`) is **evicted even if still heartbeating**.

**Cockpit** - `src/CcDirector.Cockpit/Components/SessionRail.razor`, `Cockpit.razor`
- Polls `GET /sessions?envelope=true` every **2 s** and renders `machineErrors` as the amber "director ... unreachable" row. Session dot colors come verbatim from each Director's Wingman; the Cockpit decides nothing itself.

### Evidence from the live system (2026-06-05)

Laptop Director (PID 6524, v0.6.3, port 7879 loopback-only) -> Gateway on `soren-north:7878`:

1. `director-2026-06-04-6524.log` shows the loop, repeating every ~3.5 min for hours (= 3 min `UnreachableEvictAfter` + sweep cadence):
```
21:48:30 [GatewayClient] Heartbeat returned 410 Gone, re-registering
21:48:30 [GatewayClient] POST /directors/register: endpoint=https://sorenlaptop.taildb08ed.ts.net:7879
21:48:30 [GatewayClient] Registered: status=201
21:52:00 [GatewayClient] Heartbeat returned 410 Gone, re-registering   <- ~3.5 min later, forever
```
2. Gateway is healthy and reachable from the laptop: `GET /healthz` -> `{"status":"ok","directors":3,"sessions":5}`. So heartbeats flow fine.
3. The advertised endpoint is dead: `https://sorenlaptop.taildb08ed.ts.net:7879/healthz` -> connection refused.
4. `tailscale serve status` on the laptop shows mappings only for stale high ports (49968, 52481, 49908, ...) - **nothing forwards 7879**. The Director binds loopback only (by policy), so without a Serve mapping the advertised URL can never work.
5. `TailscaleServeProvisioner` (`src/CcDirector.Gateway/Tailscale/`) shells `tailscale serve --bg --https=<port> http://localhost:<port>` - on the **Gateway's own node**. `http://localhost:<port>` on soren-north is not the laptop's Director. Nothing on the Director side provisions Serve (grep confirms: only comments reference it).

### The design gap, stated plainly

The architecture says: Directors bind loopback; remote reachability comes exclusively from Tailscale Serve, "auto-provisioned per Director by the Gateway's TailscaleServeProvisioner" (ControlApiHost.cs:23-24). That provisioning model only works for Directors co-located with the Gateway. For any cross-machine Director the advertised endpoint is structurally unreachable, the probe circuit opens permanently, and the eviction loop + Cockpit amber row follow.

### Candidate fix directions (for scoping later, NOT implemented)

- The **Director provisions its own** `tailscale serve --bg --https=<port>` mapping on startup (it owns its node and already shells `tailscale status`); Gateway provisioner keeps handling its co-located Directors / reconciling orphans.
- And/or: the registry should not evict a Director whose **heartbeats are healthy** just because fleet probes fail - surface "control-plane up / data-plane unreachable" as a distinct state instead of the register/evict churn.
- Clean up the laptop's stale high-port Serve mappings (orphans from earlier runs) once ownership is decided.

### Secondary discovery (separate issue worth filing)

`director-2026-06-05-6524.log` is **0 bytes** while the Director has been busy all day - FileLog's midnight rollover appears to lose all subsequent writes (yesterday's file stops at 23:59:57, today's was created 12:00:09 AM and never written). Filed separately.

**Linked to epic #279 (Remote-first Cockpit reliability) as a diagnostic input.**

DoR note: this issue is written as **research, not a fix** ('Fix to be scoped separately', outcome = 'a written explanation + concrete root cause'). It therefore cannot be `flow:ready-dev` as a fix - it has no fix acceptance criteria by design. Part of the yellow ('not connected') symptom is plausibly the same loopback/endpoint-advertisement mechanism proven in #268; routing the per-session legs through the Gateway (#268) and persisting Cockpit logs (#199) will remove two of the variables here. Recommend running the #170 research AFTER #268 + #199 land, then scoping any remaining liveness fix as its own `flow:ready-dev` issue. (See also the gateway-liveness pipeline notes referenced for #170/#171.)

## Closing as delivered: research complete, root cause since fixed, symptom verified gone (2026-06-11)

This issue was scoped as research-only ("written explanation + concrete root cause; fix to be scoped separately"). That outcome was delivered in full in the 2026-06-05 comment above: the Director->Gateway->Cockpit liveness pipeline writeup plus the concrete root cause (the Gateway's TailscaleServeProvisioner can only provision its OWN node, so every cross-machine Director advertised a structurally dead endpoint and looped register->evict->410->re-register).

Both fix directions the research proposed have since shipped:
- #197: the Director self-provisions its own `tailscale serve` mapping on startup (startup/self-heal/shutdown), removing the design gap.
- #223/#224: two-way Gateway handshake - GatewayConnectionMonitor is the single source of connection truth, replacing probe-churn guesswork.

Live verification today (Gateway on SOREN_NORTH, `GET /directors`): both real Directors are registered, reachable, and two-way verified within minutes - SOREN_NORTH 37cea6e2 (file source, twoWayVerifiedAt 2026-06-11T10:04:47Z) and SORENLAPTOP a0d9f4a3 (http source, twoWayVerifiedAt 2026-06-11T10:04:13Z). The original yellow symptom is gone.

Note: amber "unreachable" rows CAN still appear today from a different, newly-found cause - Gateway.Tests runs writing real instance files - which is filed separately (see the issue referencing this comment). That is a test-pollution bug, not this liveness-pipeline gap.

title: [cockpit] Director shows yellow (not connected) in Cockpit - root-cause the Director->Gateway connection lifecycle

body:
## Request

From the Gateway Cockpit, you should be able to kill a session directly from the session rail - in both the **Tree** view and the **Triage** view - without having to select the session and hunt for the Kill button in the detail header.

## Current state

- Kill exists only in the selected-session detail header (top-right actions: What's happening / Handover / Rename / Kill, with an arm -> "Confirm kill" flow) - `src/CcDirector.Cockpit/Components/Pages/Cockpit.razor` (~lines 125-132, `KillSelected`).
- The left rail session rows (Tree mode and Triage mode, `_viewMode = "tree" | "triage"`) have no kill action at all.
- `DirectorClient` already supports the operation: `DELETE /sessions/{sid}` on the owning Director (`src/CcDirector.Cockpit/Services/DirectorClient.cs:173`).

## Proposed behavior

- Add a kill affordance on each session row in the rail (e.g. hover/context action), available in both Tree and Triage views.
- Keep the two-step confirm pattern (arm -> confirm) that the detail header uses, so a stray click cannot kill a session.
- Should work for sessions on any reachable Director, same as the existing header Kill.

## Notes

- Reuse the existing `KillSelected` / `DirectorClient` path; this is a UI-surface addition, not a new API.
- Triage view is where sessions pile up when several need attention, so killing stale/stuck ones from there is the main workflow driver.

comments.body: Implemented in commit bf3ee69. SessionRail rows (shared by Tree and Triage views) have a two-step Close Session -> Confirm close kebab action; Cockpit.razor 'close' handler calls DeleteSessionAsync (DELETE /sessions/{sid}). Both views covered. Verified against code. Closing.

title: Cockpit: kill session from the rail (Tree view and Triage view)

body:
## Problem

The Cockpit awareness panel ("What's happening") is built and consumes the Director's REST surface correctly, but the data generation behind it is broken on the Director side:

- `POST /sessions/{sid}/recap` runs `claude --print` which exits with code 1 and EMPTY stderr, so no recap is ever generated.
- Turn-summaries come back empty on live sessions.
- The Cockpit handles the failure gracefully (cached-recap / git / turn-summary reads work), so this is purely a Director/Core bug, not a Cockpit bug.

## History

- Found during Cockpit Phase 3 (2026-05-31) and re-confirmed by the autonomous Cockpit QA loop the same day. Flagged for the Director side both times; never fixed.
- Reports: docs/features/cockpit-mvp/QA_REPORT.html and docs/features/cockpit-qa-loop/SUMMARY.html.

## Likely direction

The recap generator shells `claude --print`. A child claude.exe spawned from inside a Director's process tree is known to be sensitive to stdio/ConPTY context (see CLAUDE.md section on nested ConPty: "Input must be provided either through stdin or as a prompt argument when using --print"). Diagnosis should start by capturing the exact command line, working dir, and stdio wiring RecapGenerator uses, and reproducing the exit-1 outside the Director.

## Acceptance

- `POST /sessions/{sid}/recap` returns a fresh recap for a live session.
- Turn-summaries populate on live sessions.
- Cockpit awareness panel shows recap + turn summaries end-to-end without any Cockpit changes.

comments.body: Fixed in 209a509. Root cause: RecapGenerator passed --bare to the side-claude spawn; --bare disables keychain reads so the side-call could not authenticate and died with 'Not logged in' (on stdout, empty stderr - invisible in logs). Removed --bare, error reporting now includes stdout, and a build-failing audit test keeps --bare out of Core. Verified E2E: POST /recap returns a real recap and the Cockpit What's-happening panel renders it. Live Directors pick this up on their next relaunch with a new build.

title: Recap generation fails Director-side (claude --print exits 1) - Cockpit awareness panel has no data

body:
## Symptom
On a clean Workstation install (v0.3.9), the Director log shows on every startup:

`[CcDirector] [RunnersConfig] Skipping 'linkedin-connect': arg 'scripts/linkedin-connect-from-queue.py' looks like a relative file path but was not found near C:\Users\<u>\AppData\Local\cc-director\app\`

## Root cause
A default runner config ships with a **repo-relative** script path (`scripts/linkedin-connect-from-queue.py`). The `scripts/` directory is not part of the install, so the path never resolves on an installed machine and the runner is silently disabled.

## Impact
Minor: the 'linkedin-connect' runner is unavailable on every installed machine (works only from a dev clone). Silent (only in the log).

## Proposed fix
Either ship the referenced script with the install and resolve it relative to the install root, or drop the repo-only runner from the default config that ships to end users.

## Evidence
Other-machine clean install, director-2026-06-02-*.log.

labels.name: installation
labels.description: Install / uninstall / auto-update findings from the test-machine agent

title: [defect] Shipped runner config references a repo-relative script not present after install (linkedin-connect skipped)

body:
## Symptom
After install, the Complete step's launch fails to find the Director. Log:

`[CompleteStep] cc-director.exe not found at C:\Users\<u>\AppData\Local\cc-director\bin\cc-director.exe`

## Root cause
`CompleteStep.LaunchButton_Click` builds the path as `_installPath\cc-director.exe`, where `_installPath` is the **bin** dir. The Director installs to the **app** dir (`%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\app\cc-director.exe`, per InstallLayout).

## Proposed fix
Point the Complete step at the canonical Director path (the app dir / `EngineInstallRunner.AppExePath`), not the bin dir.

## Files
- tools/cc-director-setup/Steps/CompleteStep.xaml.cs
- tools/cc-director-setup/MainWindow.xaml.cs (what it passes as install path)

labels.name: installation
labels.description: Install / uninstall / auto-update findings from the test-machine agent

title: [defect] Complete step launches Director from bin\ but it installs to app\

body:
## Symptom
The wizard's Skills step shows **0/1 installed, cc-director Failed**. Log:

`[SkillInstaller] DownloadSkillFileAsync FAILED skills/cc-director/SKILL.md: Response status code does not indicate success: 404 (Not Found).`

## Root cause
`SkillInstaller` fetches `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thefrederiksen/cc-director/main/skills/cc-director/SKILL.md`, but **the repo has no `skills/` directory** - the cc-director skill is not tracked there (it exists only locally at `~/.claude/skills/cc-director/SKILL.md`). The repo's other skills live under `.claude/skills/`, not `skills/`, so the path is wrong twice over.

## Proposed fix
Add the cc-director skill to the repo at a path the installer fetches (e.g. `skills/cc-director/SKILL.md`), sourced from the canonical SKILL.md (PII-review before committing - public repo). Make a missing skill non-fatal in the UI as a secondary hardening.

## Files
- tools/cc-director-setup/Services/SkillInstaller.cs
- (new) skills/cc-director/SKILL.md

labels.name: installation
labels.description: Install / uninstall / auto-update findings from the test-machine agent

title: [defect] Skill install 404: skills/cc-director/SKILL.md is not in the repo

body:
## Symptom
Installing/re-installing the **Gateway** role on a machine where a Gateway+Cockpit are already running fails with:

`Gateway service FAILED: Cockpit extraction failed: Access to the path 'cc-director-cockpit.dll' is denied.`

(Reproduced on two machines, v0.3.9.)

## Root cause
`GatewayServiceInstaller.InstallAsync` extracts the Cockpit zip (`CockpitPackage.ExtractAsync`, which deletes + re-extracts `CockpitDir`) **before** it stops the existing `cc-gateway-service`. The still-running Cockpit child holds `cc-director-cockpit.dll` open, so the delete/extract is denied.

## Impact
Gateway re-install / migration over a running install cannot succeed. (Fails safely - it bails before deleting the old service, so the running Gateway survives.)

## Proposed fix
In `GatewayServiceInstaller.InstallAsync`, stop the existing service (and kill any orphan `cc-director-cockpit` process) **before** extracting the Cockpit. If extraction then fails, restart the previous service so the machine is not left down.

## Files
- tools/cc-director-setup-engine/GatewayServiceInstaller.cs
- tools/cc-director-setup-engine/CockpitPackage.cs

Found via setup log %LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\logs\setup\setup-*.log + %TEMP%\cc-gateway-install-*.log.

labels.name: installation
labels.description: Install / uninstall / auto-update findings from the test-machine agent

title: [defect] Gateway install fails: Cockpit extracted before service is stopped (locked cc-director-cockpit.dll)

Captured from a design voice note (transcript 2026-05-28 06:17). Reworks the voice/Wingman experience.

## Auto-run Wingman
When a session finishes its turn and goes to "waiting for user", automatically run the Wingman ("what is happening?") instead of requiring a manual trigger. This has a per-run cost, but it is judged worth it for now.

## Per-session Wingman toggle
- Option to turn Wingman on/off per session.
- Wingman ON: the Wingman tab and Voice tab are available.
- Wingman OFF: only the Terminal tab is available.
- Wingman ON by default.

## Color states
- Blue = working.
- Yellow = turn finished, Wingman is translating / running.
- Red = needs the user.
- With Wingman on, a finished turn goes yellow while Wingman runs, then turns red once the Wingman summary is ready.
@

comments.body: Implemented (commits incl. d9f19c1, a93a774). Auto-run on idle via ProactiveExplainService regenerating the briefing on the WaitingForInput transition for WingmanEnabled sessions; per-session Session.WingmanEnabled (persisted) with NewSessionDialog checkbox and tab-hiding when off (terminal-only); color states in SessionStatusWingman/StatusColor (blue working / yellow wingman-reading / red needs-you). Verified against code. Closing.

title: Wingman: auto-run when a session goes idle + per-session toggle + voice-tab color states

body:
@
Captured from a recorder test recording (transcript 2026-05-23 14:35).

## Symptom
The CC Recorder app keeps showing "uploading in the background" and does not appear to actually complete the upload.

## To verify / fix
- Do uploads actually continue while the app is backgrounded?
- When the phone screen times out or the device is locked, does the app keep running and finish the upload?

## Acceptance
- Uploads complete reliably while the app is backgrounded and across screen-lock / screen-off.
- The UI accurately reflects upload progress and completion (no permanent "uploading in background").
@

comments.body: Implemented in commits 09964ce / 10a0a30. Android UploadWorker (WorkManager) drains the upload queue even when the app is backgrounded/closed, with NetworkType.Connected constraint, retry-with-backoff, and a persistent queue (UploadScheduler) - the canonical mechanism for uploads surviving screen-lock. Upload decoupled from transcription with per-row progress. Note: the locked-phone two-checkmarks UI was not visually re-confirmed, but the code path satisfies the criteria. Closing; reopen if the locked-screen UX proves otherwise.

title: CC Recorder: uploads stall in background / unclear if they continue when screen locks

body:
## Summary

Polish the desktop Dictate dialog (`SpeakDialog`) into a comfortable **transcribe -> review -> edit -> send** loop. The transcribe-mid-session mechanic **already exists** behind the pause button - keep it. What's missing: the transcript isn't **editable**, the dialog is needlessly **small/non-resizable**, the scrollbar **auto-hides**, and the action buttons could be laid out better.

## Background - what already works (keep it)

- The `||` **pause** button (`PauseButton`) already stops the mic, transcribes what's been said so far, shows it in a `PAUSED` stage, and **Resume** appends more speech. We're keeping this mechanic as-is - no separate "Transcribe now" concept.
- The **lock-out during transcription already works**: `SwitchToTranscribing()` disables Send, Insert, the mic selector, and pause until transcription completes (`SwitchToPaused()` re-enables). Verified - no change needed.

## Changes wanted

1. **Editable transcript.** The transcript is currently a read-only `TextBlock` (`TranscriptText`). Make it an editable `TextBox` so the user can fix mis-heard words in the paused/review stage before committing. (Read-only while actively recording is fine; editable when paused.)
2. **Bigger, resizable dialog.** It's fixed at `540x416` with `CanResize="False"`. Make it **permanently bigger** and **resizable** - it's a modal that blocks everything, so there's no reason to keep it small. (This resolves the earlier "auto-grow vs always-big" question: go always-big.)
3. **Stop the auto-hiding scrollbar.** The transcript `ScrollViewer` uses Avalonia's overlay scrollbar that collapses/reappears. Set `AllowAutoHide="False"` (and reserve its space) so when there's overflow the scrollbar simply stays visible.
4. **Re-lay the action bar: controls left, commit right.** Move **Cancel** and **Pause/Resume** to the **left**; keep **Insert** and **Send** on the **right**. Splits "control the recording / back out" from "commit", and puts distance between Pause and Send to avoid mis-clicks.
5. **Resume appends to edited text.** `Resume` must append new speech to whatever is currently in the box, **including the user's edits** - re-seed `_accumulatedText` from the edited `TextBox` on resume so edits are never overwritten.
6. **Insert / Send act on the reviewed text.** Both operate on the (possibly edited) transcript - `Insert` drops it at the caret without submitting; `Send` auto-submits.

## Screen layout

**State 1 - Recording (compact; Cancel/Pause now on the left):**

```
+--------------------------------------------------------------+
| CC  Dictate                                    _   []    X   |
+--------------------------------------------------------------+
|  Mic [ Default - Microphone (FDUCE SL40 Audio Device)    v ] |
|                                                              |
|  RECORDING                                          0:02.2   |
|              | | | | | | | | | | |                          |
|              . . . . . . . . . . .                          |
|                                                              |
|  +--------------------------------------------------------+  |
|  | (your words will appear here)                          |  |
|  |                                                        |  |
|  +--------------------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                              |
|   [ Cancel ]  [ || Pause ]              [ Insert ]  [ Send ] |
+--------------------------------------------------------------+
```

**State 2 - Paused / Review (bigger dialog; text filled and EDITABLE; scrollbar shown if needed):**

```
+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| CC  Dictate                                        _    []     X     |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  Mic [ Default - Microphone (FDUCE SL40 Audio Device)            v ] |
|                                                                      |
|  PAUSED - reviewing                                         0:02.2   |
|              (meter idle / greyed)                                   |
|                                                                      |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+ ^ +  |
|  | We need to fix the desktop transcription tool. You can see | | |  |
|  | where it says your words will appear here, but they never  | # |  |
|  | do, because we only transcribe at the end. We have room on | # |  |
|  | this screen, so the pause already transcribes what we have | | |  |
|  | so far - we just want to edit it and have more room.|      | | |  |
|  |                                                            | v |  |
|  |   (editable - click to fix any mis-heard words)            |   |  |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+---+  |
|                                                                      |
|   [ Cancel ]  [ Resume ]                  [ Insert ]      [ Send ]   |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

Differences in State 2: status flips `RECORDING` -> `PAUSED - reviewing`, meter idles, the dialog is bigger, the transcript is editable (note the caret `|`), the scrollbar stays put when content overflows, and the middle-left button is **`Resume`** (appends to the edited text).

## Affected code

- `src/CcDirector.Avalonia/Voice/SpeakDialog.axaml` - swap transcript `TextBlock` -> editable `TextBox` (read-only when recording); enlarge window + `CanResize="True"`; `ScrollViewer AllowAutoHide="False"`; reorder action bar (Cancel + Pause left, Insert + Send right).
- `src/CcDirector.Avalonia/Voice/SpeakDialog.axaml.cs` - on Resume re-seed `_accumulatedText` from the edited text box; toggle the transcript box read-only/editable per stage; `Insert`/`Send` read the edited text.

## Acceptance criteria

- Transcript is editable in the paused/review stage; edits survive a Resume (new speech appends, edits not overwritten).
- Dialog is larger and user-resizable.
- Scrollbar stays visible on overflow (no auto-hide/collapse).
- Action bar: Cancel + Pause/Resume on the left, Insert + Send on the right.
- Existing lock-out during transcription is preserved (no Resume/Insert/Send mid-transcribe).
- Insert and Send both operate on the reviewed/edited text.

## Note

Scope is the **desktop** dialog (`SpeakDialog`). Web (`dictate-client.js`) and Android dictation are out of scope; parity later = separate issues (Android remains the master for voice per project convention).

comments.body: Implemented in commit a43a6eb. SpeakDialog: 640x560 resizable (CanResize), editable transcript TextBox (read-only by default, editable when paused, re-seeds accumulated text on resume), ScrollViewer.AllowAutoHide=False, action bar Cancel+Pause left / Insert+Send right. All six acceptance criteria met. Verified against code. Closing.

title: Desktop Dictate: editable transcript, bigger resizable dialog, fixed scrollbar, controls-left action bar

body:
## Summary

The terminal font in the **Cockpit** (Blazor web UI) is much harder to read than the font in the **desktop** Avalonia terminal. In side-by-side use the Cockpit terminal text is nearly unreadable. We need to bring the Cockpit terminal's readability up to parity with the desktop terminal.

Reported by Soren after testing the Cockpit terminal pane against the desktop terminal.

## Concrete differences found

**Desktop terminal** -- `src/CcDirector.Terminal.Avalonia/TerminalFonts.cs:20`
```
Cascadia Mono, Consolas, Menlo, DejaVu Sans Mono, Courier New
```
Rendered natively by Avalonia.

**Cockpit terminal** -- `src/CcDirector.Cockpit/wwwroot/js/cockpit-terminal.js:18-19`
```js
fontFamily: '"Cascadia Code", Consolas, "Courier New", monospace',
fontSize: 13, lineHeight: 1.0, ...
```
Rendered through xterm.js with the `CanvasAddon` (line 26-27).

## Likely contributing factors (to investigate when we pick this up)

1. **Cascadia Code vs Cascadia Mono.** Desktop uses Cascadia *Mono*; Cockpit uses Cascadia *Code* (which carries programming ligatures and a slightly different glyph weight). Matching the desktop's Mono chain is the obvious first step.
2. **`lineHeight: 1.0`** is very tight and hurts legibility; the rows crowd together.
3. **CanvasAddon renderer.** The xterm.js canvas renderer is often blurrier than the DOM or WebGL renderer at small font sizes on high-DPI displays. Worth comparing canvas vs WebGL vs DOM renderer.
4. **`fontSize: 13`** may be too small for the web rendering path; the desktop may render an effectively larger/crisper glyph.

## Suggested direction (not committing to it yet)

- Switch the Cockpit `fontFamily` to match the desktop chain (`Cascadia Mono, Consolas, Menlo, DejaVu Sans Mono, Courier New`).
- Bump `lineHeight` (e.g. ~1.2) and re-evaluate `fontSize`.
- Try the WebGL renderer (or DOM renderer) instead of CanvasAddon and compare crispness.

## Notes

- This is a readability/visual-quality bug, not a functional one -- the terminal works, it's just hard to read.
- Defer: filing now, to be fixed later.

comments.body: Fixed in commit c4e299d: Cockpit terminal now uses the desktop's font chain (Cascadia Mono first, same OS fallbacks), 14px, lineHeight 1.2 (renderer unchanged). Verified live side-by-side against the old build - rows breathe and the same content reads more comfortably. If canvas blur is still an issue on high-DPI later, vendoring the WebGL renderer is the follow-up lever.

title: Cockpit terminal font is hard to read vs desktop terminal

body:
## Summary

Rework the Cockpit's left panel into a **collapsible** session navigator with a **view-mode toggle** at the top. It supports two layouts for now - a Machine/Director **tree** (default) and a flat **triage** list (needs-you first, on-hold last) - and lets you **start a new session** directly from the panel. Built so more view modes and session reordering can be layered on later.

## Motivation

Today the Cockpit left rail (`SessionRail.razor`) groups Machine -> Director -> sessions but:
- It's always visible and can't be collapsed, so it eats horizontal space once you're driving a single session.
- It has exactly one layout. There's no way to switch to a "what needs me right now" view.
- Sessions are sorted alphabetically (`s.Name ?? s.RepoPath`), so they jump around as names/states change. The desktop preserves a stable, user-controllable order.
- There's no way to start a new session from the rail. New sessions only come from the global "+ New session" button in the topbar, which then makes you pick a Director from a dropdown.

## Proposed behaviour

### 1. Collapsible panel
- The left panel can be collapsed (and re-expanded), so once you're focused on one session you can reclaim the horizontal space for the terminal/detail pane.
- Collapse/expand control is on the panel itself (e.g. a chevron). Remember the collapsed state across refreshes.

### 2. View-mode toggle (top of the panel)
A small toggle at the top of the left panel switches the layout. Two modes for now; designed so more can be added later.

**Mode A - Tree (default): Machine -> Director -> Sessions**
- Roots are **Machines**. Under each machine, list its **Directors**; under each Director, list its sessions.
- A machine that runs multiple Directors shows the **machine name first, then each Director** beneath it (so the hierarchy is Machine name -> Director name -> sessions).
- Sessions under each Director are sorted the **same way the desktop sorts them** - not alphabetical. Use the same ordering source the desktop uses so a session keeps its slot and doesn't move when its name or activity state changes.
- Unreachable Directors still surface inline (current behaviour - never silently dropped).

**Mode B - Triage (needs-you first)**
- A flat, priority-ordered list across all Directors:
  - Sessions that **need you** (StatusColor red) at the **top**.
  - Everything **on hold** - including anything an agent has put on hold (`OnHold`) - at the **bottom**.
  - Everything else in between.
- This is the "what needs me right now" view; ordering is by status, not by Director.

### 3. Start a new session from the panel
- Add a "+" affordance (on each Director node in tree mode, and/or a panel-level control) that opens the existing new-session flow with the relevant Director pre-selected. Reuse `OpenNewSession` / `CreateSession` in `Cockpit.razor`, which already proxy session creation through the Gateway.

### 4. Future (not this issue)
- More view modes beyond the two above.
- Reordering / moving sessions within the Cockpit (drag to reorder, move between Directors), mirroring the desktop. Build the panel so this can be layered on later.

## Affected code

- `src/CcDirector.Cockpit/Components/SessionRail.razor` - add the two view modes; replace the `OrderBy(s => s.Name ?? s.RepoPath)` tree sort with the desktop ordering; add the triage ordering (red first, OnHold last).
- `src/CcDirector.Cockpit/Components/Pages/Cockpit.razor` - host the collapse state and view-mode toggle; wire a per-Director "new session" callback into the existing new-session modal.
- `src/CcDirector.Cockpit/wwwroot/app.css` - collapsible panel, toggle, and tree styling.
- Likely need the desktop's session ordering exposed on the wire (e.g. an order/index field on `SessionDto`) so the Cockpit can honour it instead of re-sorting.

## Acceptance criteria

- The left panel can be collapsed and re-expanded; collapsed state persists across refreshes.
- A view-mode toggle at the top of the panel switches between Tree and Triage.
- **Tree mode:** Machine -> Director -> sessions; machines with multiple Directors show machine name then each Director; session order under each Director matches the desktop and stays stable across refreshes (no alphabetical reshuffling).
- **Triage mode:** needs-you (red) sessions at the top, on-hold (including agent-held) sessions at the bottom, regardless of Director.
- A new session can be created from the panel, landing on the chosen Director.
- Unreachable Directors are still shown, not dropped, in tree mode.

comments.body:
## Design (researched, ready to implement)

### How desktop ordering works (the "they don't move" behavior)
The desktop persists a manual order via `Session.SortOrder` (int) - `src/CcDirector.Core/Sessions/Session.cs:295`. Drag-drop sets it (`MainWindow.axaml.cs:1086,1607`), it is saved to `sessions.json`, and lists render `.OrderBy(s => s.SortOrder)` (`SessionManager.cs:576`). Desktop is a flat list **per Director** (one Director per desktop app), no machine/director grouping.

### The blocking gap
`SessionDto` (the wire contract the Cockpit receives) has **no `SortOrder`** (`src/CcDirector.Gateway.Contracts/SessionDto.cs`), so the Cockpit cannot honor desktop order today - `SessionRail.razor:31` falls back to `OrderBy(s => s.Name ?? s.RepoPath)` (alphabetical -> reshuffling). Fix:
- Add `SortOrder` to `SessionDto`.
- Populate it in the Director projection at `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/ControlEndpoints.cs:~1824` (beside `StatusColor = s.StatusColor`): `SortOrder = s.SortOrder,`.
- The Gateway aggregator passes `SessionDto` through untouched, so it flows to the Cockpit automatically.

Triage data already exists on the wire: `StatusColor == "red"` (needs you) and `OnHold` (bottom).

### ASCII mockups

**Collapsed** (reclaim width for the terminal; thin status strip remains):
```
+--+--------------------------------------------------------------+
|>>| (*) mindzieWeb - build   SOREN_NORTH / dir 39aad623   WAITING |
+--+--------------------------------------------------------------+
|. |                                                              |
|o |              terminal pane fills the reclaimed width          |
|! |                                                              |
+--+--------------------------------------------------------------+
  ^ thin rail: stacked status dots + ">>" to expand
```

**Tree mode (default): Machine -> Director -> Sessions**
```
+-------------------------------------------------+
|  [ Tree ] [ Triage ]                     [ << ] |
+-------------------------------------------------+
| v SOREN_NORTH                  (8)    6 need you |
|    v director 39aad623                     [ + ]|
|       o  mindzieWeb - AI Test Loop      Working |
|       !  mindzieWeb - build           needs you |
|    v director c99a103c                     [ + ]|
|       o  cc-director - cockpit          Working |
|       !  cc-director - cockpit QA     needs you |
| v OTHER-PC                     (2)              |
|    v director a1b2c3d4                     [ + ]|
|       o  repo-x                         Working |
|    !  director deadbeef - unreachable           |
+-------------------------------------------------+
```
Sessions under each Director in SortOrder (desktop order), stable.

**Triage mode: needs-you first, on-hold last (flat across all Directors)**
```
+-------------------------------------------------+
|  [ Tree ] [ Triage ]                     [ << ] |
+-------------------------------------------------+
|  NEEDS YOU (5)                                  |  StatusColor == "red"
|    !  cc-director - cockpit QA      c99a103c    |
|    !  mindzieWeb - build            39aad623    |
|  ACTIVE (3)                                     |  everything else
|    o  mindzieWeb - AI Test Loop     39aad623    |
|    o  cc-director - cockpit         c99a103c    |
|  ON HOLD (2)                                    |  OnHold == true, last
|    -  parked-session-x              a1b2c3d4    |
+-------------------------------------------------+
   (director short-id per row, since triage drops the grouping)
```

### Implementation plan
**Backend (isolated, conflict-free):**
1. `SessionDto.cs` - add `public int SortOrder { get; set; }`.
2. `ControlEndpoints.cs:~1824` - add `SortOrder = s.SortOrder,`.

**Cockpit UI:**
3. `SessionRail.razor` - `ViewMode` param (Tree/Triage); Tree groups Machine->Director, `OrderBy(SortOrder).ThenBy(CreatedAt)`; Triage buckets red / other / on-hold; per-Director `[+]` -> new-session callback.
4. `Cockpit.razor` - collapse chevron + view toggle in `left-head`; `_leftCollapsed` + `_viewMode` persisted to localStorage; `[+]` reuses `OpenNewSession` pre-selecting that Director.
5. `app.css` - collapsed rail, toggle pills, tree disclosure/indent, triage section headers.

### Decisions
- Collapsed rail shows stacked status dots (keep at-a-glance awareness).
- Tree cross-ordering: machines alphabetical, then Director short-id (desktop has no cross-Director opinion).
- Future (separate issue): drag-to-reorder + move-between-Directors in the Cockpit.

Implemented in commit 990dba3. Cockpit collapsible left panel (persisted to localStorage, collapsed-rail of dots), Tree/Triage view-mode toggle (persisted), Tree shows Machine->Director->sessions in desktop order with multiple Directors per machine and unreachable Directors inline, Triage uses needs-you-first/on-hold-last buckets, per-Director [+] new session. All acceptance criteria met. Verified against code. Closing.

title: Cockpit: collapsible left panel with view-mode toggle (Machine/Director tree + needs-you triage) and start-new-session

body:
**Source:** voice transcript 2026-05-28 06:17 (`40c327af0a1c472693c6aab5fbe3e668`).

## The ask

When a session's turn ends and it is about to flip Red ("needs you"), if Wingman is enabled for the session, automatically run the Wingman "explain this session" briefing. Paint the dot **Yellow** while that briefing is generating ("the agent is done, but Wingman is still talking"). Flip to **Red** when the briefing is ready. Wingman is **on by default** per session. A session with Wingman off shows only the Terminal tab and behaves exactly like today.

## Why this is small

Most of the engine already exists:

- `ProactiveExplainService` already triggers a strong-model briefing on `WaitingForInput`, in background, with an in-flight guard. Today it is gated on `Session.MobileMode`; we just re-gate it on a new `Session.WingmanEnabled`.
- `SessionStatusWingman` is already the single writer of `StatusColor`. We extend its color rule to emit Yellow when a new `Session.IsExplaining` flag is set.
- `WingmanService.BriefingQuestion` is already the canonical "what is happening" prompt.
- Three-tab UI shells already exist on phone (`TalkPage.xaml`) and desktop (`session-view.html`).

Net new: one durable flag (`WingmanEnabled`), one transient flag (`IsExplaining`), one color rule extension, a re-gate, two toggle endpoints, and tab-visibility wiring in both clients.

## State machine (after)

```
                              +-----------+   bytes on PTY    +----------+
                session  ---> | Starting  | ----------------> | Working  |     <-- BLUE
                              +-----------+                   +----------+
                                                                    |
                                                                    | 10s silent (QuietThreshold)
                                                                    v
                       WingmanEnabled?  ------- NO ------->  WaitingForInput   <-- RED
                              |
                             YES
                              |
                              v
              +--------------------------------+   <-- YELLOW
              | Explaining                     |   ProactiveExplainService
              | (IsExplaining=true)            |   runs briefing in background
              +--------------------------------+
                              |
                       briefing done / failed / cancelled
                              v
                       WaitingForInput                       <-- RED
```

Color rule (in `SessionStatusWingman`):

```
if ActivityState in {Working, Starting}                       -> Blue
if WingmanEnabled and IsExplaining and ActivityState==WFI/WFP -> Yellow
if ActivityState in {WaitingForInput, WaitingForPerm, Idle}   -> Red
if ActivityState == Exited                                    -> Unknown
```

## Tab layout

**Wingman ON (default)**

```
+-------------------------------------------------------+
| [ Voice ] [ Wingman ] [ Terminal ]              [dot] |   <-- 3 tabs
+-------------------------------------------------------+
```

**Wingman OFF**

```
+-------------------------------------------------------+
| [ Terminal ]                                    [dot] |   <-- 1 tab
+-------------------------------------------------------+
```

Identical on Android and desktop. Tabs are hidden (not greyed) -- a Wingman-off session looks like a plain terminal because it is one.

## Timing (both clients, both modes)

```
                                       t=0     t=10s      t=10s+briefing      t=...
                                        |       |              |               |
  PTY active                            #####
  ActivityState                         WORK    WFI            WFI             WFI
  Wingman ENABLED:
    IsExplaining                        false   true           false           false
    StatusColor                         BLUE    YELLOW         RED             RED
    Briefing cache                      -       -              [briefing]      [briefing]
    Voice/Wingman/Terminal visible      Y/Y/Y   Y/Y/Y          Y/Y/Y           Y/Y/Y
  Wingman DISABLED:
    StatusColor                         BLUE    RED            RED             RED
    Voice/Wingman/Terminal visible      N/N/Y   N/N/Y          N/N/Y           N/N/Y
```

Quiet threshold stays at **10s** (existing `TerminalStateDetector.QuietThreshold`). Transcript said "5s" but the live value is 10s -- tuning that is a separate question. Briefing latency is capped at the existing `WingmanService.ProcessTimeout = 60s`.

## Where it lands per surface

**Server (`src/CcDirector.Core` + `src/CcDirector.ControlApi`)**
- `Session.WingmanEnabled` (default true, persisted via `SessionStateStore`)
- `Session.IsExplaining` (transient, in-memory)
- `SessionDto.WingmanEnabled` on the wire
- `SessionStatusWingman.ColorFromActivityState` extended to emit Yellow
- `ProactiveExplainService`: re-gate on `WingmanEnabled`, set/clear `IsExplaining`
- New endpoints: `POST /sessions/{id}/wingman/enable` and `.../disable`

**Desktop (`src/CcDirector.Avalonia` + `session-view.html`)**
- Hide Voice + Wingman tabs when `wingmanEnabled=false`
- Session-create dialog: Wingman toggle (default ON)
- Per-session settings: same toggle
- Add Yellow to the dot palette (tooltip: "Wingman is reading the session")

**Android (`phone/CcDirectorClient`)**
- `TalkPage.xaml`: hide Voice + Wingman tabs when `wingmanEnabled=false`
- Roster (`SessionInfo`/`RosterParser`): wire-format add
- `FifoPage`: skip Wingman-off sessions (they cannot participate in the conveyor)
- Add Yellow to the dot palette

**Gateway**
- Aggregator passes the field through. No new endpoints.

## Cost model

- Wingman OFF: zero Opus turns per session.
- Wingman ON: exactly one strong-model briefing per turn-end.
- The toggle is the bill.

## What this does NOT do

- Does not add a new `ActivityState`. The byte-only state machine stays as is.
- Does not change `QuietThreshold`.
- Does not change briefing prompt or model.
- Does not introduce scheduling or per-user overrides.

## Order of work (when approved)

1. `Session.WingmanEnabled` + persistence + `SessionDto.WingmanEnabled`
2. `Session.IsExplaining` + Yellow rule in `SessionStatusWingman`
3. Re-gate `ProactiveExplainService` + set/clear `IsExplaining`
4. REST toggle endpoints + Gateway pass-through
5. Desktop: dot Yellow, hide tabs, create-time + per-session toggle
6. Android: same tab hiding + roster field + Yellow dot
7. Tests: `SessionStatusWingmanTests` (Yellow only when enabled+explaining), `ProactiveExplainServiceTests` (no-op when off), DTO wire-format

Estimate: ~1 day server, ~1 day clients. Heavy lifting (`ProactiveExplainService`, `BriefingQuestion`, 3-tab shells) already shipped.

---

Full plan with extended ASCII diagrams: [`docs/plans/wingman-auto-explain-toggle.md`](../blob/main/docs/plans/wingman-auto-explain-toggle.md)

comments.body: Implemented (commit incl. a93a774). Session.WingmanEnabled (persisted) + IsExplaining (transient); SessionStatusWingman yellow rule while the wingman reads a turn; ProactiveExplainService gated on WingmanEnabled; toggle endpoint POST /sessions/{sid}/wingman-enabled + create-time wiring; desktop/phone tab hiding + yellow dot; tests. Verified against code. Closing.

title: Wingman auto-explain toggle + yellow translating state

body:
## Problem

In FIFO voice mode, advancing to a session is slow: today we navigate to the session first, then send it to the Wingman, then wait for the Wingman to turn it into speech. The user lands on the page and waits for the audio.

## Desired behavior

- **Pre-generate** the audio for what we are going to say **before** navigating to that session. Do not move to the FIFO session page until its audio is ready.
- **Cache only the last clip** (the most recent thing the Wingman said). 
- Add a **Replay** button on the session page so the user can re-hear it if they missed it.
- If the user asks the Wingman something on the page, cache only that last reply.
- **Clear the cache as soon as the session starts working again** (a new job starts), so stale audio is never replayed.

## Notes

Reported by voice note (Z Flip / SM-F721W, recorded 2026-05-27 16:57). Performance/UX improvement for the FIFO conveyor flow. Relates to FIFO screen work in #143.

comments.body:
Implemented and deployed to the phone (com.ccdirector.client on the Z Flip4) on 2026-05-28.

- **Pre-generate before navigating**: `VoiceConversation.PrepareExplainAsync` (new) fetches the wingman briefing + synthesizes the audio without playing it; `FifoPage.PresentNextAsync` holds on the current card showing "Preparing next session..." until the bytes are in hand, then switches and plays.
- **Cache only the last clip**: new pure `LastClipCache` (5 unit tests in `LastClipCacheTests.cs`). The briefing caches its audio; a wingman answer replaces it via the new `onClip` callback threaded into `DeliverToSessionAsync` -> `SpeakAndCacheAsync`.
- **Replay button**: added to the FIFO header, visible only when a clip is cached, disabled while busy/recording.
- **Cleared on a new job**: `ClearCachedClip()` runs on Delivered, at the start of every advance, and on Exit.
- Removed the now-orphaned `SpeakExplainAsync`.

`dotnet test` 61/61 pass; MAUI Android build 0 errors; deployed via `scripts/update-phone-client.ps1`.

title: FIFO voice mode: pre-generate the spoken briefing before navigating, cache it, add Replay

body:
## Problem

In FIFO mode (and other Android screens) the buttons frequently do not work, and it appears to correlate with having no internet connection. Buttons should never depend on being online to respond.

## Desired behavior

- A button press gives **immediate** local feedback regardless of connectivity.
- Specifically, when recording: pressing Stop should **stop the recording instantly**, then attempt the upload as a separate step.
- No button action should block on the network. After the click, either advance to the next page or show a progress bar, but the UI must react quickly.

## Notes

Reported by voice note (Z Flip / SM-F721W, recorded 2026-05-27 16:45). This is a general Android responsiveness defect; FIFO is where it was observed but the fix should cover all button handlers that currently couple the click to a network call.

comments.body: Implemented. Added `OfflineGuard` (pure, unit-tested) and an `EnsureOnline(action)` gate in FifoPage that runs BEFORE any handler enters the busy/disabled state for a network call. Gated choke points: `PresentNextAsync` (Start/Skip/advance/CheckNow), `RunFifoTurnAsync` (send / ask wingman), `OnHoldClicked` (hold), `LoadQueueCountAsync` (start panel). When offline the press shows an instant red status ("No connection - can't <action> right now. Check your signal and try again.") and the buttons stay live, instead of dimming behind the 5-minute HTTP timeout. Local feedback (briefing cut, mic stopped) still runs regardless. Tests: 4 new in OfflineGuardTests, all phone tests pass. MAUI Android build 0 errors. Deployed to the Z Flip4 (com.ccdirector.client). Scope: FIFO only - TalkPage same-pattern gate is the noted follow-up.

title: Android: buttons appear dead when offline; clicks must respond instantly, upload after

body:
## Problem

On the phone FIFO voice screen (and any screen that reads text aloud), there is no way to stop an in-progress read. If the Wingman/agent starts a long read, you are stuck waiting for it to finish. This is especially bad while driving.

## Desired behavior

- A **Cancel / Stop** control that immediately halts the current TTS playback.
- It must be **pinned at the top** of the screen and reachable **without scrolling**.
