- Showing the text being read is optional; it can sit below. The priority is being able to cancel, not to view the text.

## Notes

Reported by voice note (Z Flip / SM-F721W, recorded 2026-05-27 19:03). Related to the FIFO button-layout work in #143 (buttons pinned to top, output below), but distinct: this is about stopping playback, not naming/positioning the action buttons.

comments.body:
Already shipped. The pinned-top Stop-talking pill is implemented on both voice screens that read aloud:

- FifoPage:  (StopTalkingButton, lines 174-182) +  OnStopTalkingClicked (cancels the turn and stops playback).
- TalkPage:  (StopTalkingButton, lines 248-249) +  OnStopTalkingClicked (cancels turn + _tts.Stop()).
- Backing event: `IReplySpeaker.PlayingChanged` + `Stop()` in `AndroidReplySpeaker` (with audio-focus restore).

The button is HorizontalOptions=Center, VerticalOptions=Start, so it sits at the top of the screen reachable without scrolling, and only shows while a clip is playing. FifoTextPage is text-only (no TTS) so no Stop button is needed there.

All three files carry explicit `issue #146` comments. Closing as done.

Correction to the previous comment (paths got eaten by shell parsing). The implementation lives at:

- FifoPage: phone/CcDirectorClient/FifoPage.xaml (StopTalkingButton, lines 174-182) + phone/CcDirectorClient/FifoPage.xaml.cs OnStopTalkingClicked.
- TalkPage: phone/CcDirectorClient/TalkPage.xaml (StopTalkingButton, lines 248-249) + phone/CcDirectorClient/TalkPage.xaml.cs OnStopTalkingClicked.
- Speaker: phone/CcDirectorClient/Voice/IReplySpeaker.cs + phone/CcDirectorClient/Platforms/Android/AndroidReplySpeaker.cs.

title: FIFO/voice screens: add a Cancel button to stop an in-progress text-to-speech read

body:
## Summary

The phone client currently has two separate screens that both walk through every session that needs the user, and they overlap and confuse:

- **FIFO page** (`FifoPage`): the conveyor through sessions that need you (Answer / Skip / Hold / Ask Wingman).
- **Talk page conductor** (`TalkPage`): the "Talk to all sessions that need me" button starts an all-sessions rotation with Ask Agent / Ask Wingman / Next session.

Having both is redundant. We want to keep only the FIFO path.

## Proposed changes

1. **Remove the Talk page conductor mode.** Drop the all-sessions "Next session" rotation entirely. FIFO becomes the single way to walk through sessions that need you.

2. **Keep the session list / roster and rename it to a "Sessions" page.** The part of the current Talk page where you see all individual sessions and select one to talk to (one-on-one voice with a single session) is still useful. That screen should be repurposed/renamed to **"Sessions"** (browse all sessions, tap one to talk to it individually).

3. **Replace the conductor button with a FIFO button.** On the Sessions page, where the purple **"Talk to all sessions that need me"** button currently is (above the session list), put a **"FIFO"** button instead that launches the FIFO page.

Net result:
- **Sessions** page: list of all sessions, select one for one-on-one voice; a "FIFO" button at the top to start the conveyor.
- **FIFO** page: the only multi-session walkthrough.
- No more Talk conductor.

## Relevant code

- `phone/CcDirectorClient/TalkPage.xaml` / `TalkPage.xaml.cs` -- conductor mode, `ConductorButton` ("Talk to all sessions that need me"), `NextButton`, `_conductorMode`, `OnConductorClicked`, `OnNextClicked`.
- `phone/CcDirectorClient/FifoPage.xaml` / `FifoPage.xaml.cs` -- the FIFO conveyor to keep.
- `phone/CcDirectorClient/AppShell.xaml` and the top-right burger menu entries (Talk / FIFO / FIFO Text / Notes / Exes / Dictionary / Transcripts) will need updating to reflect the rename.

## Note

Another agent has filed a related bug on this area. These should be consolidated into one effort; cross-link and merge when picked up.

comments.body: Implemented. Talk page renamed to 'Sessions' (page title + AppShell route title). Conductor mode removed (no ConductorButton/NextButton/_conductorMode in TalkPage). Roster kept with single-session Voice/Wingman/Terminal tabs; a 'FIFO' button replaces the old conductor button and launches the FIFO page, which is now the only multi-session walkthrough. Commit 101ce97.

title: Consolidate phone voice UX: remove Talk page, keep FIFO, rename roster to a Sessions page

body:
## Summary

On the phone Sessions screen (the Talk page) there is a button at the top of the session roster labeled **"Talk to all sessions that need me"**. It launches an older in-page multi-session "conductor" voice flow, which is a different screen than the FIFO page. That button should simply be renamed to **FIFO** and navigate the user to the FIFO page instead.

## Current behavior

`phone/CcDirectorClient/TalkPage.xaml` (the Sessions roster) has:

```xml
<Button x:Name="ConductorButton" Text="Talk to all sessions that need me"
        BackgroundColor="#6B46C1" TextColor="White" HeightRequest="50"
        CornerRadius="12" Clicked="OnConductorClicked" />
```

`OnConductorClicked` in `TalkPage.xaml.cs` starts the in-page conductor flow (requests mic, walks "sessions that need me" inside the Talk page itself). This is the older path and is separate from / inconsistent with the dedicated FIFO page.

## Desired behavior

- Rename the button from "Talk to all sessions that need me" to **FIFO**.
- The button should navigate to the FIFO page (route `FifoPage`, registered in `AppShell.xaml`) rather than running the old in-page conductor flow.
- There should be one place users go to walk every session that needs them: the FIFO page.

## Files

- `phone/CcDirectorClient/TalkPage.xaml` - the `ConductorButton` definition (text + click handler).
- `phone/CcDirectorClient/TalkPage.xaml.cs` - `OnConductorClicked`; should navigate to FIFO instead of running the conductor flow.
- `phone/CcDirectorClient/AppShell.xaml` - FIFO route is already registered (`FifoPage`).

## Notes

Related to the FIFO standardization work in #143. The goal is a single, consistent FIFO entry point. The old in-page conductor flow can be retired or left unreferenced once the button points at the FIFO page. To be dealt with later.

comments.body: Implemented. The Sessions roster button is renamed to FIFO and OnFifoClicked navigates to the FIFO page (Shell.Current.GoToAsync("//FifoPage"), TalkPage.xaml.cs:363). The old in-page conductor flow was removed. Superseded by the consolidation in #145.

title: Sessions page: rename 'Talk to all sessions that need me' button to FIFO and route it to the FIFO page

body:
## Summary

On the phone FIFO voice screen the action buttons need to be standardized and repositioned. This is a driving-safety / usability issue: while driving, the buttons must never move and must never require scrolling.

## Current behavior

The FIFO active panel (`phone/CcDirectorClient/FifoPage.xaml`) has these buttons:

- **Answer** (big green push-to-talk). Its label dynamically changes to "Send" while recording and "Working..." while busy (see `SetAnswerButton` in `FifoPage.xaml.cs`). This is why the label looks inconsistent: sometimes "Answer", sometimes "Send".
- **Skip** / **Hold** (side-by-side row)
- **Ask Wingman**

The buttons sit inside a `ScrollView`, after the session card, the "What's happening" briefing, and the recording card. As content above them grows, the buttons move down and can scroll off screen.

## Desired behavior

1. **Standardize the naming.** Use consistent "Ask X" naming for the two primary actions:
   - Rename the primary **Answer** button to **Ask Agent** (it talks to the session / Claude Code agent).
   - Keep **Ask Wingman** as-is.
   - Point: both primary actions read as "Ask [who]" so it is instantly obvious which one talks to the agent vs. the wingman. It does not need to say "Answer".
   - Avoid the label morphing into different words ("Send" / "Working...") in a way that confuses which button is which. Pick a clear convention for the recording/busy state that does not change the button's identity.

2. **Pin the buttons to the top of the screen.** The action buttons (Ask Agent, Ask Wingman, Skip, Hold) must stay fixed at the top and never move. The user is driving and must not have to scroll to reach them.

3. **Output goes underneath, scrollable.** Whatever is being answered for the session (the wingman/agent output, the "What's happening" briefing, transcript, reply) should appear clearly BELOW the fixed buttons. Only that output area scrolls; the buttons do not.

4. **Big and up front.** Buttons must be large and immediately reachable. The current Answer button is a good size; apply similar prominence across the standardized layout.

## Files

- `phone/CcDirectorClient/FifoPage.xaml` - button layout currently lives inside the ScrollView; needs the buttons hoisted into a fixed top region with a scrollable output region below.
- `phone/CcDirectorClient/FifoPage.xaml.cs` - `SetAnswerButton` drives the dynamic "Answer" / "Send" / "Working..." text.

## Notes

Reported from a driving context. Priority is that the buttons are predictable, fixed-position, and reachable without scrolling. To be dealt with later.

comments.body: Implemented. FifoPage active panel now pins the action buttons in a fixed top region (Grid.Row=0) that never scrolls, with the per-session output scrolling underneath (Grid.Row=1). Primary actions renamed to 'Ask Agent' / 'Ask Wingman'; the Ask Agent label no longer morphs into 'Send'/'Working...' (state shown by colour). See phone/CcDirectorClient/FifoPage.xaml and commits e128861, 101ce97.

title: FIFO screen: standardize action buttons (Ask Agent / Ask Wingman), pin them to the top, output below

body:
## Defect class
The inbound voice-transcript cleanup stage frequently fails to complete: the underlying `claude --print` call does not finish within the 60s limit and the pipeline falls back to passing the raw transcript through verbatim. In this review run it occurred on 5 of 15 turns. Critically, it also happened on short transcripts, not just long dictations, which points to per-call process spawn / cold-start cost rather than input length being the driver.

## Why it matters
The verbatim fallback is fidelity-safe (no meaning is lost), so this is not a correctness regression. But it means the cleanup stage simply does not run a third of the time: filler words and obvious mishearings that cleanup would normally fix are left in, so the downstream agent receives noisier input and the voice experience is inconsistent. One affected short turn carried a likely-misheard token that cleanup never got the chance to correct.

## Recommended fix
Make the inbound cleanup call reliable rather than racing a cold 60s `claude --print` per turn. Options, in order of preference:
- Use a warm/persistent session for cleanup instead of spawning a fresh `claude --print` per turn (eliminates cold-start cost, which the short-input timeouts implicate).
- Failing that, use a faster cleanup model and/or raise the timeout, and instrument the call latency so the cause (spawn vs. inference) is measurable.
Likely location: the inbound cleanup path in `src/CcDirector.Core/Wingman/WingmanService.cs`. Keep the verbatim fallback as the safety net, but it should be the rare exception, not a routine outcome.

## Evidence (local, personal - not included here)
Turn ids: 75fd3bc0, 95d2efa2, 31593b22, 9e70cfbd, a73a8071
Archived at: %LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\voice-review\flagged\<turnId>\ (one folder per id above, each with review.md)
Reviewed by the voice-review skill on 2026-05-26.

comments.body:
Recurrence in the 2026-05-28 voice-review run:

The same inbound-cleanup failure class observed again on two more turns. One was the original `claude --print did not finish within 60s` timeout. The other was a `wingman JSON parse failed` cleanup error - functionally equivalent (verbatim fallback fires, cleanup stage is skipped). Both fell back to verbatim, so no meaning was lost, but the cleanup stage simply did not run. Adding `wingman JSON parse failed` to the failure modes already enumerated here suggests the fix scope should cover not just the timeout but the wingman call itself returning unusable output.

Evidence (local, personal - not included here):
- 618ec96a - %LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\voice-review\flagged\618ec96aaf734899a0091d2c8bb713fe\
- f045a5e6 - %LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\voice-review\flagged\f045a5e695c34871be7543f9076803e7\

Both archived with review.md by the voice-review skill on 2026-05-28.

Implemented in WingmanService. Inbound transcript cleanup switched from per-turn 'claude --print' cold-spawn to a direct OpenAI chat call (gpt-4.1-nano) on a shared HttpClient with a 20s timeout; verbatim fail-open fallback retained; latency instrumented. Comments cite #142. Verified against code. Closing.

title: Voice inbound transcript cleanup frequently times out (60s) and skips cleanup

body:
## Problem

In voice mode ("wingman") the spoken reply did not line up with what the agent actually said in the raw Claude session: it was too abbreviated and took too big liberties with the meaning. When this happens there is no trail to inspect, so we cannot prove where the divergence came from.

## Two jobs

**Job 1 - fidelity.** The outbound summarizer (`src/CcDirector.Core/Voice/Services/ClaudeSummarizer.cs`) forces every agent reply into "two to four short, casual sentences... concepts only," which drops facts (names/numbers) and reframes the answer. Make the spoken version faithful to the agent's actual answer; remove the silent truncation fallbacks (on failure, return empty so the client speaks the genuine reply).

**Job 2 - observability.** Add a per-turn comparison log capturing audio + user transcript (raw + cleaned) + agent reply (`DisplayText`) + wingman spoken (`Summary`), correlated per voice turn, with short retention (~5 days, then auto-purge), so a future divergence can be flagged and the four artifacts compared.

## Plan

Detailed implementation plan: `docs/plans/voice-mode-fidelity-and-logging.md`

Related (separate, word-level mishearing bug): `docs/problems/voice-dictionary-not-applied-on-mobile.md`

## Scope notes

- Server-side only; no phone/MAUI or contract changes (correlation is stateless by sessionId + recency, justified by the per-session sequential voice orchestrator).
- The inbound meaning-drift suspect (transcript cleanup) is intentionally NOT changed here; with the log in place we capture a real example and decide from evidence.

comments.body:
## Additional scope from voice note (2026-05-27 15:10)

Beyond the comparison logging in Job 2, the Wingman should be able to **read its own prior turns back**, not just have them logged for human comparison:

- Persist every Wingman turn (what it said) **and** the terminal bytes it looked at, correlated per session/turn with correct timing.
- Expose a **retrieval API** so the Wingman can look up what it said last time and what the terminal showed at that moment.

Rationale: "the Wingman's not very good," so we need it to review its own prior decisions + the terminal state it saw, in order to improve over time. Job 2 here already captures the artifacts; this adds the read-back API on top.

Source: recorder voice note, Z Flip / SM-F721W, 2026-05-27 15:10.

Closing: both original jobs (filed 2026-05-25) are implemented on the current tree.

Job 1 - fidelity: src/CcDirector.Core/Voice/Services/ClaudeSummarizer.cs no longer forces 'two to four short casual sentences'. The prompt is now fidelity-first ('Your job is FIDELITY, not brevity ... preserve every concrete fact: names, numbers, yes/no ... completeness wins over shortness'), and the silent truncation fallback is removed - on failure it returns empty so the client speaks the genuine reply.

Job 2 - observability: src/CcDirector.Core/Voice/VoiceTurnLog.cs implements the per-turn comparison log (audio + inbound.json raw/cleaned transcript + outbound.json agent reply vs wingman spoken), correlated per session by recency, 5-day retention with auto-purge.

NOT done, and deliberately left out of this close: the read-back/retrieval API from the 2026-05-27 comment (Wingman reading its own prior turns + the terminal bytes it saw). That is a separate, larger feature and should get its own issue if pursued; VoiceTurnLog is currently write-only and does not persist the terminal bytes the Wingman looked at.

title: Voice mode: fix wingman over-abbreviation + log audio/transcript/reply chain for comparison

body:
## Problem

The gateway today is a foreground **console app** (`cc-director-gateway`, `OutputType=Exe`). Someone has to start it in a terminal and keep that window open. That is not sustainable for a process meant to always run whenever the user is logged in.

A Windows Service is the wrong model: the gateway (and CC Director itself) depends on the logged-in user session. A service under `svchost`/SYSTEM gains nothing and complicates the lifecycle.

## Solution

Wrap the existing gateway host in a small **Avalonia system-tray app** (notification-area icon, lower-right of the taskbar). Runs quietly in the background, starts on login, and is never really "closed" - closing minimizes to the tray; only explicit Quit stops it.

This is a lifecycle swap, not a rewrite. `GatewayHost` (`src/CcDirector.Gateway/GatewayHost.cs`) is already a self-contained `IAsyncDisposable` (`StartAsync`/`StopAsync`), and `Program.cs` just news it up and blocks on Ctrl+C. The tray app replaces "block on Ctrl+C" with "block on the Avalonia app lifetime, owning the same `GatewayHost`."

## Design decisions (agreed)

| Decision | Choice |
|---|---|
| Hosting model | Wrap gateway in ONE process; Avalonia shell hosts `GatewayHost` in-process. |
| UI framework | Avalonia (matches CC Director; built-in `TrayIcon` + `NativeMenu`). |
| Autostart | `HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run` entry, registered idempotently. |
| Single instance | Enforced via named mutex; second launch surfaces existing instance and exits. |
| Console exe | Kept. `cc-director-gateway` stays for debug/CI/scripts; both reference the same `GatewayHost`. |
| Status window | Deferred. v1 is tray icon + right-click menu only. |

## Scope - v1 (in)

- New project `CcDirector.GatewayApp` - Avalonia, `OutputType=WinExe` (no console flash), references `CcDirector.Gateway`.
- Hosts `GatewayHost` in-process on a background task at startup.
- Tray icon + status dot (running/stopped) + right-click menu.
- `ShutdownMode = OnExplicitShutdown`.
- Named-mutex single-instance guard.
- Idempotent `HKCU\...\Run` autostart registration.
- Clean `StopAsync()` on Quit.

## Out of scope (later)

- Status window (double-click -> port, token, Tailscale state, log tail, director count).
- "Start on login" toggle UI.
- Retiring the console exe.

## Tray menu (v1)

```
[*] Gateway running on :7878
    Open Dashboard
    Open Logs Folder
    Restart Gateway
    ----
    Quit
```

## Open questions

- Install location for the tray exe; who writes the Run key (app on first run vs `cc-director-setup-avalonia`)?
- Tray icon asset: reuse CC Director icon or a distinct gateway icon?

Full plan: `docs/plans/gateway-tray-app.md`

comments.body:
Shipped. Two commits landed it:

- 56da651 ("feat(gateway): add system-tray app hosting the gateway in-process (#140)") - introduced the `CcDirector.GatewayApp` Avalonia tray host (`src/CcDirector.GatewayApp/`).
- d9c0086 ("feat(gateway): harden tray app - install script, port-conflict status, distinct icon (#140)") - the install script (`scripts/install-gateway-tray.ps1`), port-conflict status surface, and the distinct tray icon.

Missed this in the 2026-05-28 sweep; closing now.

title: Convert gateway from console app to Avalonia system-tray app

body:
> **Delegation note (2026-05-24):** The personal assistant **brain** (the `PA/` Karpathy wiki, the `personal-assistant` skill, the `pa` library, and ingesting Soren's data) is being implemented in the **private repo** (`thefrederiksen/private`), tracked in **private issue #1**, plan at `private/PA/docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md`. It is built brain-first and proven by talking to a session directly. **This issue (#139) now covers only the ORCHESTRATION layer** that wraps the brain once it works: gateway queue hub, centralized communication queue, and voice/email channels. Phase 3 below is delegated to the private repo.

## Summary

Build a Personal Assistant that handles Soren's three domains (consulting / mindzie / health), reachable by **voice** (CC Director Client app) and **email** (a mailbox the gateway watches). It triages and handles email, listens to recordings/dictations, answers questions, validates programming tasks, and answers back.

Full design: [docs/plans/personal-assistant.md](../blob/main/docs/plans/personal-assistant.md)

## Core architecture

- **Gateway is dumb about meaning.** No LLM in the gateway; it is the nervous system (watch, dedup, queue, dispatch, route). All judgment lives in director sessions.
- **The brain = one always-warm director session** running a `personal-assistant` skill out of the `private` repo, fanning out **sub-agents** (isolated context, summary-only return) so the parent stays clean. One session now; the queue makes growing to 2-3 a dial, not a redesign.
- **Shared memory = a git markdown wiki** (Karpathy loop): retrieve -> derive -> write back, indexed into the vault for semantic search. Lets the warm session survive compaction and keeps sub-agents consistent.
- **Gateway = queue hub, two lanes:** inbound work (to sessions) and outbound approvals (the centralized Comm Manager queue). Durable store, priority lanes (voice > direct asks > background), idempotency keys. **Dispatch = push the top item only when the session is Idle** (uses existing Wingman activity-state detection), so a running sub-agent is never interrupted; background work is chunked small so voice rarely waits.
- **Tooling split:** gateway = queue + channel watchers + idle dispatcher + router + session supervision. Session = the skill + a `pa` Python library that is the **single safe writer** to the wiki (commit-retry/serialized) plus plumbing (inbox fetch, draft queue, answer log).
- **Centralized Comm Queue (multi-machine):** the gateway owns the one comm DB behind an API (enqueue/list/approve/reject); directors lose direct DB access; all clients (CLI, desktop UI, sessions) go through the API. **Two-path sending on the always-on gateway, Approved-only:** deterministic sends via plain Python, judgment-needed sends dispatched to a session. Approval gate preserved (proposed exemption: self-addressed digest may auto-send).

## Phases (data migration is LAST on purpose)

Build empty, prove each piece, migrate live data in only at the end so development never risks real data.

- [ ] **Phase 1 - Gateway inbound queue hub.** Durable priority queue + idempotency; enqueue/claim/complete API; idle-watcher dispatcher (push top item only when target session Idle).
- [ ] **Phase 2 - Gateway-owned Communication Queue API.** Add the API over a dev/empty DB; repoint all clients (cc-comm-queue CLI, desktop Comm Manager, sessions); remove directors' direct-DB code; two-path sending scaffold (Approved-only). Do NOT migrate live data yet.
- [ ] **Phase 3 - Assistant brain.** `personal-assistant` skill in `private` (persona, orchestration, conventions safety rules, sub-agent fan-out, retrieve->derive->write-back); `pa` Python library (single safe writer + plumbing); git markdown wiki + vault indexing; gateway keeps one session warm (spawn/supervise/restart).
- [ ] **Phase 4 - Email triage (urgent slice).** Gateway mailbox watcher (read-only) enqueues small triage chunks, cron a few/day; assistant categorizes/summarizes/drafts-to-approval-queue/produces digest; digest delivery per the self-reply decision.
- [ ] **Phase 5 - Voice integration.** CC Director Client "Assistant" target routes the voice round-trip to the warm session via the gateway (top-priority lane, no boot latency).
- [ ] **Phase 6 - Data migration (LAST).** Migrate ALL existing data: Comm Manager live 14 pending + 100 history into the gateway DB; assistant knowledge into wiki/vault (MyHealthHelper/Jarvis, coaches/LOS, private/assistant identities + tasks, relevant vault). Verify counts/integrity; nothing lost.

## Open decisions (not blockers)

- Front-door name (keep "Jarvis" for health; umbrella maybe "Life").
- Which mailbox/domain (recommend personal, identity-neutral).
- Email cadence (recommend a few scheduled scans/day).
- Digest delivery (recommend single self-addressed summary, readable or spoken).
- Confirm self-reply exemption to the no-direct-send rule.

## Non-goals (for now)

- Unbounded concurrent assistants (start with one; queue scales it).
- Auth/security gating on gateway APIs (single-user tailnet).
- Hands-free voice (wake word) - separate effort.

## Touches

- **Gateway** (`src/CcDirector.Gateway`): queue hub, comm-queue API, channel watchers, dispatcher, two-path sender, session supervision.
- **Director / Core** (`src/CcDirector.Core`, ControlApi): remove direct comm-DB access; session-state-driven dispatch hooks.
- **CC Director Client** (`phone/CcDirectorClient`): Assistant target.
- **private repo**: `personal-assistant` skill, `pa` library, wiki.
- **cc-comm-queue tool**: repoint to gateway API.

comments.body: Closing per the 2026-05-28 sweep. The Personal Assistant work has effectively moved out of this repo: the brain (Phase 3) is in the private repo (`thefrederiksen/private` #1, `private/PA/docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md`), and the comm-queue scheduler infrastructure shipped as #127. The remaining gateway/director orchestration phases can be refiled as focused issues if and when they're picked up. Design doc remains at `docs/plans/personal-assistant.md` for reference.

title: Personal Assistant: gateway queue hub + warm assistant session + git wiki (director + gateway)

body:
## Summary

Build a new native Android app, **CC Director Client** (`com.ccdirector.client`), by cloning the existing CC Recorder app. The recorder stays completely untouched (Soren uses it daily). The new app is a hands-busy, voice-first client for talking to Claude Code sessions while driving or walking, with two modes:

1. **Single session** - pick one session and talk to it, same as the web voice mode works today.
2. **All sessions (conductor)** - the app rotates through every session, reading you only the ones that need you, one at a time.

Interaction stays button-driven for now: **push-to-talk**, then **push-to-send**, exactly like the current web voice mode. Hands-free (wake word / end-word) is explicitly deferred to a follow-up (see "Out of scope").

## Why

Voice mode on the phone works well today but is locked to a single session inside a browser page, and the browser suspends the page when backgrounded, so when an agent finishes while another app is in front the reply fails to play ("problem fetching"). Soren wants to:
- Talk to multiple sessions in one trip (car, dog walk) without juggling tabs.
- Keep the screen awake like Waze.
- Hear replies while another app is in front, with music ducking under the voice.

A native app is the only way to get reliable background audio, a wake lock, and audio-focus ducking; a web page cannot hold a backgrounded network connection or play fresh audio reliably.

## Decisions locked with Soren

- **New app, recorder untouched.** Clone `phone/CcRecorder` to `phone/CcDirectorClient`. Do not edit the recorder.
- **Buttons stay** (push-to-talk / push-to-send). No silence detection, no auto-send.
- **Conductor scope:** only speak for sessions that need you (just finished a turn, or asking a question). Stay silent on sessions still working or idle. No "still working" roll-call noise.
- **Turn-taking:** after reading one session, **wait**. Soren either push-to-talk replies to that session, or presses **Next** to move on. Nothing auto-advances.
- **Reply voice:** native Android text-to-speech (`Android.Speech.Tts.TextToSpeech`). No `/tts` network fetch to speak, so it stays reliable backgrounded and on weak signal.
- **Keep screen awake** while voice mode is active (Waze behavior).
- **Background speaking** via an Android foreground service that owns the network round-trip + playback.
- **Music ducks** under the voice (audio focus `AudioFocusGain.TransientMayDuck`), it does not stop.

## Architecture

The app is a smart **client**; no new gateway endpoints are required for v1.

- **Roster:** `GET /sessions` on the gateway (`https://soren-north.taildb08ed.ts.net`) returns every session across all directors, with name, state, and the owning director's Tailnet base URL (`TailnetEndpoint` / `ViewUrl`). This is the conductor's source of truth.
- **Per-session voice round-trip:** done directly against the owning director's Control API (the same endpoints the web voice page uses today), using the base URL from the roster:
  1. Record audio (reuse recorder capture) and upload chunks: `POST /voice/utterance` (register) -> `/voice/utterance/{id}/chunk/{n}` -> `/voice/utterance/{id}/complete` (server transcribes).
  2. Send the transcript: `POST /chat { sessionId, text }`, then follow the turn to completion to get the reply text.
  3. Speak the reply with native Android TTS.
- **Recap ("explain what we were doing"):** `GET /sessions/{sid}/recap` via the gateway (cached; `POST` to regenerate). Prefix each spoken item with the session name + the recap + the answer/question.
- **Conductor loop (client-side):** poll `GET /sessions`, filter to sessions needing attention (finished turn / pending question), queue them, speak one (name + recap + answer), wait for reply or Next, then advance. Round-robin falls out naturally.

State classification (which sessions "need you") reuses the existing session state already surfaced in the roster / wingman, no new detector.

## Work breakdown

### Phase 1 - Clone the app (no behavior change)
- [ ] Copy `phone/CcRecorder` to `phone/CcDirectorClient` verbatim.
- [ ] Rename project/namespace `CcRecorder` -> `CcDirectorClient`; `RootNamespace`, `ApplicationTitle` = "CC Director Client", `ApplicationId` = `com.ccdirector.client`.
- [ ] Add the new csproj to the solution. Confirm it builds and sideloads alongside the recorder (distinct package id, both installable at once).
- [ ] Leave `phone/CcRecorder` byte-for-byte unchanged.

### Phase 2 - Single-session voice (prove the clone end-to-end)
- [ ] New "Talk" screen (separate from the recorder UI) with a session list from `GET /sessions`; tap a session to enter single-session mode.
- [ ] Push-to-talk / push-to-send buttons driving capture -> `/voice/utterance` upload -> transcribe -> `/chat` -> reply.
- [ ] Speak the reply via native Android TTS.
- [ ] Wake lock: keep the screen on while the Talk screen is active; release on leave.
- [ ] This should match the current web voice experience, just native.

### Phase 3 - Background audio + ducking
- [ ] Foreground service (clone of `RecorderForegroundService`, type `mediaPlayback`/`microphone`) that keeps the round-trip + TTS alive when backgrounded or screen-off. Fixes "problem fetching".
- [ ] Request audio focus `TransientMayDuck` while speaking; abandon focus after, so music ducks then restores.
- [ ] Verify: app in background, music playing, agent finishes -> reply speaks over ducked music, no fetch error.

### Phase 4 - All-sessions conductor
- [ ] Mode toggle / "Talk to all" entry alongside single-session.
- [ ] Conductor loop: poll roster, filter to sessions needing you, queue, speak one at a time prefixed with session name + recap + answer.
- [ ] After each: wait. Push-to-talk replies to *that* session; **Next** button advances. No auto-advance.
- [ ] A way to switch between sessions / jump back to single-session mode.

## Out of scope (follow-up issues)
- Hands-free start (wake word) and end-word ("over") to replace buttons. Deferred per Soren; buttons must be brought to foreground to use for now.
- Server-side gateway "voice conductor" (only needed if client-side orchestration proves insufficient).
- iOS build (recorder is Android-only today).

## Notes / constraints
- ASCII-only in all code and output (project rule).
- Enterprise logging on public methods; responsive UI; no fallback programming (project `CLAUDE.md`).
- Recorder app is the daily driver - do not touch it.

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: CC Director Client: native voice app for single + multi-session (clone of CC Recorder)

body:
## Summary

Consolidated, prioritized backlog for making Wingman / session-state detection
stable and trustworthy. This is the umbrella that ties together the flip-flop work
(#136), the foundational state-detection work (#129), and the misclassification
report stream (#131), plus everything found while fixing #136. Goal: the colored
badge and the "what Claude wants" text always agree with reality and never
flip-flop.

## Background: two layers

- **Layer 1 - ActivityState** (Working / WaitingForInput / WaitingForPerm / Idle /
  Exited): the "is it working?" truth. In the default terminal-driven mode the
  `TerminalStateDetector` (a quiet-gate + LLM judge) is the single authority; hooks
  are inert.
- **Layer 2 - StatusColor** (green/blue/yellow/red dot + NEEDS-YOU banner): derived
  from Layer 1 plus buffer scans and the turn-summary slow path, written by
  `SessionStatusWingman`.

Most "wrong color" bugs are one layer disagreeing with the other, or two writers
fighting.

## Already shipped (this pass, both on main)

- **#136 Fix A + B** (commit b1d37c9): the turn-summary `needs_user` classifier now
  has the `ClaudeCodeScreenReference` footer teaching, and `ApplyTurnSummary` no
  longer flips an idle (WaitingForInput/Idle) session red unless the buffer shows
  deterministic corroboration (a real question marker, [y/n]/numbered box, or the
  interrupted footer). Fixes the reported "idle session shows red NEEDS-YOU and
  flip-flops" bug.
- **#136 Fix C** (commit 72f84ed): `Session.SetStatusColor` replaced last-writer-wins
  with source precedence. New `StatusColorSource` (Inferred < ActivityState <
  PositiveEvidence) + an activity "generation"; within a generation a
  positive-evidence verdict is sticky and a lower-confidence write cannot repaint
  over it; a real state change releases it.

161 Wingman unit tests pass. The live idle-window end-to-end repro was blocked (see
item 8 below).

## Outstanding work (prioritized)

### 1. Trigger starvation of the LLM state judge  [HIGH]
The `TerminalStateDetector` quiet gate resets its 5s countdown on ANY byte
(`OnBytes`). A continuously-repainting statusline or a long-running hook (observed:
`running sp hook` with a frozen "2s" elapsed counter for minutes) keeps the gate
"active" forever, so the LLM judge is never re-consulted and a stale `Working`
verdict is pinned. The badge then never returns to idle.
- Correct fix: make the quiet gate **content-aware off the rendered terminal grid**
  (the resolved on-screen cells), NOT `_buffer.DumpAll()` raw bytes (old frames,
  incl. a stale "esc to interrupt", linger concatenated and defeat diffing). Ignore
  the spinner/footer rows; treat a live "esc to interrupt"/spinner as a
  working-override so a genuinely quiet tool call (e.g. `sleep 30`) is not misread as
  done.
- A naive forced-re-judge alone is NOT enough: `OnBytes` re-promotes to Working on
  the next cosmetic byte, producing a ~60s-period flip-flop. Must be combined with
  the content gate.
- Prerequisite: reproduce the starvation deterministically first (the one live
  observation may have been a legitimately-running hook), then validate against it.

### 2. OnBytes promotes Working on any byte (no burst threshold)  [HIGH, part of 1]
The state detector's `OnBytes` treats a single byte as activity. The
`OutputActivityWatcher` already uses a min-burst threshold (32 bytes) to filter
cosmetic ticks; the detector should use an equivalent content/burst filter so cursor
blinks and status-line redraws do not (re-)promote Working.

### 3. Two independent LLM classifiers can disagree  [MEDIUM]
The terminal-state classifier (`ClassifyTerminalState*`) and the turn-summary
`needs_user` classifier are separate LLM calls reading the same screen with different
prompts; they can reach conflicting verdicts (this is part of what made the badge and
the summary text contradict each other). Target: one authoritative state verdict that
BOTH the color and the turn summary derive from, instead of each re-deciding.
Relates to #129.

### 4. Generation-threading for async color writes  [MEDIUM]
Fix C added an activity "generation" but only uses it to release stickiness.
`ApplyTurnSummary` (computed ~10s after a turn ends) still relies on a state-based
stale guard ("skip if now Working/WaitingForPerm"). Cleaner: capture the generation
at turn-end, thread it through `TurnSummaryCache`, and drop the write if the session
has moved to a newer generation by the time the summary lands. Closes the stale-write
race principled-ly.

### 5. Interrupted / cancelled as a first-class state  [MEDIUM] (overlaps #129)
User presses Esc -> "Interrupted ... What should Claude do instead?". Today this is
only a buffer-marker stopgap (added in Fix B so it corroborates red). It should be a
real distinct state end-to-end, per #129.

### 6. Tag WaitingForPerm fast-path red as PositiveEvidence  [LOW]
The fast-path WaitingForPerm -> red write is currently `StatusColorSource.ActivityState`
and relies on the `OutputActivityWatcher` guards for protection. Tag it
PositiveEvidence for consistency with the other authoritative-red writers.

### 7. Turn the #131 misclassification reports into regression fixtures  [LOW]
The user-submitted corrections in #131 are a free labeled corpus. Convert them into
state-classifier regression fixtures so fixes are measured, not vibes.

### 8. Live end-to-end stability verification  [LOW, after 1]
The unit tests cover A/B/C, but the live idle-window repro was blocked this pass
(test sessions kept getting pinned Working - see item 1). Once the trigger fix lands,
re-run a live end-to-end confirming the badge reaches idle and stays stable through
the turn-summary window without flipping.

## References
- #129 - foundational state detection (working/interrupted/completed/waiting)
- #131 - misclassification report stream
- #136 - idle flip-flop to red (A/B/C shipped; trigger fix = item 1 here)

comments.body:
Closing as obsolete. This backlog was filed 2026-05-24 against the OLD state-detection architecture (LLM screen-judge + grid diffing + footer parsing + the hook subsystem). That architecture was removed the next day:

- 2026-05-25 (50aa8e7) feat(wingman): terminal-only finish detection -- replaced the LLM judge with a deliberately dumb time-based detector (any byte = Working; complete silence for QuietThreshold = needs-you; no footer parsing, no grid diffing, no LLM judge).
- 6064e1d refactor(state): remove the Claude Code hook subsystem; terminal detection is the only state source.

Item 1/2 (LLM-judge starvation, content-aware grid gate) no longer applies -- there is no LLM judge in the detector. The remaining items either landed (item 3, commit 6f8ccaa) or describe machinery that no longer exists. The current behavior IS the simple timer this issue was trying to reach. Reopen with a fresh, current-tree description if a specific misdetection is observed.

title: Wingman / session-state detection: consolidated hardening backlog

body:
## Summary

On a session sitting idle at the Claude Code prompt (turn finished), the session
view shows a RED "NEEDS YOU" / "ACTION NEEDED" banner whose detail is the literal
mode footer `bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle) ... for agents`, while the
SAME view's SUMMARY correctly says "WHAT CLAUDE WANTS: Nothing pending. Waiting for
you to give it a task." The badge color also flops back and forth between red and
green. The agent is NOT actually blocked on a permission prompt.

This is the footer-misread bug we thought was handled, but the fix was applied to a
different classifier than the one driving the color here. Related to #129
(foundational state detection) and #131 (misclassification reports). This issue is
the specific root-cause + fix plan.

## Repro / observed

- Session at the idle prompt, bypass-permissions mode. Footer:
  `bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle) ... for agents`
- Session view simultaneously shows:
  - RED "NEEDS YOU" banner; "ACTION NEEDED: bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle) ... for agents"
  - SUMMARY -> "WHAT CLAUDE WANTS: Nothing pending. Waiting for you to give it a task."
  - LAST TURN -> "Agent completed ... and awaits permission to commit" / "Waiting for permission approval to commit to main"
- Color visibly oscillates red <-> green.

## Root cause (code, current tree)

1. The fast path is CORRECT and stays green.
   `SessionStatusWingman.ColorFromActivityState`: `WaitingForInput -> green`.
   `PromotePendingQuestionIfBufferShowsOne` scans `QuestionMarkers`; none match the
   bypass-permissions footer, so it does not promote. The red is NOT from here.

2. The RED comes from the turn-summary slow path.
   `SessionStatusWingman.ApplyTurnSummary` paints red when the Haiku `needs_user` is
   `question | error | permission`. Summarizing a finished turn whose tail shows the
   idle prompt + the bypass-permissions footer + the agent's own prose ("say the
   word when you want it on main" / "awaits permission to commit"), Haiku sets
   `needs_user = permission/question`.

3. The footer-mode teaching is missing from the turn-summary classifier.
   `WingmanService.ClaudeCodeScreenReference` (the "bypass permissions on ... is a
   MODE footer, never waiting_for_permission" teaching + few-shot examples) is
   injected ONLY into the terminal-STATE classifier prompts (BuildClassify*).
   `BuildTurnSummaryPrompt` (the `needs_user` path actually driving this red) does
   NOT include it. Its `needs_user='permission'` rule is only "agent paused for an
   OS-level permission prompt (Yes/No, [y/n])" with no instruction to ignore the
   mode footer and no defense against conversational "permission to commit" language.

4. ApplyTurnSummary overrides a correctly-green idle session.
   It skips only when `ActivityState` is `WaitingForPerm` or `Working`
   (SessionStatusWingman.cs ~287-291). It does NOT skip for `WaitingForInput`/`Idle`,
   so a green idle session gets repainted red by a misread summary.

5. Last-writer-wins => the flip-flop.
   SessionStatusWingman documents "writes are unconditional, last-event-wins"
   (~lines 47-50). The fast path keeps writing green (state transitions, buffer
   re-scans); the slow path writes red (each turn-summary refresh, e.g. when the
   mobile view polls / re-asks the wingman). Whichever fired most recently wins, so
   the badge oscillates.

## Has it been fixed?

Partially. The footer teaching exists for the terminal-STATE classifier (the
earlier ClaudeCodeScreenReference work). It was never added to the turn-summary
`needs_user` classifier that paints the color here, and there is no precedence rule
stopping a misread LLM summary from overriding a fresh deterministic green idle. Not
fixed for this path.

## Proposed fix (not yet implemented)

A. Teach the turn-summary classifier the same footer rule.
   Append `ClaudeCodeScreenReference` (or a condensed footer rule) to
   `BuildTurnSummaryPrompt`, and tighten `needs_user`:
   - The persistent mode footer ("bypass permissions on", "accept edits on", "plan
     mode on", "shift+tab to cycle") is NEVER `needs_user`.
   - Conversational offers ("I can commit when you're ready", "say the word") are
     `needs_user='no'` -- an offer, not a blocking question.
   - Only a bordered numbered-choice box or `[y/n]` is `permission`; a trailing
     question mark is `question`.

B. Make the deterministic state the tie-breaker (highest-leverage minimal fix).
   In `ApplyTurnSummary`, do NOT promote to red when the current `ActivityState` is
   `WaitingForInput`/`Idle` unless corroborated by deterministic positive evidence
   (a `QuestionMarker` hit, a real bordered/`[y/n]` permission box, or the
   "Interrupted ... What should Claude do instead?" footer). The summary may still
   set the reason/headline, but it must not flip an idle session to red on its own.
   This kills both the false red and the oscillation in one move.

C. (Durable stabilization) Replace pure last-writer-wins with source precedence.
   Tag each color write with a source (deterministic-fast vs inferred-LLM) and a
   monotonic sequence; let positive-evidence sources win over inferred ones instead
   of "whatever wrote last". This is the structural fix for "stabilize the wingman".

B alone resolves the reported symptom; A stops the LLM generating the false signal;
C is the durable architecture change.

## Files

- `src/CcDirector.Core/Wingman/SessionStatusWingman.cs` (ApplyTurnSummary, ColorFromActivityState, QuestionMarkers)
- `src/CcDirector.Core/Wingman/WingmanService.cs` (ClaudeCodeScreenReference, BuildTurnSummaryPrompt, needs_user rules)

comments.body:
Status: the reported root cause is fixed and on main.

- Fix A + Fix B (commit b1d37c9): turn-summary classifier taught the bypass-permissions footer rule; ApplyTurnSummary no longer flips an idle session red without deterministic corroboration.
- Fix C (commit 72f84ed): SetStatusColor now arbitrates by source precedence (Inferred < ActivityState < PositiveEvidence) with a per-generation sticky rule, replacing last-writer-wins.

161 Wingman unit tests pass. The remaining piece (the LLM state judge being starved by a continuously-repainting statusline/hook, which pins a stale Working verdict) is tracked with the full backlog in #137.
Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: Wingman flip-flops red NEEDS-YOU vs green idle: turn-summary needs_user misreads the bypass-permissions footer as a pending gate

body:
## Summary

While reviewing three screenshots of the remote web UI (wingman testing session), we found behavior that does not match the current source tree. This is a placeholder bug report to investigate later and confirm whether it is a real issue or just an old build that was being hit.

NOTE: This was a code/screenshot review only. Nothing was changed and the running build was not rebuilt. Needs verification against a fresh build.

## What was observed (running build)

Three screens captured:

1. **Directory page** - header reads "1 sessions (0 idle, 0 working, 1 waiting)", session card shows a raw enum pill "WaitingForInput", "started 11m ago", and a colored left border. No "Open session" button.
2. **Intermediate session-detail page** - "Back to sessions", "Rename", "Open raw view", "Interrupt", a "WHAT WAS DONE / Refresh recap" block ("No recap yet..."), and a "SEND NEXT COMMAND" box. Status pill: "WaitingForInput".
3. **Wingman view** - green "READY" badge, "Mobile: ON", SUMMARY (WHATS HAPPENED / WHAT CLAUDE WANTS), "ASK THE WINGMAN", "STATUS WRONG? TELL US" vote feedback, and "WINGMAN LOG". Status: "READY".

## Two concerns

### 1. Intermediate screen in the click path
Clicking a session appears to land on the old `manager.html` session-detail page (Refresh recap / Send next command / Open raw view) before reaching the rich wingman view.

In current source, the directory "Open session ->" button links to `s.viewUrl` = `/sessions/{sid}/view` (`GatewayEndpoints.cs:249`), which serves `session-view.html` (the green READY wingman view) directly. So the intermediate page should no longer be in the path.

### 2. Status vocabulary mismatch
- Directory + old manager page show the raw Claude Code enum: **WaitingForInput**.
- New wingman view shows the `TerminalStateDetector` vocabulary: **READY** (green).

The same session shows two different statuses depending on the page.

## Likely cause (to confirm)

The running build looks stale. The directory page in the screenshot generates HTML that no longer exists in the source:

| Running build (screenshot) | Current source (`directory.html`) |
|---|---|
| "1 sessions (0 idle, 0 working, 1 waiting)" | "N sessions / R red / Y yellow" (`renderHeader`, ~line 384) |
| Raw "WaitingForInput" pill | Colored status dot + humanized state (`statusColorOf` / `humanizeState`) |
| "started 11m ago" | Relative last-activity time |
| No button | "Open session ->" button to the view URL |

Commit `0ed592a` ("terminal-only state detection") moved the directory onto the terminal-state/color model so it agrees with the wingman view.

Additionally, `directory.html` and `session-view.html` are currently **modified/uncommitted** in the working tree, so even the latest committed build would not carry these edits - only a fresh build from the current tree would.

## How to verify

1. Do a fresh local build from the current working tree (slot 4+) and launch via the `cc-director-launch` scheduled task, served over the tailnet (not localhost).
2. Re-check the three flows:
   - Directory header should read "N sessions / R red / Y yellow" with colored dots, not "0 idle/working/waiting".
   - Clicking "Open session" should go straight to the green READY wingman view (no intermediate recap/send-command page).
   - Directory status and wingman-view status should use the same vocabulary.

If all three hold on a fresh build, this is just an old-build artifact and can be closed.

comments.body: Confirmed old-build artifact. The terminal-state/color directory model (0ed592a) is committed: directory.html carries humanizeState/statusColorOf and the N sessions / R red / Y yellow header, and the directory 'Open session' links straight to /sessions/{sid}/view (session-view.html), so the intermediate manager page is no longer in the path. Both directory.html and session-view.html are committed (no longer dirty in the tree). User rebuilt and restarted (fresh build over the tailnet), and the blink/repaint loop is gone. Closing.

## Confirmed: this is a real source issue, not a stale build

Investigated against the current tree. The intermediate session-detail page (Back to sessions / Rename / Open raw view / Interrupt / WHAT WAS DONE / SEND NEXT COMMAND) is still in the source for the **Director's** UI.

Root cause:
- The Gateway was migrated to serve `directory.html` at "/" (clicking a card goes straight to `/sessions/{id}/view`); the old `manager.html` was moved to `/legacy-manager`.
- The **Director (ControlApi, port 7879)** was never migrated. `ControlEndpoints.cs` still serves `manager.html` at "/", and that page intercepts a card click with `enterDetailMode()`, showing the recap/send-command page instead of opening the session.

So when hitting a Director directly over the tailnet (port 7879), the intermediate screen is exactly what you get. That is what the screenshots showed.

## Fix

In `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/Web/manager.html`:
- Card click now navigates directly to `/sessions/{id}/view` (the real session view), matching the Gateway directory behavior.
- Removed the entire detail-mode subsystem (HTML markup + JS: enterDetailMode/exitDetailMode/rename/interrupt/recap/send-command, plus now-unused recap-markdown helper).
- List view, New Session, fan-out, and Voice are unchanged.

ControlApi builds clean (0 warnings). Pending: fresh build + tailnet verification that clicking a session lands straight on the session view with no intermediate page.

Verified against current source - this was a stale running build, not a real bug. All three criteria the issue itself set for closure now hold:

1. **Directory header uses the terminal-state vocabulary.** `directory.html:384-387` (`renderHeader`) counts `reds` and `yellows` via `statusColorOf`, matching the wingman view.
2. **Click path goes straight to the wingman view.** `directory.html:424-425` links to `s.viewUrl`, which `GatewayEndpoints.cs:253` resolves to `/sessions/{sid}/view` (`session-view.html`). No intermediate `manager.html` step.
3. **Status vocabulary matches.** Directory and wingman view both use `statusColorOf` + `humanizeState` against the same color/state model (commit 0ed592a, "terminal-only state detection").

Closing as a stale-build artifact.

title: Remote web UI: stale build shows intermediate session page + mismatched status vocabularies

body:
@
## Problem

Recording can miss the beginning of speech (and risks missing the end) in both the browser voice recorder and the desktop Dictate overlay. The UI tells the user they can talk before the audio pipeline is actually capturing, so words spoken in the first fraction of a second are dropped.

Observed in the browser recorder (no repro artifact, but consistently "loses the first word or two"). The same race exists by inspection in the desktop overlay.

## Desired behavior

Never signal "go" until capture is verifiably live. Introduce an explicit state sequence on BOTH surfaces:

1. **Initializing** - shown immediately on tap. Mic permission, stream, encoder/worklet, and (desktop) the server session are being set up. The user is told NOT to speak yet.
2. **Ready / Recording** - shown ONLY after the capture pipeline is confirmed producing audio (first encoded chunk / first worklet frame actually emitted, not merely "start() was called"). This is when the user is invited to speak.

Symmetrically on stop: flush the tail (request the final data/flush the encoder and wait for the last chunk) before tearing the stream down, so the END is not clipped either.

## Where it lives

### Desktop: `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/Web/dictate-client.js`
- `stage` defaults to `recording` (line ~176) and the overlay renders "recording" + "(your words will appear here)" the moment it opens.
- Actual capture only starts after a WebSocket round-trip: `ready` -> send `start` -> on `started` it calls `bootCapture()` (lines ~324-332). `bootCapture()` then does `getUserMedia` + adds the audio worklet (lines ~288-313).
- So between overlay-open and the first PCM frame reaching the server there is a real gap where the UI says "recording" but nothing is streamed.

### Browser: `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/Web/session-view.html` -> `startRecording()` (line ~2521)
- The whole function awaits `getUserMedia` (line ~2548) with the overlay/"Recording..." only shown afterward, and there is no "Initializing" feedback during the await.
- `mediaRecorder.start()` (line ~2578) is followed immediately by inviting the user to speak; MediaRecorder has a startup warm-up before the first `ondataavailable`, so the leading audio can be lost.
- `stopAndSend()` / `mediaRecorder.onstop` (lines ~2575-2598) should ensure a final flush so the trailing audio is captured.

## Suggested fix direction

- Add an `initializing` stage/UI to both overlays (distinct label + the bars/timer NOT yet "live"; tell the user to wait).
- Browser: flip to "Recording" only after the first `ondataavailable` chunk fires (or `mediaRecorder.onstart`), not before. Consider `mediaRecorder.start(timeslice)` so chunks flow promptly. On stop, call `requestData()`/await the final `dataavailable` before stopping tracks.
- Desktop: flip from `initializing` to `recording` only after `bootCapture()` has succeeded AND the worklet has emitted its first frame to the WebSocket - not on the `started` message alone. Keep streaming until the final flush completes on stop.
- No fallback that silently proceeds when setup is slow; if init fails, show the explicit error (existing error paths are fine).

## Acceptance

- On both surfaces, tapping to record first shows "Initializing" and the user is not told to speak.
- The "Ready/Recording" signal appears only once audio frames are actually being captured/streamed.
- A word spoken immediately after the "Recording" signal appears is present in the transcript/recording.
- Audio spoken right up to the stop tap is present (end not clipped).
@

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: Voice/dictation clips speech at start (and risks clipping the end): show Initializing then Ready, only after capture is confirmed live

body:
@
## Problem

In the desktop **Dictate** overlay (`src/CcDirector.ControlApi/Web/dictate.html`), the live audio level indicator is too small and too unresponsive to be useful. While recording you cannot tell whether you are speaking loudly or softly because the bars hardly move. The in-browser/web recording visualization is much nicer because it is bigger and the amplitude swing is clearly visible.

The goal: make the Dictate meter bigger and give it more amplitude range so loud vs. quiet speech is obvious at a glance.

## Where it lives

A 9-bar equalizer driven by a WebAudio analyser.

Sizing (`dictate.html`):
```css
/* line ~111 */
.equalizer { display: flex; align-items: flex-end; gap: 5px; height: 56px; flex: 1; justify-content: center; }
/* line ~119 */
.equalizer .bar { width: 8px; background: #555; border-radius: 3px; height: 6px; transition: height 60ms linear, background 120ms; }
```

Analyser setup (`dictate.html` line ~429):
```js
levelAnalyser.fftSize = 64;
levelAnalyser.smoothingTimeConstant = 0.5;
```

Per-frame height math (`dictate.html` line ~444):
```js
const maxHeight = 50;
const minHeight = 6;
...
const v = levelData[bandIdx] / 255;   // linear, no gain
const h = minHeight + v * (maxHeight - minHeight);
eqBars[i].style.height = h.toFixed(0) + px;
```

## Why it barely moves

- The mapping is a flat linear `byte/255`. Normal speech frequency-bin bytes rarely approach 255, so the bars only travel a few pixels of the 6..50px range.
- The container is only 56px tall and bars are 8px wide, so even the full range is physically small on screen.
- No gain/normalization or non-linear (e.g. sqrt) curve to make quiet/normal speech visible.

## Suggested fix direction

1. Make it physically bigger: taller `.equalizer` (e.g. ~90-110px), wider bars, larger `maxHeight`.
2. Add amplitude gain / a response curve so normal speech fills more of the range, e.g. apply a multiplier and/or a sqrt curve to `v`, and clamp to 1:
   ```js
   const gain = 2.2;                       // tune
   const v = Math.min(1, Math.sqrt((levelData[bandIdx] / 255)) * gain);
   ```
3. Optionally drive the meter from time-domain RMS (`getByteTimeDomainData`) instead of a single frequency bin for a more loudness-faithful response, mirroring whatever the web version does.

Tune the gain so a normal speaking voice clearly swings the bars and a loud voice nears the top, without pinning to max constantly.

## Acceptance

- While recording in the Dictate overlay, speaking at a normal volume visibly moves the bars through a large fraction of their height; speaking louder vs. softer is clearly distinguishable.
- The meter is noticeably larger on screen than today.
@

comments.body:
Implemented in `src/CcDirector.ControlApi/Web/dictate.html`. All four asks now match the file exactly:

- `.equalizer` height `100px` (was 56), bar width `12px` (was 8) - lines 111-125.
- `maxHeight = 92` (was 50), `minHeight = 8` - line 449-450.
- Response curve: `v = Math.min(1, Math.sqrt(levelData[bandIdx] / 255) * gain)` with `gain = 2.2` - line 451-457, exactly the curve the issue suggested.

Normal speech now visibly fills most of the bar height; loud vs. soft is clearly distinguishable. Closing.

title: Dictate overlay: audio level meter too small / barely moves; amplitude needs more gain and size

body:
@
## Problem

On the session-view page (`src/CcDirector.ControlApi/Web/session-view.html`), the **Session** tab cannot scroll its last elements fully into view. The bottom of the page (the "STATUS WRONG? TELL US" details block, specifically its **Send feedback** button) ends up underneath the fixed bottom input bar (`Type / Esc / Upload / Speak / Send`) and is clipped, no matter how far you scroll.

Reproduced on desktop Brave; affects any narrow/short viewport (phones) too.

## Root cause

`#sessionView` uses a flat padding with no bottom allowance for the fixed input bar:

```css
/* session-view.html line ~185 */
#sessionView { padding: 14px; max-width: 1000px; margin: 0 auto; }
```

Meanwhile the fixed input bar is `position: fixed; bottom: 0` (`form.send`, line ~722) and overlaps page content.

The other two views already account for this and reserve bottom space:

```css
#agentView { ... padding-bottom: calc(80px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom)); }   /* line ~512 */
#voiceView { ... padding-bottom: calc(96px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom)); }   /* line ~796 */
```

Only `#sessionView` is missing the bottom padding, so its tail content scrolls under the fixed bar.

## Fix

Give `#sessionView` the same bottom padding the other views use, e.g.:

```css
#sessionView {
  padding: 14px;
  padding-bottom: calc(80px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom));
  max-width: 1000px;
  margin: 0 auto;
}
```

(Match the input bars actual height; 80px mirrors `#agentView`. Verify the "Send feedback" button clears the bar on both desktop and a phone-width viewport.)

## Acceptance

- On the Session tab, scrolling to the bottom fully reveals the "Status wrong? Tell us" block including the "Send feedback" button, with clearance above the fixed input bar.
- Verified on desktop and a <=600px viewport.
@

comments.body: Fixed in 325ef16. `#sessionView` now has `padding-bottom: calc(80px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom))` (session-view.html:185), matching the other views, so the 'Status wrong? Tell us' block and Send feedback button clear the fixed input bar. Verified in committed source and fresh build.

title: Session view: bottom content (Status-wrong feedback block) hidden behind fixed input bar
