title: Wingman terminal-state detector: misclassification reports

body:
## Vision

A voice mode so the user can talk to a single Claude Code session hands-free-ish
from the car. The mobile Voice tab is NOT a general chat box -- it is a voice
front-end to the Wingman. You speak to the session; when the session finishes a
turn, the Wingman summarizes it cleanly and we speak that summary back so you can
make a decision without looking at the screen.

Not building this now. This issue records the design, the decisions, and what has
already shipped, so we can pick it up later without re-deriving everything.

## The interaction model (decided with the user)

Walkie-talkie, manual on both ends -- deliberately NOT fully hands-free for v1:

1. Tap the big button to start recording (tap-to-talk).
2. Speak.
3. Tap again to send (tap-to-send). No VAD, no auto-pause / silence detection.
   The user explicitly dislikes the pausing/automatic behavior.
4. Transcript goes into the session (Whisper STT + Wingman transcript cleanup).
5. Session works; when the turn completes the Wingman produces a spoken-friendly
   summary.
6. We speak that summary back (what happened + what Claude wants), summarized so
   the user can decide while driving.
7. Tap to talk to answer. Loop repeats.

Car-grade UI: much bigger buttons than the current Voice tab. Mobile-only.

## Key architectural decision: voice is a MODE on the session

The Wingman and the session run IN-PROCESS in the same CC Director app. They share
state in memory; there is no network between them. So "notify when the turn is
done" is just the Wingman writing a field and the session reading it -- do NOT
over-engineer this with SSE/push channels. The only thing crossing HTTP is the
phone reading what the Wingman already produced in memory (a cheap read).

It is a mode the session enters, driven by which mobile tab is active:

- Session tab  -> Text mode  -> Wingman writes normal text remarks.
- Voice tab    -> Voice mode -> Wingman writes spoken-friendly remarks.
- Switching back to the Session tab returns to Text mode.

The Wingman keys its remark STYLE off the session's mode.

## What has already shipped (commit daf1eab, on main)

The session-flag foundation is done and pushed:

- `Session.cs`: `MobileViewMode { Off, Text, Voice }` enum + `ViewMode` property as
  the single source of truth. `MobileMode` (=> ViewMode != Off) and `VoiceMode`
  (=> ViewMode == Voice) are derived. Not persisted (tracks what a remote viewer
  is looking at right now).
- `ControlEndpoints.cs`: `/mobile-mode` now drives `ViewMode` (Text/Off); new
  `POST /sessions/{sid}/voice-mode` drives it (Voice/Text) and warms the briefing
  cache via ProactiveExplainService.
- `ProactiveExplainService.cs`: `VoiceModeRequest` record.
- `session-view.html`: the tab-switch handler POSTs `/voice-mode` so the active
  tab sets the session's mode (Voice tab -> voice on, any other tab -> off/text).

## What remains (the actual voice-mode work, later)

1. Wingman reads `session.VoiceMode` and switches its remark style to
   spoken-friendly (no tables, no file paths/commands, conversational, short
   enough to listen to). This is the one remaining integration point and lives in
   `WingmanService` (the explain-prompt path / `BuildExplainPrompt` /
   `AskAboutSessionAsync`). Left to the Wingman work deliberately to avoid
   cross-contaminating an in-flight Wingman fix.
2. Voice tab consumes and SPEAKS the voice remark on turn completion. Today the
   Voice tab is self-contained and does not read the Wingman briefing. It should
   poll the in-memory cache (cheap) and TTS the spoken summary when a new turn
   lands.
3. Car-grade UI rebuild: much bigger Tap-to-talk / Send buttons, clear spoken
   state, minimal visual clutter. The old Voice tab was built long ago and most of
   it can be ripped out.
4. Quick replies by voice: the Wingman explain path already emits tap-to-answer
   quick replies. In the car, speak the options ("you can say: approve, or stop")
   and also show them as big buttons.

## Reusable plumbing already in the codebase

- Whisper STT: `VoiceService.TranscribeAsync()` + `/voice/command`.
- Transcript cleanup: `WingmanService.CleanVoiceTranscriptAsync()`.
- TTS with chunking + SpeechSynthesis fallback: `TtsService` + `/tts`.
- Proactive briefing cache warmed on mode change: `ProactiveExplainService`
  (already gated on `MobileMode`, which Voice mode also turns on).
- Audio cues (WebAudio tones) in `session-view.html`.
- `TurnSummary.SpokenText` (already TTS-friendly) and `NeedsUser` / `NeedsUserShort`.

## Hard dependency

This is only as good as the Wingman's session-state detection. If the Wingman
cannot reliably tell working / completed / waiting-for-question / interrupted /
idle apart, the spoken summary is garbage in, garbage out. See #129
(reliable session state detection) -- that must work first.

## Notes

- Deferred: do not implement yet. Recorded for future reference.
- v1 scope: single session, conversational, manual tap-to-talk/tap-to-send.
  Voice command layer (switch sessions by voice, "what's waiting") is later.

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: Implement Voice Mode for Session Interaction in Car

body:
## Summary

The Session Wingman is only useful if it reliably knows what STATE a Claude Code
session is in. Right now state detection is incomplete: in particular, it does not
recognize the "Interrupted / cancelled" state (when the user presses Esc mid-turn).

This issue tracks making Wingman state detection robust and well-tested. This is
foundational: if state detection is wrong, every downstream Wingman feature
(turn summaries, spoken voice remarks, "what Claude wants", goal assessment,
notifications) is also wrong. Garbage in, garbage out.

## What triggered this

User interrupted a session mid-turn (pressed Esc). The Claude Code terminal footer
showed:

```
Interrupted <middot> What should Claude do instead?
```

(the separator is a middle-dot glyph). The Wingman did not register this as a
distinct state. "Interrupted / cancelled" is one of the states Wingman MUST know
about.

## The states Wingman needs to distinguish (not exhaustive)

- Working / running (agent is actively generating or running tools)
- Completed (turn finished cleanly, agent went back to the prompt)
- Waiting for a question (agent asked the user something and is blocked on an answer)
- Waiting for permission (agent is blocked on a tool/permission prompt)
- Interrupted / cancelled (user pressed Esc; agent stopped mid-turn -> the
  "Interrupted ... What should Claude do instead?" footer)
- Idle (nothing pending; never started or fully done)

These map loosely to the existing `ActivityState` enum and `TurnSummary.NeedsUser`
field, but the detection that drives them is what needs hardening. In particular,
"Interrupted" is currently not a first-class state.

## The detection problem (how does Wingman actually KNOW?)

We need to nail down the mechanism, not guess. Candidate signals to investigate:

- Terminal output scraping: Claude Code injects spinner / status characters while
  it is working. Detecting that animation (or its absence) is a possible "working
  vs not working" signal. Need to confirm what characters/sequences it injects and
  whether they are stable across Claude Code versions.
- Footer / status-line markers: the "Interrupted ... What should Claude do instead?"
  line is a concrete string we can match to detect the interrupted state. There are
  likely other footer strings for other states (permission prompts, "esc to
  interrupt", etc.) we should catalog.
- Hook events: UserPromptSubmit / Stop hooks already drive turn-completion. Confirm
  whether an interrupt fires (or fails to fire) a Stop hook, and whether we can
  detect the interrupt purely from hooks or whether we must read the terminal.
- Trailing question detection for "waiting for a question".

We should pick a primary signal per state and document why, rather than stacking
fragile heuristics.

## Why this matters / acceptance

- Each state above is detected correctly and promptly.
- "Interrupted / cancelled" becomes a first-class state the Wingman reports.
- A LOT of testing: this needs a test matrix that exercises each transition
  (working -> interrupted, working -> completed, completed -> waiting-for-question,
  permission prompt, idle, etc.) with real Claude Code sessions, because the whole
  Wingman value chain depends on it.

## Notes

- Deferred: filing this now to track it; we will design and implement later.
- Related code: `CcDirector.Core/Wingman/WingmanService.cs`,
  `ActivityState`, `TurnSummary` (`NeedsUser`), the Stop / UserPromptSubmit hook
  wiring, and the terminal buffer that Wingman reads.

comments.body: Closing as stale. Terminal-only state detection is the clear, single source now (commit 6064e1d removed the Claude Code hook subsystem), the detector has a test suite (`TerminalStateDetectorTests`, `SessionStatusWingmanTests`, `StateChangeLogTests`, `StatusColorTests`, `TurnSummaryCacheTests`), and the broader "foundational and untested" framing no longer applies. If the Interrupted/cancelled state turns out to matter in practice, a focused successor issue can be filed for that specific gap.

title: Wingman: reliable session state detection (working / interrupted / completed / waiting) is foundational and untested

body:
@
## Summary

`cc-settings` crashes immediately on any invocation. The frozen (PyInstaller) build is missing the `mdit_py_plugins` module, which is pulled in transitively via `cc_shared.markdown_parser`.

## Reproduction

```
cc-settings get screenshots.source_directory
cc-settings path
```

## Actual output

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "main.py", line 3, in <module>
  File "pyimod02_importers.py", line 457, in exec_module
  File "src\cli.py", line 11, in <module>
  File "pyimod02_importers.py", line 457, in exec_module
  File "src\settings.py", line 12, in <module>
  File "pyimod02_importers.py", line 457, in exec_module
  File "cc_shared\__init__.py", line 14, in <module>
  File "pyimod02_importers.py", line 457, in exec_module
  File "cc_shared\markdown_parser.py", line 11, in <module>
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named mdit_py_plugins
[PYI-85360:ERROR] Failed to execute script main due to unhandled exception!
```

## Impact

- Every `cc-settings` subcommand fails (get/set/path all crash before doing anything).
- The settings surface (e.g. `screenshots.source_directory`) is unreadable from the CLI. Consumers that need a setting currently have to resolve it another way.

## Likely cause / fix

`cc_shared/__init__.py` eagerly imports `markdown_parser`, which imports `mdit_py_plugins`. The PyInstaller spec for `cc-settings` does not bundle `mdit_py_plugins` (and its submodules). Options:

1. Add `mdit_py_plugins` (and submodules) to `hiddenimports` in the cc-settings PyInstaller spec, or
2. Make `cc_shared/__init__.py` import `markdown_parser` lazily so tools that do not need markdown do not drag in the dependency.

Option 2 is preferable: `cc-settings` has no reason to pull in a markdown parser at import time.

## Environment

- Windows 11
- Frozen build on PATH at %LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\bin\cc-settings.exe
@

comments.body:
@
## Fixed

**Root cause:** `cc_shared/__init__.py` eagerly imported `markdown_parser`, which imports `markdown_it` + `mdit_py_plugins`. Importing any sibling submodule (e.g. `cc_shared.config`, all cc-settings needs) runs the package `__init__.py` first, so cc-settings dragged in `mdit_py_plugins` even though it never parses markdown. The cc-settings PyInstaller build did not bundle `mdit_py_plugins`, so it crashed at import time.

**Fix:** Made `markdown_parser` lazy in `cc_shared/__init__.py` via PEP 562 module `__getattr__`. `cc_shared.parse_markdown` / `cc_shared.ParsedMarkdown` still resolve on demand, but the package no longer imports `markdown_it`/`mdit_py_plugins` at init time. A grep confirmed no tool actually references `parse_markdown` anywhere, so the eager import was pure dead weight.

**Verified:**
- `python -c "import cc_shared.config"` no longer loads `mdit_py_plugins` (confirmed via `sys.modules`); accessing `cc_shared.parse_markdown` still loads it lazily.
- Rebuilt `cc-settings.exe` (PyInstaller) and redeployed to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\bin`. `cc-settings get screenshots.source_directory` now returns `D:/Personal/OneDrive/Pictures/Screenshots` with exit 0 instead of crashing.

Note: the source change to `tools/cc_shared/__init__.py` is staged locally and not yet committed; the installed binary on this machine is already rebuilt and fixed.
@

title: cc-settings crashes on startup: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'mdit_py_plugins'

body:
## Summary

Build a scheduler inside cc-director Avalonia that picks up approved comm-queue items and dispatches them via the per-platform runner scripts (e.g. `scripts/linkedin-connect-from-queue.py`). No new Windows service, no nssm, no Administrator step - everything stays inside the Avalonia app the user already runs.

Multiple cc-director instances can run concurrently. A Windows named mutex (`Global\cc-director-scheduler`) elects exactly one leader. Followers re-poll every 30s and promote when the leader exits.

## Why now

The LinkedIn connection-request runner shipped today (2026-05-22) - `scripts/linkedin-connect-from-queue.py`, verified by sending three queue items (#171, #172, #173) end-to-end via browser-harness. Approved items currently require a human to invoke the runner. The scheduler closes that loop.

## Design doc

`docs/plans/comm-queue-scheduler.md` - full design, file list (~600 lines incl. tests), acceptance criteria, open questions.

## Why not the existing Python scheduler service

`scheduler/cc_director/` deployed via `nssm` was rejected for this use case: requires Administrator install step, runs as SYSTEM (sends would fire while the user is signed out), adds a second always-on process. May still be the right home for tasks that genuinely need machine-wide cron behavior; comm-queue sends are not one of those.

## Acceptance criteria (excerpt)

- Two Avalonia instances launched concurrently: exactly one is leader, the other is follower.
- Kill the leader: follower promotes within 60s.
- Approved LinkedIn connection-requests dispatched at the scheduled time with 0-60min jitter, marked posted by the runner.
- Zero new install steps - fresh user clone-and-runs.
- Nothing sends when no Avalonia instance is running.

## Related

- Runner shipped: `scripts/linkedin-connect-from-queue.py`
- Connection profile flow docs: `%LOCALAPPDATA%/cc-director/connections/linkedin/README.md` (updated 2026-05-22)

comments.body:
Implemented. Commit 8110e73 ("feat(scheduler): in-process comm-queue scheduler with UI, hot reload, leader handoff") landed the design from `docs/plans/comm-queue-scheduler.md`:

- `src/CcDirector.Core/Scheduler/CommQueueScheduler.cs` - picks up approved items and dispatches them.
- `src/CcDirector.Core/Scheduler/MutexLeaderElection.cs` + `LeaderIdentityStore.cs` - Windows named-mutex leader election with follower repoll/promotion.
- `src/CcDirector.Core/Scheduler/SchedulerService.cs` - the in-process service host.
- `src/CcDirector.Avalonia/Controls/SchedulerView.axaml` + `.cs` - the Avalonia UI.
- Tests: `LeaderIdentityStoreTests`, `MutexLeaderElectionTests`.

No new Windows service, no nssm, no Administrator step - everything runs inside the Avalonia app. Closing.

title: Comm-queue scheduler in cc-director (leader-elected, in-process)

body:
## Problem

The Speak dialog (`src/CcDirector.Avalonia/Voice/SpeakDialog.axaml`) only has Cancel and Stop. Once you click Speak, you are committed to a single uninterrupted recording. If you need to think, take a phone call, look something up, or just collect your thoughts, the only options are:

- Keep talking (and waste tokens / pollute the transcript with filler)
- Cancel and start over (lose what you already said)
- Stop early and re-open Speak to append more (two separate cleanup passes, two separate inserts)

None of these are good.

## Proposal

Add a third button to the SpeakDialog action bar: **Pause / Resume**.

### Recording state
- Action bar: `[hint text]  [Cancel] [Pause] [Stop]`
- Status label: RECORDING (red, as today)
- Equalizer: animated against live mic, as today

### Paused state
- Clicking Pause:
  - Suspends `MicAudioCapture` (stop the `WaveInEvent`, do NOT dispose the buffer or close the realtime session)
  - Equalizer freezes (or fades to a flat neutral)
  - Timer freezes
  - Status label: PAUSED (e.g. amber `#DCDCAA`)
  - Pause button becomes Resume
- Clicking Resume:
  - Restart `MicAudioCapture` feeding the same `DictationSession`
  - Status label flips back to RECORDING
  - Timer resumes from the paused value
- Cancel and Stop still work from the paused state (Stop runs cleanup on whatever was captured so far).

## Why this matters

Real dictation sessions are not single takes. Native Windows Dictation, every macOS dictation surface, and every voice memo app on a phone has Pause. We are competing with those.

## Scope

- `SpeakDialog.axaml`: third button + amber paused style
- `SpeakDialog.axaml.cs`: Pause/Resume click handler, state machine update (add `Stage.Paused`), freeze timer + equalizer
- `MicAudioCapture.cs`: expose `Pause()` / `Resume()` that stop/start the `WaveInEvent` without tearing down the device
- `SpeakService.cs`: pass-through Pause/Resume; do NOT close the realtime provider or audio buffer
- Browser parity: same Pause button in `dictate-client.js` overlay (`session-view.html` and any future surfaces that drop the client in)

## Out of scope (for this issue)

- Global hotkey for pause (separate work, tied to the system-wide hotkey feature)
- Auto-pause on silence

## Acceptance

- Speak dialog has Pause button while recording
- Pause freezes timer + equalizer, suspends mic capture, keeps the session/buffer alive
- Resume picks up the same session; final transcript contains BOTH chunks as a single cleaned output
- Browser overlay has the same Pause control

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: Dictation: add Pause button to SpeakDialog recording screen

body:
## Summary

Phase 1 (#123) made the Gateway machine-centric. That removed the proxy churn we wanted gone, but it also stripped out the session-level view that is the actual reason to look at the Gateway. Phase 2 puts sessions back at the center while keeping Phase 1's "Director is canonical" principle.

The Gateway becomes the **network-wide hub** where every running agent session across the fleet is visible at a glance, with deep-links to each session's own view on its owning Director. Same data is exposed as a clean public `GET /sessions` API for Claude Code (or any other consumer) to talk to.

## Plan

Full plan: [docs/architecture/gateway/GATEWAY_SESSION_VIEW_PLAN.md](https://github.com/thefrederiksen/cc-director/blob/main/docs/architecture/gateway/GATEWAY_SESSION_VIEW_PLAN.md)

## Scope

- **Slice 1** — Add `LastActivityAt` (terminal-buffer write timestamp on `SessionDto`)
- **Slice 2** — Stamp `MachineName`, `User`, `TailnetEndpoint`, `ViewUrl` on every session from the Gateway aggregator
- **Slice 3** — Parallel fan-out with per-Director timeout; `{ sessions, machineErrors }` response envelope; new filter params (`statusColor`, `machine`, `includeExited`, `q`)
- **Slice 4** — Rewrite `Web/directory.html` as a session-centric view with Cards / List toggle, status pills, agent filter, search, show-exited toggle, machine error placeholders
- **Slice 5** — Public `GET /api` reference page
- **Slice 6** — HTML preview report (standalone), aggregation tests, doc updates, smoke

## Out of scope (Phase 2.1+)

- Write-through proxy on the Gateway (writes stay direct-to-Director using `tailnetEndpoint + sid`)
- SSE / push (2s polling fine for v1)
- Cross-fleet orchestration / fan-out prompts
- Removing the legacy `/legacy-manager` route (separate PR, gated on `ManagerView` migrating off proxy routes)

## Done criteria

See plan, section "Done criteria". Highlights:

1. `GET /sessions` returns every session across the fleet, each carrying `MachineName`, `TailnetEndpoint`, `ViewUrl`, `LastActivityAt`.
2. One slow Director surfaces in `machineErrors`, not silently dropped.
3. `/` renders both Cards and List views; toggle persists.
4. Exited hidden by default; toggle reveals.
5. Clicking "Open" goes directly to the owning Director (Gateway not in request path).
6. Existing tests still pass; new aggregation tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

comments.body: Shipped on main in commit a721452 (Phase 2 + 3 in one commit). Session-centric directory, supervisor-owned colors (green/blue/yellow/red, with gray reserved for unknown/unreachable), exited sessions filtered out at the Director. 30 new tests (14 aggregation + 16 supervisor) passing.

title: Gateway Phase 2 — session-centric directory + public /sessions API

body:
## Summary

Implement Phase 1 of the Gateway/Director architecture pivot described in [docs/architecture/gateway/GATEWAY_DIRECTOR_TARGET.md](../blob/main/docs/architecture/gateway/GATEWAY_DIRECTOR_TARGET.md). The Gateway becomes a thin receptionist that lists every live Director and deeplinks users to each Director's own manager UI. It does not proxy session traffic.

Concrete plan: [docs/architecture/gateway/GATEWAY_AS_DIRECTORY_PLAN.md](../blob/main/docs/architecture/gateway/GATEWAY_AS_DIRECTORY_PLAN.md).

## Goal

Point a browser at the Gateway, see every live Director on the fleet, click through to a Director, and interact with that Director's sessions on the Director's own HTTP port. The Gateway never sees the session traffic.

## Slices

- [ ] **Slice 1** - Persist Director identity (`director-id.txt`) so the registry sees the same Director across restarts.
- [ ] **Slice 2** - `POST /directors/register`, `POST /directors/{id}/heartbeat`, `DELETE /directors/{id}/registration` on the Gateway. Heartbeat-based TTL sweep (60s).
- [ ] **Slice 3** - `GatewayClient` on the Director side. Reads `gateway.url` / `gateway.token` from `config.json`, registers + heartbeats + unregisters. No-op when unset.
- [ ] **Slice 4** - Directory page at `GET /` listing all registered Directors with "Open Director" deeplinks. Old manager UI moves to `/legacy-manager`.
- [ ] **Slice 5** - Smoke test on one machine, then doc update.

## Out of scope (Phase 1)

- Removing the proxy routes (`/sessions/*`, `/handover`, `/fanout`). They stay functional this PR because the embedded Avalonia `ManagerView` still calls them. A follow-up will migrate `ManagerView` to per-Director direct calls and delete the proxy routes.
- Cross-machine smoke test (covered when we have a second box configured).
- Live SSE events from Director to Gateway. Polling the directory every few seconds is fine for Phase 1.

## Done criteria

1. New Director starts with `gateway.url` configured -> Gateway directory shows it within 5s.
2. Restart the Director -> same row updates in place (same `directorId`).
3. Kill the Director -> row disappears within ~60s (heartbeat TTL).
4. "Open Director" anchor opens the Director's own `manager.html` and session interactions work as today.
5. No `gateway.url` configured -> Director boots normally; file-watch discovery on the same box still works.
6. All existing tests pass; new tests for the slices pass.

comments.body:
Shipped. Commit 4d92a21 ("feat(gateway): Phase 1 - directory + HTTP register/heartbeat (#123)"), with Phases 2-3 layered on top in a721452. All slices visible in code:

- `POST /directors/register` - `GatewayEndpoints.cs:149`
- `POST /directors/{id}/heartbeat` - `GatewayEndpoints.cs:161`
- `DirectorRegistry` - injected at `GatewayEndpoints.cs:16`
- Director-side client - `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Discovery/DirectorEndpointClient.cs`
- Directory page - `src/CcDirector.Gateway/Web/directory.html`
- Old manager moved to `/legacy-manager` - `GatewayEndpoints.cs:51`

Closing.

title: Gateway-as-directory: implement Phase 1 (register/heartbeat + directory page)

body:
## Problem

On initial session load, the bottom status bar area has background colors leaking across the full width of the terminal row. The colored background extends into empty cells where there should be no background.

**This goes away on resize** -- resizing the window triggers SIGWINCH which makes Claude Code redraw everything from scratch, and the redraw renders correctly.

This bug has been reported and "fixed" multiple times but keeps coming back because there is no automated way to verify the terminal rendering is correct.

## Screenshots

### screen_1: Initial load (BROKEN)
The bottom status bar ("bypass permissions on") has a grey/dark background spanning the full terminal width. Colored blocks bleed behind text elements. Empty space to the right of the text has a colored background that should not be there.

### screen_2: After resize (CORRECT)  
Same content but now the status bar renders properly. "bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)" shows with correct formatting, no full-width background bar, no color bleeding.

## Root cause analysis so far

- `ClearRow()` in `AnsiParser.cs` uses `BceCell()` (Background Color Erase) which inherits the current `_bg` attribute
- Scroll operations (`ScrollUp`, `ScrollDown`, `InsertLines`, `DeleteLines`) call `ClearRow()`, so if `_bg` is non-default at scroll time, the cleared row gets colored backgrounds on every cell
- Claude Code's ink-based UI uses differential rendering -- it only overwrites cells that changed, leaving the leaked backgrounds on unwritten cells
- A partial fix was applied (scroll operations now use `ClearRowDefault()`) but the issue also occurs on initial render, not just during scroll
- The initial buffer parse may have additional BCE or state issues not yet identified

## What's needed

1. **Raw byte capture** -- ability to dump the exact ANSI byte stream from CircularTerminalBuffer to a file so we can replay it in tests
2. **Automated cell-grid tests** -- tests that parse captured ANSI streams and assert cell backgrounds are correct (8 tests already written in `AnsiParserScrollBceTests.cs`)
3. **Root cause the initial render issue** -- capture the bytes from a fresh session startup and trace through the parser to find where backgrounds leak

## Files involved

- `src/CcDirector.Terminal.Core/AnsiParser.cs` -- ANSI parser, scroll/erase/BCE logic
- `src/CcDirector.Terminal.Avalonia/TerminalControl.cs` -- Avalonia terminal control
- `src/CcDirector.Core/Memory/CircularTerminalBuffer.cs` -- raw byte buffer with `DumpAll()`
- `src/CcDirector.Core.Tests/AnsiParserScrollBceTests.cs` -- existing test coverage

## Partial fix already applied

`ScrollUp`, `ScrollDown`, `InsertLines`, `DeleteLines` now call `ClearRowDefault()` instead of `ClearRow()` to avoid BCE leaking during scroll. But this does NOT fix the initial render issue shown in the screenshots.

comments.body:
Closing as superseded. The root rationale - "this keeps coming back because there is no automated way to verify" - has been addressed:

- **Parser rewrite**: commit 3a8dad9 ("feat(terminal): Rewrite ANSI parser as Williams DEC state machine, validated vs xterm.js") replaces the prior hand-rolled parser with a standards-conformant implementation validated against the gold-standard emulator.
- **Cell-grid tests**: `src/CcDirector.Core.Tests/AnsiParserScrollBceTests.cs` (the 8 tests the issue called for) is in place.
- **Web + mobile out of the parser entirely**: `TerminalStreamEndpoint.cs` streams raw PTY bytes straight to xterm.js (`session-view.html` and the phone Terminal tab), so leaks there can only be xterm.js bugs, not ours.
- Earlier targeted fix: a7fb6a5 ("fix: Terminal background color leaking across full row width (#121)").

If a desktop Avalonia leak still reproduces on a fresh build, file a focused bug with a captured byte stream - the parser tests + `CircularTerminalBuffer.DumpAll()` make that a 5-minute repro now.

title: Terminal: background colors leak across full row on initial render

body:
## Problem

When using cc-browser for UI testing, several issues were encountered:

### 1. Screenshot fails with "image readback failed"
```
cc-browser screenshot
[cc-browser] ERROR: Failed to capture tab: image readback failed
```

### 2. No JavaScript evaluation command
Tried `cc-browser execute` and `cc-browser eval` -- both return "Unknown command". There's no way to run JavaScript on the page to inspect DOM state, check element visibility, or debug CSS issues.

### Expected
- `cc-browser screenshot` should reliably capture the current tab
- A command like `cc-browser eval "document.querySelector('.foo').textContent"` should exist for DOM inspection

### Context
Was trying to visually verify CSS fixes on a local dev server (localhost:5188). Without working screenshots or JS eval, cc-browser can't be used for UI debugging/verification.

### Environment
- Windows 11
- cc-browser from cc-director bin

comments.body: Resolved by the cc-browser v2 rewrite. JS evaluation now exists as `cc-browser evaluate --fn "() => document.title"` (the original attempts used `eval`/`execute`, which were never the command names; the real command is `evaluate`). A `screenshot` command also ships. The core 'no way to run JavaScript' gap is closed. If the GPU 'image readback failed' screenshot case recurs in a specific environment, please open a focused issue with the connection/GPU details. Closing.

title: cc-browser: screenshot fails and no JS eval command

body:
## Summary

cc-browser cannot bypass SSL certificate warnings, requiring manual intervention when scraping sites with broken certificates (e.g. ~10 Canadian swim club websites). Add a per-connection `ignoreCertErrors` flag that launches Chrome with `--ignore-certificate-errors` for that connection only.

## Context

During cc-swimming coach profile enrichment, ~10 clubs have broken SSL certs. Currently, cc-browser shows Chrome's "Your connection is not private" interstitial and cannot proceed automatically.

## Implementation

Add `ignoreCertErrors` boolean to the connection schema:

1. **Connection config** -- add `ignoreCertErrors` field (default: false)
2. **Chrome launch** -- add `--ignore-certificate-errors` to Chrome args when the flag is true
3. **Daemon** -- pass `ignoreCertErrors` from connection config through to the Chrome launch function

### Usage

```bash
cc-browser connections add broken-ssl --url "https://site.com" --ignoreCertErrors
```

## Security

- Per-connection only, NOT global
- Default is `false`
- Only affects the specific connection where it's enabled
- Logged when active so users know cert validation is disabled

## Verification

1. Create test connection with `ignoreCertErrors: true`
2. Navigate to a site with a broken/expired certificate
3. Confirm no error interstitial -- page loads directly

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

## Summary

Add a visual interface for managing MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers instead of hand-editing JSON config files. MCP servers are increasingly important for extending Claude Code capabilities.

## Details

### Core (`McpConfigManager.cs`)
- Read/write Claude's MCP config: `~/.claude/mcp-settings.json` and project-level `.mcp.json`
- Parse server entries: `{ "mcpServers": { "name": { "command": "...", "args": [...], "env": {...} } } }`
- Import from Claude Desktop config location

### UI (`McpServersDialog.xaml`)
- List of configured MCP servers with name, command, transport type, status
- Add/Edit/Remove servers (supports stdio and SSE transports)
- Test connection button (spawn server briefly, verify handshake)
- Import from Claude Desktop button
- Scope selector: global (user) vs project-level
- Accessible from Settings or main menu

## Existing code to reuse

- `ClaudeOptions.McpConfig` -- MCP config path property
- `ClaudeArgBuilder` -- already handles `--mcp-config` flag

body:
## Problem

The old cc-gmail build (shipped via cc-director) stored credentials in:
```
%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\config\gmail\accounts\{account}\
```

The new cc-gmail build (using cc_shared/config.py from cc-tools) resolves data directory to:
```
%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-tools\data\gmail\accounts\{account}\
```

After rebuilding cc-gmail, the tool cannot find OAuth credentials (credentials.json, token.json, config.json) because it looks in the new location while the files are in the old location.

## Impact

- cc-gmail completely breaks after rebuild -- "OAuth credentials not found"
- No error explaining the migration issue
- User has to manually find and copy credential files
- Affects both consulting and personal accounts

## Root Cause

`cc_shared/config.py::get_data_dir()` resolves to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-tools\data` when that directory exists. The old build either:
- Used a different bundled config module that resolved to `cc-director/config/`
- Or the `cc-tools/data` directory didn't exist yet so it fell through to a different path

## Manual Fix Applied

Copied credentials from old to new location:
```bash
cp %LOCALAPPDATA%/cc-director/config/gmail/accounts/consulting/* %LOCALAPPDATA%/cc-tools/data/gmail/accounts/consulting/
cp %LOCALAPPDATA%/cc-director/config/gmail/accounts/personal/* %LOCALAPPDATA%/cc-tools/data/gmail/accounts/personal/
```

## Proper Fix Needed

One or more of:

1. **Migration on startup**: cc-gmail should check for credentials in the old `cc-director/config/gmail/` path and automatically migrate them to the new `cc-tools/data/gmail/` path on first run. Print a clear message: "Migrated credentials from cc-director/config to cc-tools/data."

2. **Fallback resolution in get_data_dir()**: Add `cc-director/config` as a checked path in the resolution chain, but ONLY as a read fallback (not for new writes).

3. **Audit all cc-tools**: Any tool that previously shipped via cc-director may have the same issue. Check cc-outlook, cc-vault, and others for similar credential/config path mismatches.

## Files

- `cc-tools/src/cc_shared/config.py` - `get_data_dir()` function
- `cc-tools/src/cc-gmail/src/auth.py` - uses `get_data_dir() / "gmail"` for CONFIG_DIR
- Old location: `%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\config\gmail\`
- New location: `%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-tools\data\gmail\`

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: fix: cc-gmail data directory migration -- old builds use cc-director/config, new builds use cc-tools/data

body:
## Problem

Commands with interactive confirmation prompts (typer.confirm) silently hang when stdin is not a TTY (piped input, called from scripts, automation).

**Affected commands:**

### cc-gmail
- `delete` - has `--yes` flag but hangs without it in non-interactive mode
- `archive-before` - same issue

### cc-outlook
- `delete` - hangs waiting for [y/N] confirmation
- `archive` - hangs waiting for confirmation
- `unarchive` - hangs waiting for confirmation
- `accounts remove` - hangs waiting for confirmation
- `calendar delete` - hangs waiting for confirmation

## Root Cause

typer.confirm() reads from stdin. When stdin is piped (not a TTY), the prompt is written to stdout but there is no interactive user to respond. The command hangs indefinitely with no error output.

## Impact

- Email automation skill had to use `echo "y" | cc-outlook archive` hack (fragile)
- `cc-gmail delete` without `echo "y" |` silently did nothing -- entire batch of blacklisted emails was skipped
- No error message, no exit code, no indication of failure

## Fix Applied

Added `_require_confirmation()` guard to both cc-gmail and cc-outlook CLIs. The guard runs before typer.confirm() and checks:

1. If `--yes` is set -> skip (confirmation bypassed, no prompt needed)
2. If stdin is a TTY -> skip (user can answer interactively)
3. If stdin is NOT a TTY and `--yes` is NOT set -> exit with code 1 and clear error:

```
Error: 'delete' requires confirmation but stdin is not interactive.
Fix: Use --yes / -y flag to skip confirmation in non-interactive mode.
```

## Files Changed

- cc-tools: `src/cc-gmail/src/cli.py` - added _require_confirmation(), applied to delete and archive-before
- cc-tools: `src/cc-outlook/src/cli.py` - added _require_confirmation(), applied to delete, archive, unarchive, accounts remove, calendar delete
- cc-consult: Email skill updated to use -y flag on all delete/archive commands

## Principle

CLI tools used by automation must NEVER silently hang. Either succeed, or fail loudly with a clear error and fix instruction.

body:
## Bug Report

**Tool:** cc-powerpoint (from-markdown)
**Version:** Current (installed via cc-director)

## Description

When a markdown slide uses numbered lists (`1. 2. 3.`), cc-powerpoint generates a slide with only the title -- the body content is completely missing. Bullet lists (`- `) work correctly.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a markdown file with a slide using numbered list:

```markdown
# My Slide Title

1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item
```

2. Run: `cc-powerpoint from-markdown input.md -o output.pptx`
3. Open the generated .pptx

## Expected

Slide shows the title and all numbered list items in the body.

## Actual

Slide shows only the title and a decorative line. Body text is completely empty.

## Workaround

Convert numbered lists to bullet lists (`-`) before generating.

## Screenshot

Slide 3 "Today's Plan -- Mar 9" showed an empty body. All 7 numbered items were missing entirely. Other slides using `-` bullets rendered correctly.

comments.body: Fixed in be910c8. Root cause: `_extract_bullets()` in cc-powerpoint's parser only tracked `bullet_list_open/close` tokens, so ordered lists (1. 2. 3.) never raised list depth and produced an empty slide body. Now counts `ordered_list_open/close` too. Added regression tests (numbered list + numbered sub-bullets); all 19 parser tests pass. Rebuilt and deployed cc-powerpoint.exe; verified the numbered-list slide body now renders.

title: cc-powerpoint: Numbered lists render as empty slide body

body:
## Bug

Copying from the terminal or file viewer screens triggers:

```
This type of CollectionView does not support changes to its SourceCollection
from a thread different from the Dispatcher thread.
```

## Root Cause (suspected)

Pipe events from `DirectorPipeServer` fire `OnMessageReceived` on a background thread. The chain is:

1. `DirectorPipeServer` background thread -> `OnMessageReceived`
2. -> `EventRouter.Route` -> `Session.HandlePipeEvent`
3. -> `OnTurnCompleted` / `OnActivityStateChanged` (fires on pipe thread)

Most subscribers dispatch to UI thread via `Dispatcher.BeginInvoke`, but there may be a race condition where an `ObservableCollection` is modified between the background event arriving and the dispatch completing -- or a subscriber that doesn't dispatch correctly.

## Key Collections to Audit

- `_sessions` (ObservableCollection, MainWindow)
- `_hookEvents` (ObservableCollection, MainWindow)
- `_screenshots` (ObservableCollection, MainWindow)
- `_queueItems` (ObservableCollection, MainWindow)
- `_summaryItems` (ObservableCollection, MainWindow)
- `_messages` (ObservableCollection, SimpleChatView)
- `_connections` (ObservableCollection, ConnectionsView)

## Reproduction

1. Have an active session running in CC Director
2. Switch to terminal or file viewer tab
3. Try to copy text (Ctrl+C or right-click copy)
4. Error dialog appears

## Notes

- All checked handlers use `Dispatcher.BeginInvoke` but the timing of the copy operation vs the dispatch may cause the race
- Pre-existing issue, not related to workflow recorder changes
title: CollectionView thread error when copying from terminal/fileview

body:
## Problem The `paste` command only sends `text/plain` clipboard data. Rich text editors like Medium's custom editor need `text/html` format to properly create paragraph elements (`

`, `

`, `

`, etc.). ### What happens ```bash cc-browser -c medium paste --selector "div.postArticle-content" --text "Title\n\nParagraph one.\n\nParagraph two." ``` Reports `pasted: true` but Medium renders everything inside a single `

` element with `
` tags instead of creating separate paragraph elements. The editor ignores the plain text newlines for structural formatting. ### What should happen ```bash cc-browser -c medium paste --selector "div.postArticle-content" --format html --text "

Title

Paragraph one.

Paragraph two.

" ``` Or auto-convert plain text with double newlines to HTML paragraphs: ```bash cc-browser -c medium paste --selector "div.postArticle-content" --format html --text "Title\n\nParagraph one.\n\nParagraph two." # Internally converts to:

Title

Paragraph one.

Paragraph two.

``` The ClipboardEvent should include `text/html` in the DataTransfer object so rich text editors can create proper DOM structure. ### Why this matters - LinkedIn's Quill editor handles `text/plain` paste fine (Quill does its own paragraph splitting) - Medium's custom editor (`g_editable="true"`) does NOT split plain text into paragraphs - Any editor that relies on `text/html` clipboard data for structure will have this problem - This is the only blocker for automating Medium article posting ### Context - `paste --selector "h3[data-testid='editorTitleParagraph']"` works for single-line title - `paste --selector "p[data-testid='editorParagraphText']"` reports success but text does not render - `paste --selector "div.postArticle-content"` puts everything in the title h3 with br tags - `type` works character-by-character but is too slow for articles and Enter doesn't create paragraph breaks - Medium skill created: `medium.skill.md` documents these findings ### Suggested implementation In the ClipboardEvent creation, include both formats: ```javascript const dt = new DataTransfer(); dt.setData('text/plain', plainText); if (format === 'html') { dt.setData('text/html', htmlContent); } ``` ### Related - GitHub #103 (setInputFiles for image upload) -- also blocks Medium image attachment ### Environment - cc-browser v2 - Windows 11 - Tested on medium.com 2026-03-07

body:
## Problem

`cc-browser` has no way to programmatically set files on `input[type='file']` elements. This blocks automation of any workflow that requires file uploads (e.g., LinkedIn post with image, form attachments).

### What happens

- `fill --selector "input[type='file']" --value "path/to/file.png"` fails with: `Failed to set the 'value' property on 'HTMLInputElement': This input element accepts a filename, which may only be programmatically set to the empty string.`
- `upload` command does not exist
- `click` on the file input opens the native OS file picker, which cannot be controlled programmatically
- `evaluate` is blocked on sites with strict CSP (e.g., LinkedIn)

### What should happen

cc-browser should expose Playwright's `setInputFiles` (or equivalent) as a command, e.g.:

```bash
cc-browser --connection linkedin set-file --selector "input[type='file']" --file "D:/path/to/image.png"
```

This is the standard approach in Playwright/Puppeteer for handling file inputs without triggering the native file dialog.

### Context

The LinkedIn navigation skill (`linkedin.skill.md`) references using an "upload endpoint" for hidden file inputs, but no such endpoint exists in cc-browser today. The "Create a Post" workflow (step 5) says to "Find `input[type='file']`, use upload endpoint" but this is not possible.

### Affected workflows

- LinkedIn: Create a Post with image
- Any site with file upload forms

### Environment

- cc-browser v2
- Windows 11
- Chrome extension + native messaging architecture

body:
## Summary

A popup dialog inside CC Director with a split-pane layout for visually authoring content (articles, book sections, posts) with Claude Code as the writing engine.

## The Vision

**You steer, Claude types, and you watch it take shape in real time.**

The goal is to be the creative director of content without doing the typing. Think and direct while Claude does the mechanical writing -- and see the result rendered live as it's built.

## Layout

- **Left pane**: A Claude Code terminal session
- **Right pane**: A live visual preview of the content being written

## Structured JSON Format

Content is stored as a **structured JSON file on disk** (not flat text). Sections/paragraphs have IDs so you can reference them precisely:

- "Change paragraph 4"
- "Rewrite the intro"
- "Expand section 2.3"

## Interaction Flow

1. View rendered content on the right
2. Select one or more sections visually
3. Selection updates the JSON file on disk (marking what's selected)
4. Talk to Claude Code on the left, referencing the selection
5. Claude Code edits the JSON file
6. Right pane re-renders with the changes

## Key Design Decisions Needed

- [ ] JSON schema for structured content (sections, paragraphs, metadata)
- [ ] How selection state is communicated (written to JSON? separate file?)
- [ ] Rendering approach for the right pane (HTML/WebView? WPF RichText?)
- [ ] File watching mechanism to detect Claude Code's changes and re-render

## Constraints

- **No concurrent edits**: For now, don't interact with the visual side while Claude is mid-response (honor-system warning, not a lock)
- Should open as a popup dialog from CC Director
- Must follow CC Director UI style guide (VisualStyle.md)

## Why This Matters

- Be the driver/director of all content without doing the typing
- See what you're building as you build it (not raw markdown or code output)
- JSON structure gives precise control over specific sections
- Selection mechanism enables point-and-talk instead of describe-and-hope

body:
## Summary

The `--background` flag on `cc-browser connections open` currently uses `--start-minimized`, which breaks screenshots because Chrome stops rendering the GPU buffer for minimized windows. `captureVisibleTab` returns blank/black images.

**Better approach:** Move the window off-screen instead of minimizing. Chrome still renders "normal" state windows even when positioned beyond all monitors, so `captureVisibleTab` works perfectly with zero screen flashing.

## What Works Today

- `connections open <name> --background` launches Chrome minimized
- Click, type, scroll, hover, navigate -- all use DOM events via content.js, work regardless of window state
- Extension + native messaging connection -- works regardless of window state

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Add `system.display` permission to extension manifest

Add `"system.display"` to `extension/manifest.json` permissions. This gives `chrome.system.display.getInfo()` to query actual monitor geometry so the window can be positioned reliably off-screen on any multi-monitor setup.

### 2. Add off-screen positioning in background.js

Add `getOffScreenPosition()` helper that queries all displays and calculates a position just past the bottom edge of the lowest monitor. Add `cmdMoveOffscreen()` command that moves the window there.

```javascript
async function getOffScreenPosition() {
  const displays = await chrome.system.display.getInfo();
  let maxBottom = 0;
  for (const d of displays) {
    const bottom = d.bounds.top + d.bounds.height;
    if (bottom > maxBottom) maxBottom = bottom;
  }
  return { left: 0, top: maxBottom + 200 };
}
```

### 3. Change chrome-launch.mjs

Replace `--start-minimized` with `--window-position=0,32000` as a rough initial off-screen position. Store the `background` flag in `cc-browser.json` so the daemon knows to refine positioning once the extension connects.

### 4. Daemon sends move-offscreen on connection ready

When the extension WebSocket connects and the connection was opened with `--background`, the daemon sends the `move-offscreen` command to position the window precisely off-screen using actual display bounds.

### 5. No changes to screenshot

Since the window is "normal" state (just off-screen), the GPU buffer is rendered and `captureVisibleTab` works as-is. No flash, no restore/minimize dance.

## What This Achieves

- Window is off-screen: invisible to user on any monitor setup
- Chrome still renders the GPU buffer (window is "normal" state)
- Screenshots work perfectly -- no flashing, no restore/minimize
- All DOM commands (click/type/scroll) continue to work
- Multi-monitor safe via `chrome.system.display.getInfo()`

## Known Limitations

- Window appears briefly on launch before extension moves it off-screen (~1-2s race window, mitigated by `--window-position=0,32000` on launch)
- Alt-Tab still shows the browser window
- Taskbar still shows the browser icon

## Files to Modify

- `tools/cc-browser/extension/manifest.json` -- add `system.display` permission
- `tools/cc-browser/extension/background.js` -- add `getOffScreenPosition()`, `cmdMoveOffscreen()`, register command
- `tools/cc-browser/src/chrome-launch.mjs` -- replace `--start-minimized` with `--window-position`, store background flag
- `tools/cc-browser/src/daemon.mjs` -- send `move-offscreen` on background connection ready

body:
## Problem

`cc-vault contacts log-interaction` crashes when the contact name doesn't match any vault entry:

```
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'lower'
```

Stack trace:
```
in contacts_log_interaction:2388
in add_interaction:2613
in get_contact:1133
```

## How to reproduce

```bash
cc-vault contacts log-interaction "Mayank" --type linkedin --date 2026-03-06T18:30:00 --direction outbound --summary "test" --format json
```

If "Mayank" doesn't match any vault contact, it crashes instead of returning an error.

## Expected behavior

Return a clean error like "Contact not found: Mayank" with exit code 1, not an unhandled exception.

## Impact

`cc-comm-queue mark-posted` calls `log-to-vault` which calls this command. Every LinkedIn message send hits this crash. The message still sends fine, but vault interaction logging fails silently.

comments.body:
Fixed. Two changes:

1. `db.py:get_contact()` -- added null check at top: if identifier is None/empty, return None instead of crashing on `.lower()`

2. `cli.py:contacts_log_interaction()` -- handle contacts with null email field: fall back to `contact['name']` for the `add_interaction` lookup instead of passing None

Root cause: contacts matched by name may have a null email field. The code passed `contact['email']` (None) to `add_interaction` -> `get_contact`, which called `.lower()` on it.

title: cc-vault: get_contact crashes with AttributeError when contact not found

body:
## Problem

The LinkedIn Send a Message workflow only documents the happy path. Three real sends have worked perfectly, but the skill needs to handle:

### Failure cases not documented

1. **Profile 404** -- destination_url might be wrong or profile deleted
   - Check for `/404` in URL or "page not found" in body after navigation
   - Skip item, log error, move to next

2. **Not connected** -- no "Message {FirstName}" button on profile
   - Profile shows "Connect" or "Follow" instead of "Message"
   - Skip item, log that connection is needed first

3. **Paste fails** -- `pasted: false` or text doesn't appear
   - Retry once, then skip with error

4. **Send button not found** -- overlay didn't open properly
   - Retry click on Message button, or close overlay and try again

5. **Rate limiting** -- LinkedIn may show "you've sent too many messages" or similar warning
   - STOP immediately, do not continue batch
   - Log which items were sent and which remain

6. **Session expired / login wall** -- redirected to sign-in page
   - Detect via auth check (no `.global-nav__me-photo`)
   - STOP and report to user

### Also needed

- Guidance on safe batch size (how many messages per session before risking detection?)
- Recommended delay between messages in a batch (currently 5s between close/open -- is that enough?)

body:
## Problem

Sending a LinkedIn message from the queue requires 7+ manual cc-browser commands in exact sequence. The pattern is identical every time -- only the profile URL, first name, and message content change.

## Current workflow (per message)

```
cc-browser connections open linkedin          # 1. open browser
sleep 6 && cc-browser connections status      # 2. wait + verify
cc-browser -c linkedin navigate <url>         # 3. navigate to profile
sleep 5 && snapshot | grep "Message {Name}"   # 4. wait + find button
cc-browser -c linkedin click --ref eXX        # 5. click Message button
sleep 3 && click textbox + paste message      # 6. focus + paste
snapshot | grep Send + click --ref eXX        # 7. find Send + click
sleep 3 && snapshot to verify                 # 8. verify sent
cc-comm-queue mark-posted <id>                # 9. mark posted
cc-browser connections close linkedin         # 10. close browser
```

## Proposed

`cc-comm-queue send <ticket>` -- single command that:
1. Reads queue item (content, destination_url, first name from content)
2. Opens LinkedIn browser (with retry)
3. Navigates to profile URL
4. Clicks "Message {FirstName}" 
5. Pastes message content
6. Clicks Send by ref
7. Verifies sent
8. Marks as posted
9. Closes browser

For batch: `cc-comm-queue send --all` or `cc-comm-queue send --next`

This could be implemented as a Python script that calls cc-browser commands, or as a cc-browser macro.

## Benefit

- 34 messages at ~25s each = ~14 minutes manual. Single command = fire and forget.
- Eliminates human error in the mechanical steps
- Agent can focus on handling exceptions rather than typing commands

## Problem

The campaign workflow that created 34 LinkedIn message queue items did not populate the `recipient` field. All items have:
```json
"recipient": null
```

But they DO have recipient info scattered across other fields:
- `destination_url`: `"https://linkedin.com/in/daina-o-92019b1"` (profile URL)
- `notes`: `"LinkedIn DM to Daina Oku"` (name)
- `content`: starts with `"Daina,"` (first name)

## Expected Behavior

When adding LinkedIn messages to the queue (especially via campaign), the `recipient` field should be populated:
```json
"recipient": {
  "name": "Daina Oku",
  "profile_url": "https://linkedin.com/in/daina-o-92019b1"
}
```

This is critical because:
1. `mark-posted` uses `recipient` for vault logging
2. Automation agents use `recipient.profile_url` for Path A navigation
3. `cc-comm-queue show` displays recipient info in the text output

## Fix Options

1. Fix the campaign creation code to pass `--recipient-name` and `--recipient-url` when adding items
2. Add a backfill command: `cc-comm-queue backfill-recipients` that parses destination_url and notes to populate null recipients

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: cc-comm-queue: campaign add should populate recipient field

body:
## Problem

`cc-comm-queue mark-posted` attempts to log the communication to the vault, but fails with:
```
NOTE: Could not log to vault (no matching contact or cc-vault unavailable)
```

This happens because vault matching only uses `recipient.name`, and many queue items have `recipient: null` (especially campaign-generated items). However, the `destination_url` field contains the LinkedIn profile URL (e.g., `https://linkedin.com/in/minglis`), which could be matched against vault contacts' `linkedin_url` field.

## Expected Behavior

When `recipient` is null, `mark-posted` should attempt to match the vault contact by:
1. `recipient.name` (current behavior)
2. `recipient.profile_url` if name match fails
3. `destination_url` as final fallback (extract LinkedIn username and match against vault `linkedin_url`)

## Impact

- 34 approved campaign items all have `recipient: null` but valid `destination_url`
- Every mark-posted for these items fails vault logging
- Interaction history is not being tracked

## Example

```json
{
  "destination_url": "https://linkedin.com/in/minglis",
  "recipient": null,
  "notes": "LinkedIn DM to Michael (Mike) Inglis"
}
```

The vault likely has a contact with `linkedin_url` containing "minglis" -- this should match.

body:
## Problem

When queuing an email reply via cc-comm-queue, two issues were discovered:

### 1. No validation of persona vs. email thread context
When replying to a thread that originated from soren.frederiksen@mindzie.com, the queue accepted `--persona personal` without warning. This would send the reply from the wrong email account, breaking the thread and looking unprofessional.

**Expected behavior:** The Communication Manager should either:
- Require explicit `--email-from` when replying to a thread, OR
- Warn when the persona's email doesn't match the thread context, OR  
- Auto-detect reply context from the subject line prefix ("Re:") and validate

### 2. No CC field support
The original email thread had CC: damyot@uottawa.ca. There is no `--email-cc` flag on cc-comm-queue, so CC recipients are silently dropped.

**Expected behavior:** cc-comm-queue should support `--email-cc` and `--email-bcc` flags.

## Steps to Reproduce

```bash
# This was accepted without warning despite wrong persona for the thread
cc-comm-queue add email email "body" \
    --persona personal \
    --email-to "wvanwoen@uottawa.ca" \
    --email-subject "Re: Guidance for students on software development with GenAI" \
    --json
```

## Impact

Email sent from wrong account to a university professor. Caught before dispatch but could have been embarrassing.

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: Communication Manager: email sent from wrong account, no CC field support

body:
## Problem

When replying to an email (Re: subject), the agent/write skill can pick a different send-from account than the one that received the original email. This happened in practice: an email received on `soren.frederiksen@mindzie.com` (Outlook) was replied to from `soren@bfrederiksen.com` (Gmail).

This is a serious issue -- replying from the wrong address looks unprofessional and confusing to the recipient.

## Expected Behavior

When composing a **reply** (subject starts with `Re:` or user says "reply to this email"):
1. The send-from address MUST match the account that received the original email
2. If the original email was received on Outlook, the reply must go through Outlook
3. If the original email was received on Gmail, the reply must go through Gmail
4. The system should NOT allow overriding this with auto-inference

## Where to Fix

- `/write` skill - when drafting a reply, enforce send-from = received-on
- Communication Manager queue files - include `reply_to_account` field
- cc-director send service - validate account matches before sending

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Reply emails always use the same account that received the original
- [ ] If account cannot be determined, ask the user explicitly
- [ ] New emails (not replies) can still auto-infer the send-from account
- [ ] Add validation in the send pipeline to reject mismatched reply accounts

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

body:
## Problem

When `cc-vault contacts scan-emails` runs, it creates interactions with correct direction (inbound/outbound) but the `email_activity` summary row is not properly populated:

1. **received_count is always 0** - The scan correctly tags interactions as inbound/outbound, but the email_activity row only gets a sent_count (from the raw cc-gmail search total), never a received_count
2. **first_email_date and last_email_date are NULL** - These are never set during the scan
3. **email_count and sent_count are raw search totals** - They represent how many results cc-gmail returned, not how many were actually imported (after dedup)

### Root Cause

The scan-emails command writes to email_activity using raw cc-gmail output counts without deriving the actual sent/received split from the imported interactions.

### Suggested Fix

After scanning, update email_activity from actual interactions:

```sql
UPDATE email_activity 
SET sent_count = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM interactions WHERE contact_id=? AND account=? AND direction='outbound'),
    received_count = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM interactions WHERE contact_id=? AND account=? AND direction='inbound'),
    email_count = sent_count + received_count,
    first_email_date = (SELECT MIN(interaction_date) FROM interactions WHERE contact_id=? AND account=?),
    last_email_date = (SELECT MAX(interaction_date) FROM interactions WHERE contact_id=? AND account=?)
```

Or compute these dynamically and remove the cached columns.

## Files
- `tools/cc-vault/src/cli.py` - scan-emails command
- `tools/cc-vault/src/db.py` - email_activity table

comments.body: Fixed in 5e0b21e (root cause of #76). scan-emails now queries actual interaction records after scanning and calls upsert_email_activity() with proper sent_count, received_count, and date ranges.

title: cc-vault: scan-emails doesn't populate email_activity dates or received counts

## Problem

There is no way to update the label on an existing email in `cc-vault contacts emails`. To change a label, you must remove and re-add the email:

```bash
cc-vault contacts emails 156 --remove sergei@accessair.ca
cc-vault contacts emails 156 --add sergei@accessair.ca --label work
```

This is error-prone and creates unnecessary churn in the contact_emails table (new row IDs).

### Suggested Fix

Add an `--update-label` or `--relabel` option:

```bash
cc-vault contacts emails 156 --update-label sergei@accessair.ca --label work
```

Alternatively, a simple `--set-label <email> <label>` would work too.

## Files
- `tools/cc-vault/src/cli.py` - contacts emails command
- `tools/cc-vault/src/db.py` - add update_email_label() function

comments.body: Fixed in 5e0b21e. contacts emails now supports --update-label <email> -l <label> to change a label in-place without remove+re-add.

## Problem

`cc-vault contacts show <id>` does not support `--format json`, making it difficult for agents and skills to parse contact data programmatically.

Most other cc-vault commands support `--format json` (e.g., `contacts last-comm`, `contacts search`, `contacts emails`), but `contacts show` only outputs rich text tables.

### Impact
Agents working with contact data have to either:
- Use multiple separate commands to get the same data
- Parse rich text output (fragile and error-prone)

### Suggested Fix
Add `--format json` option to `contacts show` that outputs all contact fields, tags, recent interactions, and linked emails as a single JSON object.

## Files
- `tools/cc-vault/src/cli.py` - contacts show command

comments.body: Fixed in 5e0b21e. contacts show now supports --format json, outputting all contact fields plus tags, memories, recent_interactions, email_activity, and emails.

title: cc-vault: contacts show missing --format json

body:
## Problem

The `email_activity` table stores data that doesn't match reality. For contact #156 (Sergei, 202 interactions):

**email_activity says:**
- consulting: email_count=250, sent_count=290, received_count=0
- personal: email_count=62, sent_count=266, received_count=0

**Actual interactions table:**
- gmail: 82 (41 inbound, 41 outbound)
- gmail:consulting: 120 (56 inbound, 64 outbound)

### Issues:
1. **received_count is always 0** - never populated despite 97 inbound emails existing
2. **sent_count/email_count are inflated** - these seem to be raw search result totals from cc-gmail, not actual imported interaction counts
3. **first_email_date and last_email_date are NULL** - never populated
4. **Account names don't match** - email_activity uses "consulting"/"personal" but interactions use "gmail:consulting"/"gmail"

### Impact
The `contacts last-comm` display shows misleading "Sent: 290, Received: 0" data, making it look like all communication is one-directional.

### Suggested Fix
Either:
- Populate email_activity from actual interactions data (derived, not raw scan counts)
- Or remove email_activity and compute stats dynamically from the interactions table

The account naming should be normalized to match the interactions table format.

## Files
- `tools/cc-vault/src/db.py` - email_activity table and scan logic
- `tools/cc-vault/src/cli.py` - scan-emails command, last-comm display

comments.body: Fixed in 5e0b21e. scan-emails now populates email_activity with actual sent/received counts derived from interaction records, replacing the misleading raw search counts.

title: cc-vault: email_activity table data is disconnected from actual interactions

body:
## Bug 1: Summarize runs only ONE batch (CRITICAL)

When adding the OneDrive library (91,709 files), the scan phase completed correctly but the summarize phase only processed 50 files (1 batch) out of 41,162 pending entries. Documents are cataloged but never promoted to searchable vault docs.

**Root cause:**

- `BackgroundScanService.cs:105` calls `_client.SummarizeAsync(label, 50, ct)` which spawns `cc-vault catalog summarize --library "OneDrive" --batch 50 --stream`
- `catalog.py:195` - `summarize_entries()` fetches exactly `limit=batch_size` entries and processes them. No loop. One batch and exit.
- `VaultCatalogClient.cs:135-153` - `SummarizeAsync()` spawns one process and reads its output. No loop.

**Result**: Only 50 files ever get summarized per scan, no matter how many are pending.

**Fix needed**: Add a loop in `summarize_entries()` so `--batch 50` means "process 50 at a time until done", not "process exactly 50 and stop".

**Files:**
- `tools/cc-vault/src/catalog.py:191-296`
- Possibly `src/Services/VaultCatalogClient.cs` (C# side)

---

## Bug 2: FTS search crashes on hyphenated terms (MINOR)

`cc-vault catalog search "mindzie-T4-2024"` crashes with `OperationalError: no such column: T4`.

**Root cause:**

- `db.py:5811-5818` - `search_catalog_fts()` passes the query directly to SQLite FTS5 MATCH
- Hyphenated terms like `mindzie-T4-2024` are interpreted as column subtraction (`mindzie` minus column `T4`)

**Fix needed**: Quote or escape special characters before passing to FTS5 MATCH.

**Files:**
- `tools/cc-vault/src/db.py:5806-5821`

---

## Impact

Personal tax documents (T4-2024, NOA-2024) cannot be found via `cc-vault docs search` because they were never summarized, and searching for hyphenated filenames crashes FTS.

## Verification Steps

1. Create small test library with ~10 files
2. `cc-vault library add "D:\tmp\TestTax" --label TestTax --category personal`
3. `cc-vault catalog scan --library TestTax --stream` - confirm all cataloged
4. `cc-vault catalog summarize --library TestTax --batch 3 --stream` - confirm ALL files summarized (not just 3)
5. `cc-vault catalog search "mindzie-T4"` - confirm no crash
6. Re-add OneDrive, scan + summarize, verify tax docs appear

## Note

A code fix has already been applied locally to both files but has not been committed.

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: Document Library: Summarize phase only processes 1 batch, FTS crashes on hyphens

body:
## Problem

`cc-browser text` returns ALL text content from the DOM, including elements that are hidden via CSS (`display: none`, `visibility: hidden`, etc.). This causes false positives during automated testing.

## Example

mindzieStudio has a standard Blazor error boundary element (`#blazor-error-ui`) that is **always in the DOM but hidden** (`display: none`). When running `cc-browser text`, the output includes:

```
Something went wrong
An unexpected error has occurred.
Don't worry, we're on it!
```

This text is never visible to the user -- the screenshot confirms the page rendered correctly with no error. But the text output makes it look like an error occurred.

## Root Cause

The `text` command extracts `innerText` or `textContent` from the page without filtering out hidden elements.

## Suggested Fix

Either:
1. **Default behavior**: Filter out elements with `display: none` or `visibility: hidden` (matches what users actually see)
2. **Add a flag**: `cc-browser text --visible-only` to opt into filtering

Option 1 is preferred since the primary use case for `text` is "what does the user see on screen?"

## Impact

- Automated QA testing generates false error reports
- Requires screenshot verification to distinguish real errors from hidden DOM content
- Wastes investigation time on non-issues

## Environment

- cc-browser v2
- Discovered during mindzieStudio Data Designer testing

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: cc-browser text returns hidden DOM elements (display:none)

body:
## Summary

Attempting to send a LinkedIn DM via cc-browser revealed multiple issues that make the Send Message workflow from the LinkedIn skill non-functional.

## Issues Found

### 1. `evaluate` blocked by CSP on LinkedIn
LinkedIn's Content Security Policy blocks `unsafe-eval`, so `cc-browser evaluate --fn` fails on LinkedIn pages:
```
Evaluating a string as JavaScript violates the following Content Security Policy directive
```
This breaks all JavaScript-based element discovery documented in the LinkedIn skill (profile extraction, auth checks, etc.).

### 2. `type` command: `--text` flag conflicts with `--selector`
```
cc-browser type --connection linkedin --selector "div.msg-form__contenteditable" --text "message"
ERROR: Only one of ref, text, or selector can be used
```
The `--text` flag (for the text to type) collides with the `--text` flag (for finding an element by text content). There's no way to type into an element found by selector.

### 3. `fill` reports success but doesn't actually fill contenteditable
```
cc-browser fill --selector "div.msg-form__contenteditable" --value "message text"
{"filled": "selector=\"div.msg-form__contenteditable\"", "value": "..."}
```
Returns success but the field remains empty ("Write a message..." placeholder still visible). LinkedIn uses a contenteditable div, not a standard input -- `fill` likely sets `.value` which doesn't work on contenteditable elements.

### 4. Extension not deployed by build
The `extension/` folder was missing from the deployed `_cc-browser/` directory. Had to manually copy it. The `build.ps1` failed because `native-host/` was locked by a running process, but the error is silent about the extension not being copied.

## Expected Behavior

The LinkedIn skill's "Send a Message" workflow should work end-to-end:
1. Navigate to profile
2. Click Message button
3. Type message text into the contenteditable input
4. Click Send

## Steps to Reproduce

1. `cc-browser connections open linkedin`
2. `cc-browser navigate --connection linkedin --url "https://www.linkedin.com/in/anyone"`
3. Click Message button (this works)
4. Try to type into the message field (all three approaches fail)

## Environment
- Windows 11, cc-browser v2
- LinkedIn as of 2026-03-05

## Priority
High -- this blocks the Communication Manager dispatch workflow for LinkedIn messages.

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: cc-browser: LinkedIn messaging workflow broken - CSP, fill, and type issues

Two issues preventing cc-linkedin from working:

