body:
## Summary

Add a utility feature to CC Director that scans directories (or an entire disk) for NUL files and deletes them.

NUL files (`NUL`) can be accidentally created on Windows (e.g., when a program mistakenly writes to the reserved `NUL` device name as a file path). These files can be difficult to remove through normal means and clutter the filesystem.

## Requirements

- Scan a configurable root path (default: entire disk) for files named `NUL`
- Display found NUL files to the user before deletion
- Require user confirmation before deleting
- Log results (files found, files deleted, errors)
- Handle permissions errors gracefully (skip inaccessible directories)

## Notes

- Windows reserves certain device names (`NUL`, `CON`, `PRN`, `AUX`, etc.) which can cause issues when created as actual files
- Deletion of these files may require special handling (e.g., `\?\` prefix paths)

comments.body: Implemented in commits 6f8bebd and e1f52c0. NulFileWatcher monitors the drive for stray NUL files, auto-deletes them using \?\ prefix paths, and logs all deletions to FileLog.

title: Feature: Scan disk and clean up NUL files

body:
## Problem

Sessions in the left panel are listed in creation order and cannot be reordered. Users need to organize sessions by workflow priority (e.g. move "implementation" above "enter issues") but currently have no way to do so.

## Requirements

1. **Drag-and-drop reordering** — Allow users to drag session entries up and down in the left panel `ListBox` to reorder them.
2. **Persist order** — Save the session display order so it survives app restarts. The order should be stored alongside the existing session configuration (likely extending `RecentSessionStore` or the session config file at `~/Documents/CcDirector/`).
3. **Visual feedback** — Show a drop indicator (insertion line or highlight) while dragging so the user knows where the session will land.

## Current state

- Sessions are displayed in a `ListBox` in `MainWindow.xaml`
- `RecentSessionStore` persists recent session info to `~/Documents/CcDirector/recent-sessions.json`
- No ordering/index field exists on `RecentSession` today

## Implementation notes

- Add `MouseMove`, `DragOver`, `Drop` handlers to the session `ListBox` for WPF drag-and-drop
- Add a `SortOrder` or `DisplayIndex` property to the session model and persist it
- Update `SessionManager` or the store to respect the persisted order when loading

comments.body: Implemented in 6eb9269. Drag-and-drop session reordering in the left panel with blue insertion line indicator. Order is persisted via sessions.json on drop.

title: Add drag-and-drop reordering for session tabs in the left panel

## Context

We need a simple way to deploy CC Director. For now, this means a release script that publishes the app into a single directory and zips it up with the version number.

## Requirements

- Script (PowerShell) that:
  1. Reads the version number from the project (e.g. `Version` property in `.csproj`)
  2. Runs `dotnet publish` for the WPF project (self-contained or framework-dependent — TBD)
  3. Copies all required output into a clean staging directory
  4. Zips the directory into `CcDirector-v{version}.zip`
- Output zip should contain everything needed to run the app — no manual steps
- Script should be runnable from the repo root

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Release script exists (e.g. `scripts/release.ps1`)
- [ ] Running the script produces a zip named `CcDirector-v{version}.zip`
- [ ] Zip contains a working, runnable application
- [ ] Version number is sourced from the project, not hardcoded in the script
title: Add release script to package and zip application

body:
## Context

The current `MainWindow` layout has several usability issues:

- **Session close (X) buttons** are vertically centered inline with the name — should be top-right like VS Code, visible on hover only
- **Pipe Messages panel** is always visible at 280px, wasting space when not debugging
- **Prompt text box** starts at 4 lines and the console overlay doesn't resize when it grows (see #4)
- The conceptual grouping of panels isn't clear — the tabs + prompt bar should read as one unified "command window"

This issue supersedes #4 — the prompt resize fix is included as sub-task 3 below.

---

## Current Layout

```
+---------------------+---+--------------------------------------+---+--------------------------+
| SESSIONS            |   |  [Terminal] [Source Control]          |   | PIPE MESSAGES    [Clear] |
|                     | s |  +----------------------------------+| s |                          |
| +------------------+| p |  |                                  || p | 12:34:56 Stop            |
| |  repo-name    [X]|| l |  |    ConPTY Console Overlay        || l | abc123  tool_use         |
| |  Idle (PID 1234) || i |  |    (separate native window)      || i | ...                      |
| +------------------+| t |  |                                  || t |                          |
| |  another      [X]|| t |  |                                  || t |                          |
| |  Working (5678)  || e |  |                                  || e |                          |
| +------------------+| r |  +----------------------------------+| r |                          |
|                     |   |  +----------------------------------+|   |                          |
|                     |   |  | prompt text...        [Send] [R] ||   |                          |
|                     |   |  | (MinLines=4, cut off)            ||   |                          |
| [+ New Session    ] |   |  +----------------------------------+|   |                          |
| [Kill Session     ] |   |                                      |   |                          |
+---------------------+---+--------------------------------------+---+--------------------------+
  Col 0 (220px)              Col 2 (*)                                  Col 4 (280px, always)
```

**Problems:**
1. `[X]` is centered vertically next to session name — hard to target, not standard
2. Pipe Messages always takes 280px — useless most of the time
3. Prompt bar has `MinLines=4`, console overlay doesn't reposition when prompt grows

---

## Desired Layout (pipe messages collapsed — default)

```
+---------------------+---+----------------------------------------------+--+
| SESSIONS            |   |  [Terminal] [Source Control]                  |<<|
|                     | s |  +------------------------------------------+|  |
| +------------------+| p |  |                                          ||  |
| |  repo-name     X || l |  |     ConPTY Console Overlay               ||  |
| |  Idle (PID 1234) || i |  |     (fills all available space)          ||  |
| +------------------+| t |  |                                          ||  |
| |  another       X || t |  |                                          ||  |
| |  Working (5678)  || e |  |                                          ||  |
| +------------------+| r |  +------------------------------------------+|  |
|                     |   |  +------------------------------------------+|  |
|                     |   |  | prompt text...              [Send] [Ref] ||  |
|                     |   |  | (MinLines=3, grows up to MaxHeight)      ||  |
| [+ New Session    ] |   |  +------------------------------------------+|  |
| [Kill Session     ] |   |                                              |  |
+---------------------+---+----------------------------------------------+--+
  Col 0 (220px)              Col 2 (*)                                   Toggle
                                                                         (~24px)
```

## Desired Layout (pipe messages expanded)

```
+---------------------+---+--------------------------------------+---+--------------------------+
| SESSIONS            |   |  [Terminal] [Source Control]          |   | >> PIPE MESSAGES [Clear] |
|                     | s |  +----------------------------------+| s |                          |
| +------------------+| p |  |                                  || p | 12:34:56 Stop            |
| |  repo-name     X || l |  |    ConPTY Console Overlay        || l | abc123  tool_use         |
| |  Idle (PID 1234) || i |  |    (shrinks to fit)              || i | ...                      |
| +------------------+| t |  |                                  || t |                          |
| |  another       X || t |  |                                  || t |                          |
| |  Working (5678)  || e |  |                                  || e |                          |
| +------------------+| r |  +----------------------------------+| r |                          |
|                     |   |  +----------------------------------+|   |                          |
|                     |   |  | prompt text...        [Send] [R] ||   |                          |
|                     |   |  | (MinLines=3)                     ||   |                          |
| [+ New Session    ] |   |  +----------------------------------+|   |                          |
| [Kill Session     ] |   |                                      |   |                          |
+---------------------+---+--------------------------------------+---+--------------------------+
  Col 0 (220px)              Col 2 (*)                                  Col 4 (280px)
```

---

## Sub-tasks

### 1. Session close button — reposition to top-right, hover-only

**Current:** `[X]` button is `DockPanel.Dock="Right"`, vertically centered, always visible.

**Desired:** X sits in the top-right corner of the session item, revealed on hover (Opacity 0→1).

```
CURRENT:                              DESIRED:
+----------------------------+        +----------------------------+
| | repo-name          [X]  |        | | repo-name              X |  <-- top-right corner
| | Idle (PID 1234)         |        | | Idle (PID 1234)          |      visible on hover
+----------------------------+        +----------------------------+
```

Use a `Grid` overlay with `HorizontalAlignment="Right"`, `VerticalAlignment="Top"`, and a trigger on the `ListBoxItem` `IsMouseOver` to animate opacity.

### 2. Collapsible pipe messages panel — hidden by default

- Replace the fixed 280px column with a narrow toggle strip (~24px) containing `<<` / `>>` chevron button
- Clicking the chevron expands/collapses the pipe messages panel (sets column width to 280px or 0)
- **Default state: collapsed** — center area gets full width
- Toggle button text flips between `<<` (collapsed, meaning "click to expand") and `>>` (expanded, meaning "click to collapse")
- When collapsed, pipe messages `ListView` has `Visibility=Collapsed`

### 3. Prompt text box sizing — fix #4

- Change `MinLines` from 4 to 3
- Set a `MaxHeight` so the text box doesn't grow unbounded
- On `SizeChanged` of the prompt area, call `DeferConsolePositionUpdate()` so the ConPTY console overlay repositions itself
- This directly addresses #4

---

## Panel Grouping (conceptual)

```
+---------------------+ +------------------------------------------------+ +----+
|                     | |              COMMAND WINDOW                      | |    |
|   SESSION           | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | P  |
|   MANAGER           | | |  [Terminal tab] [Source Control tab]        | | | I  |
|                     | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | P  |
|   - list of         | | |                                            | | | E  |
|     sessions        | | |   Terminal / Source Control content         | | |    |
|   - new/kill        | | |                                            | | | M  |
|                     | | +--------------------------------------------+ | | S  |
|                     | | |  Prompt input          [Send] [Refresh]    | | | G  |
|                     | | +--------------------------------------------+ | |    |
+---------------------+ +------------------------------------------------+ +----+
     Always visible       Always visible (tabs + content + prompt = 1 unit)  Toggle
```

The tabs, content area, and prompt bar form a single conceptual "command window" unit.

---

## Files to Modify

- `CcDirector.Wpf/MainWindow.xaml` — grid layout, session item template, pipe messages panel, prompt sizing
- `CcDirector.Wpf/MainWindow.xaml.cs` — toggle logic, `SizeChanged` handler for prompt → `DeferConsolePositionUpdate()`

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Session X button is top-right aligned, hidden by default, visible on hover
- [ ] Pipe messages panel is collapsed by default with a `<<` toggle strip on the right edge
- [ ] Clicking toggle expands pipe messages to 280px with `>>` chevron to re-collapse
- [ ] Prompt text box uses `MinLines=3` with a sensible `MaxHeight`
- [ ] Growing/shrinking the prompt repositions the ConPTY overlay via `DeferConsolePositionUpdate()`
- [ ] Console overlay fills available vertical space correctly in both collapsed and expanded states
- [ ] No visual regressions in session list or tab content areas

Closes #4
title: UI restructure: session X button, collapsible pipe messages, prompt resize

body:
## Problem

When a Claude Code agent is actively working in a repo, the working tree is dirty and the branch is occupied. Reviewing a PR requires checking out the PR branch, which would destroy the agent's state. We need a workflow that lets you review, build, test, approve, and merge PRs without disrupting active sessions or opening a browser.

**Key finding:** VS Code's GitHub PR extension is *not* a solution here — it switches the working branch, which disrupts any active agent. Git worktrees are the correct primitive.

## Recommended Approach: Git Worktrees + `gh` CLI

Git worktrees create an isolated working directory sharing the same `.git` database. The agent continues undisturbed in the main directory. Combined with `gh` CLI, the entire PR lifecycle happens from the terminal.

**Core workflow:**
```bash
gh pr list                                          # See open PRs
gh pr view 42                                       # Read details + CI status
git worktree add ../cc_director-pr42 main           # Create isolated directory
cd ../cc_director-pr42 && gh pr checkout 42         # Get PR code
dotnet build && dotnet test                         # Build + test
dotnet run --project src/CcDirector.Wpf             # Run the app visually
gh pr review 42 --approve --body "Tested, LGTM"     # Approve from CLI
gh pr merge 42 --squash --delete-branch             # Merge + cleanup
cd .. && git worktree remove ../cc_director-pr42    # Remove worktree
```

## Implementation Plan

### Level 1: Claude Code Skill (MVP — quick win)

- New `/review-pr <number>` skill following the pattern of existing skills (`implement-issue`, `read-issues`)
- File: `.claude/skills/review-pr/SKILL.md`
- Orchestrates the full workflow interactively:
  1. Fetch PR details and CI status via `gh pr view`
  2. Optionally create a git worktree for isolated build/test
  3. Build and run tests in the worktree
  4. Approve, comment, or request changes via `gh pr review`
  5. Merge via `gh pr merge` with squash/rebase/merge options
  6. Clean up worktree when done
- Zero code changes to CC Director itself

### Level 2: CC Director Core Feature (medium term)

- `PullRequestProvider` wrapping `gh` CLI (like `GitStatusProvider` wraps `git status`)
- `WorktreeManager` for worktree lifecycle (create, list, remove)
- New "Pull Requests" tab in MainWindow alongside Terminal and Source Control
- Action buttons: View Diff, Build & Test, Approve, Merge

### Level 3: Full Review Panel (long term)

- Inline diff viewer with syntax highlighting
- Dedicated "review session" that auto-creates worktree + Claude session in it
- Cross-repo PR queue (aggregate PRs from all registered repos)
- Inline GitHub comment threading

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **Level 1:** `/review-pr` skill exists and can orchestrate the full review lifecycle from the terminal
- [ ] **Level 2:** PR list and worktree management are integrated into the WPF UI
- [ ] **Level 3:** Full inline review experience with cross-repo support

comments.body: Closing during the 2026-05-28 stale-issue sweep. If this still applies, please refile with current context.

title: PR Review Workflow with Worktree Isolation

body:
## Problem

The prompt text box at the bottom of the center panel is being cut off. The ConPTY console overlay window doesn't account for the prompt bar's height, causing overlap.

## Expected Behavior

1. **Initial size**: The prompt text box should start with **3 rows** of visible text (currently `MinLines="4"`)
2. **No overlap**: The console overlay window must not overlap the prompt text box at any size
3. **Dynamic growth**: As the user types more text, the prompt box should grow vertically, and the console window above it should shrink to accommodate
4. **Max height**: There should still be a reasonable max height so the prompt doesn't consume the entire window

## Technical Context

- The prompt bar is in `MainWindow.xaml` at center `Grid.Row="1"` with `Height="Auto"`
- The terminal area is at `Grid.Row="0"` with `Height="*"`
- The ConPTY console is a **separate process window** positioned as an overlay on top of the WPF TerminalArea border — it doesn't automatically respond to WPF layout changes
- When the prompt TextBox grows (via `AcceptsReturn`/`TextWrapping`), the WPF grid will shrink Row 0, but the console overlay position/size needs to be recalculated and updated

## Changes Needed

- Change `MinLines` from 4 to 3 on the PromptInput TextBox
- Ensure the console overlay repositions/resizes whenever the prompt bar height changes (e.g., hook into `SizeChanged` or `LayoutUpdated` events)
- Verify the initial console overlay placement accounts for the prompt bar's actual rendered height
title: Prompt text box: start at 3 rows, grow dynamically without console overlap

body:
## Description

The session tabs in the left panel should be renamable, similar to how VS Code allows renaming tabs. Users often name tabs based on what they're working on, so this is an important usability feature.

## Screenshot

![CC Director - Current UI](https://github.com/thefrederiksen/cc_director/assets/screenshot-placeholder)

## Requirements

### Tab Renaming
- Allow users to rename session tabs in the left panel (e.g., double-click to edit, or right-click context menu)
- The custom name should replace or supplement the default display name (currently shows folder name + status)

### Persistence
- Save the custom tab name associated with the session/process ID
- When reopening/reattaching a session, restore the custom tab name so the user sees their chosen name

### Left Panel Sizing
- Make the left session panel approximately 20% wider than it currently is
- Allow a bit more height for each tab item if needed for better readability

## Current Behavior

Currently, session tabs display the working directory name and status (e.g., `cc_director / WaitingForInput (PID 42604)`). There is no way to customize the tab name.

## Expected Behavior

Users can rename tabs to meaningful names (e.g., "Frontend work", "Bug fix #123", "Refactoring") and these names persist across session detach/reattach cycles.
title: Add renamable session tabs with persistent names

body:
## Description

Upgrade the existing `GitChangesControl` to display changes in a VS Code-style tree view, matching the layout shown in VS Code's Source Control panel. The control should remain on its current tab since it's tied to the active Claude Code session.

## Motivation

The current control uses a flat list with file name + path stacked vertically. VS Code's approach is more compact and scannable — it shows the file name and relative folder path on the same line, with a color-coded status letter on the right and a count badge on the section header.

## Screenshot Reference

See VS Code's Source Control panel for the target layout:
- Section header "Changes" with a count badge (e.g. `3`) on the right
- Each file row shows: `FileName  relative\folder\path` on the left, status letter (`U`, `M`, `A`, `D`, `R`) on the right
- File name in white, folder path in muted gray, status letter color-coded
- Collapsible tree sections for "Staged Changes" and "Changes"

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] File rows display file name and relative folder path on the **same line** (name in white, path in muted gray)
- [ ] Status letter (`U`, `M`, `A`, `D`, `R`) right-aligned and color-coded
- [ ] Section headers ("Staged Changes", "Changes") are collapsible with a count badge
- [ ] Tree view grouping by folder (expandable/collapsible folder nodes) — matching VS Code's tree mode
- [ ] Keep the control on its current tab, tied to the active Claude Code session

## Implementation Notes

- Affected files: `GitChangesControl.xaml`, `GitChangesControl.xaml.cs`
- Current control: `src/CcDirector.Wpf/Controls/GitChangesControl.xaml`
- Consider using WPF `TreeView` with `HierarchicalDataTemplate` for folder grouping
- Existing `GitStatusProvider` already supplies the data — may need to add a tree-building step to group files by directory
title: Enhance git changes control with VS Code-style tree view

body:
## Description

Add a dedicated Repositories tab to the main window that provides full repository management — clone from GitHub, create new repositories, view all registered repos, and quickly launch a new Claude Code session from any repository.

## Motivation

Currently, repositories can only be browsed/added through the New Session dialog. A dedicated tab would make repository management a first-class feature, letting users manage their workspace of repos and spin up Claude Code sessions more efficiently.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **Clone Repository** — Clone a repo from a GitHub URL into a local directory and auto-register it in the `RepositoryRegistry`
- [ ] **Create New Repository** — Create a new local git repository (`git init`) in a chosen directory and register it
- [ ] **Repositories Tab** — A new tab in the main window that lists all registered repositories (name, path, status)
- [ ] **Launch Session from Repo** — Select a repository from the list and launch a new Claude Code session directly (without going through `NewSessionDialog`)

## Implementation Notes

- Existing `RepositoryRegistry` already handles persistence and add/duplicate detection — extend it as needed
- Clone can use `git clone <url> <path>` via `Process.Start`
- Create can use `git init <path>`
- The Repositories tab could be a `TabItem` alongside the existing session tabs, or a persistent sidebar panel
- Consider showing basic repo info in the list: name, path, last-used date, current branch

comments.body: Implemented in 6eb9269. Repositories tab with clone, add existing, create new, launch session, open in explorer, and remove. Includes CloneRepoDialog with remembered last destination folder. List is sorted alphabetically.

title: Add repository management tab with clone, create, and session launch
